### kathylu — 2012-06-20T15:02:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32378>

If you are struggling with having removed grains from your diet, try baking with coconut flour. I tend to use coconut flour for a treat a couple times per week and I make goodies for my 5 year old, especially to take to school so he doesn't feel deprived. Coconut flour is finely ground, dried, defatted coconut meat. It's a little high in fiber, which is not a bad thing for me as it increases regularity. There are lots of great recipes on the web, or you can get a copy of Bruce Fife's "Cooking with Coconut Flour". There's absolutely no reason to miss grains anymore...

From the Tropical Traditions website:

Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour is fiber from the coconut meat after most of the oil has been extracted to make Virgin Coconut Oil. It is certified organic, unsweetened, and has not been treated with sulfites. Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour is high in dietary fiber and protein, and is gluten-free. It has more fiber than gluten-based grains. Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour can be added to standard wheat-based recipes to add extra fiber, by substituting 10-30% of the grain-based flour with our coconut flour. Some recipes, such as muffins and quick breads, can be accomplished with 100% Tropical Traditions organic coconut flour and therefore be 100% gluten-free! (See some free recipes below.) Since organic coconut flour contains natural sugar from the coconut meat, baked goods need less sugar added. The organic coconut flour has a mildly sweet coconut taste!

Gluten Free Coconut Flour Banana Muffins recipe photoTropical Traditions organic coconut flour also contains over 19% non-gluten protein! Therefore, it makes an excellent addition to shakes and smoothies where fiber and protein are needed. Or simply dissolve a spoonful in water as a high-fiber drink with a coconut flavor! This organic coconut flour is very versatile and very tasty. Sprinkle it over your favorite dishes to add a wonderful coconut flavor, use it as a thickener in soups and sauces - the possibilities are endless!

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-20T17:38:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32416>

> **Jack Kruse;32417 wrote:** If you really want to know how I feel about grains......download the song So Happy by Theory of Deadman listen once then look at the lyrics.......that about covers it.

Hi Dr Kruse,

You are so funny! I couldn't agree with you more about grains. I am glad they are out of my life too.

BUT remember who you are. If anyone in the world can have a different opinion from society, it is you. First, you are a man and a Neurosurgeon at that. I have never been on Facebook but it sounds like you have taken a kicking at times for your views.

As a woman, wife and mother, I just don't have the "power" you have to go my own way. Autonomy is important in my household but I am in a sea of community and sometimes the fist of the state will come down on miscreants like me. Read this a weep and the stupidity that can come out of current wisdom:

[http://eatkamloops.org/funny-troubles/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/funny-troubles/)

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-20T18:08:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32426>

> **Caroline Cooper;32530 wrote:** Hi Dr Kruse,
>
>
>
> You are so funny! I couldn't agree with you more about grains. I am glad they are out of my life too.
>
>
>
> BUT remember who you are. If anyone in the world can have a different opinion from society, it is you. First, you are a man and a Neurosurgeon at that. I have never been on Facebook but it sounds like you have taken a kicking at times for your views.
>

>
> As a woman, wife and mother, I just don't have the "power" you have to go my own way. Autonomy is important in my household but I am in a sea of community and sometimes the fist of the state will come down on miscreants like me. Read this a weep and the stupidity that can come out of current wisdom:
>
> [http://eatkamloops.org/funny-troubles/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/funny-troubles/)

Wow! It's crazy what CW does to those around it. I am in the process of eliminating things from my 12 yr old son's diet, starting with wheat. Although with the "teenage personality" coming through it is not going to be easy. One day he asked "Why do you keep learning this stuff"!! We are fortunate that he attends a Montessori school and the teachers are so open-minded. The kids eat if & when they are hungry. His teacher is going to make their classroom wheat free next year (she read Wheat Belly!) so it's definitely a step in the right direction.

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-20T19:17:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-we-get-a-ranking-of-grains.1926/post-32443>

Yes, it is a cautionary tale, but that doesn't mean I want to give up. At the time I was in trenches with Social Services, PAC, school authorities, and the sometimes confused, and dirty looks, of my friends, family and neighbors. I am so grateful that I had the choice to pull Erika out of the school system. A generation ago that choice would have made my child into a truant. As a parent, I would have been breaking the law; I don't think I would have made the choice under those kind of pressures.

I am also very grateful to Dr Kruse and his work. I believe his work will bring down the walls. I have read almost every word on his website. Though, I likely have only understood about 50% of it. (Kind of like my grade point average in grade school!) I find Dr Kruse's work brilliant and I hope he can make inroads with the medical system. The system needs all the help we can give it, if we are going to save ourselves and our children.

I also believe in the power of Mothers to change the world by changing what happens in the household and in the neighborhoods. Motherhood's "power" is in transforming the dinner table, the garden, and the community we all share. That will take facing our worst fears and having courage to fight for our children's health:

[http://eatkamloops.org/dealing-with-children-societal-pressure-and-halloween/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/dealing-with-children-societal-pressure-and-halloween/)

[http://eatkamloops.org/industrial-food-sickness/](http://http://eatkamloops.org/industrial-food-sickness/)

# Thread: PaleoCowgirl Battles Hashis, Adrenal Fatigue, PCOS  Binge Eating With CT/Keto-Paleo
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 354

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-18T21:41:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-31982>

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, 57.5 grams of chuck steak with 3 T CO.

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising No, 40 minutes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner No dinner

**\- Finished dinner by 7 No dinner

**\- no snacking** Check

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime** Yep

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** Slept in and got right on computer, so I missed the morning sun. Going to go get some Vit. D this afternoon while staining my deck

\- Darkening the house at sunset** I was still outside staining the deck when the sunset. I will do better tomorrow.

\-CT -** I did a hot epsom salt bath and did the croak method of adding cold to the tub water. Then, I did 2 hours of spot-CT with compression shorts.

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check

\-

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-31983>

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-18T21:45:53+0300

### allison09 — 2012-06-19T04:13:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32064>

Hey Cowgirl!

I just started a journal on here too, and it seems like we've got some of the same issues going on. I'm also a binge eater and my main goal is to use the Leptin Rx to end that. And, I also binged yesterday and feel like total crap It's funny- here I am sipping on a mug of Smooth Move while reading that you did the same! I guess we both just want the bad stuff OUT. Ha, sorry. Hopefully you're not sensitive to bathroom talk  Anyway, we can be detox buddies. Right now I'm so bloated I think I might pop. I hate this process!

There are lots of people who offer great advice around here. I'm not really one of them yet, but hopefully I can get my butt in gear and be one of the success stories one day  Good luck with the detox, stay strong!!

### allison09 — 2012-06-19T04:15:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32065>

PS- I love Paleo Pepper! She is such a great advocate for Paleo women, and women in general.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-19T17:24:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32143>

> **allison09;32173 wrote: Hey Cowgirl!
>
> I just started a journal on here too, and it seems like we've got some of the same issues going on. I'm also a binge eater and my main goal is to use the Leptin Rx to end that. And, I also binged yesterday and feel like total crap It's funny- here I am sipping on a mug of Smooth Move while reading that you did the same! I guess we both just want the bad stuff OUT. Ha, sorry. Hopefully you're not sensitive to bathroom talk  Anyway, we can be detox buddies. Right now I'm so bloated I think I might pop. I hate this process!
>
>
>
> There are lots of people who offer great advice around here. I'm not really one of them yet, but hopefully I can get my butt in gear and be one of the success stories one day  Good luck with the detox, stay strong!!**

**

Yes, let's be detox buddies!!! I'm sorry you feel bloated and are struggling with binging, too. I'm not sensitive to bathroom talk; you're okay.  How about this for TMI -- I had to stop my husband from getting frisky with me because my stomach hurt to much to have him touch me! That was depressing. For someone who has struggled with libido issues for a long time, it's finally returning, and then I have to say no because I've binged? Sad...

Anyway, the angry cysts/zits on my face are a good reminder that gluten and dairy are not my friends. I dropped 5.2 lbs. of inflammation from yesterday's steak day. Quelsen has told me I know how to reduce (that's not my problem), but now I need to focus on eating the right kinds of foods in the correct quantities. And, if a binge comes my way, I'm going to take paleo pepper's advice and binge on a head of cabbage instead of a piece of cake and ice cream. LOL!

I'm really focusing on changing my mindset. I've also gotten rid of most non-paleo foods in my house. I was still accommodating to my husband, making him treats, but I believe having to look at these things day in and day out and say no everyday can be exhausting, so I will limit the mental mind games to when I'm out and about.

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, 64.1 grams of ground beef (8 oz) with 1 T CO.

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch. I will be in town getting a back massage over lunch and will have an early dinner.

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner Yep.

**\- Finished dinner by 7 Done eating by 5:30 pm I ate over a lb. of bacon along with my pork burger. I wouldn't call it a binge, but I was definitely wanting more. If I would have had carbs in the house, I would have grabbed them. I was craving pumpkin puree and coconut cream oddly.

\- no snacking** Check...the urge to snack on carbs and sugar really hits me in the evenings. It's like I feel like I will never get to eat again, so my brain tells me to buckle down and eat, eat, eat! I have that urge right now, so I'm going to go outside and do my cattle chores and hope for a nice distraction.

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime** Check.

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** Didn't get outside again for morning light. Need to improve in this area.

\- Darkening the house at sunset** Wore my blue blockers for the last hour I was up. I was sleeping by 11. I was still outside doing cattle stuff at sundown. I'm not sure how to get better at this, unless I totally ditch my husband with the work and get inside. Maybe I will wear the glasses outside. What a sight!

\-CT -** spot-CT for 1 hour this morning

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check, although my bacon had nitrates...so maybe not?

\-

Just got back from a one-hour deep tissue massage...so relaxing. Going to spot CT and take a nap. I ignored it and went to my appointment, ran some errands and plan on eating an early dinner of ground pork and CO.

**Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, 88 grams of ground pork and ground beef with 2 T of CO. I'm really amping up my BAB to see if it stops me from wanting to eat so much at dinner. I wouldn't call yesterday a binge, but I ate a TON of bacon on top of my dinner, so it was a lot of bulk and a lot of calories. I'm going to try to swap the trend and eat most of my food at breakfast.

**Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes, 20 minutes.

**5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch.

**5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner

**Finished dinner by 7

**No snacking** Check

3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime**

Exposure to morning temp & sun** Again, how do I incorporate this in while taking my supplements and readying BAB? I also oil pull at this time, as well. Maybe I should start cooking my BAB over an open fire outside, LOL!

Darkening the house at sunset**

CT** Spot CT (I wear an ice pack on my stomach that wraps around and velcros on. I also shove ice packs in my compression tights and in my sports bra. I'm quite a sight!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-20T17:28:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32411>

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T17:28:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32413>

Hi There

I'm also wondering about why we would crave/overeat protein and fat. I understand the biochemistry behind the carb/everything binges but the wanting to overeat protein and fat just really mystifies me. It just doesn't make sense..... we're made to eat this stuff and it doesn't really "set off" anything in the brain that I know of that would lead to uncontrolled eating.

I do this, too.... wanting to eat way too much protein and fat. It comes in phases, right now it's fat. Coconut oil in particular. Can't get enough of it. I buy bacon one pack at a time b/c I know I'll eat the whole darn thing in one day. It's a treat for every once in a while and I just plan on eating the whole thing in advance.

I'd say if you can at all, don't worry about overeating the good stuff right now and just focus on staying on AI.... one thing at a time. And I know that's hard if you're afraid of gaining weight from it. But then... the other side of fear isn't so bad I'm finding out.... I'm really liking it actually. So maybe time to let go of some fear.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T17:51:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32420>

> **Souldanzer;32527 wrote:** Hi There
>
>
>
> I'm also wondering about why we would crave/overeat protein and fat. I understand the biochemistry behind the carb/everything binges but the wanting to overeat protein and fat just really mystifies me. It just doesn't make sense..... we're made to eat this stuff and it doesn't really "set off" anything in the brain that I know of that would lead to uncontrolled eating.
>
>
>
> I do this, too.... wanting to eat way too much protein and fat. It comes in phases, right now it's fat. Coconut oil in particular. Can't get enough of it. I buy bacon one pack at a time b/c I know I'll eat the whole darn thing in one day. It's a treat for every once in a while and I just plan on eating the whole thing in advance.
>
>
>
> I'd say if you can at all, don't worry about overeating the good stuff right now and just focus on staying on AI.... one thing at a time. And I know that's hard if you're afraid of gaining weight from it. But then... the other side of fear isn't so bad I'm finding out.... I'm really liking it actually. So maybe time to let go of some fear.

Yeah, I really need to dump my scale and my relationship with it. The bacon was a lot to eat, but you're right -- it didn't set off the crazy in the brain, I've got to eat more, more, more feeling that I get with carbs. Thanks for the reminder about AI. I was just pining away after a brownie recipe to take to the lake this weekend, but that would mean allowing eggs and almonds back in, and that, I cannot do.

I usually plan out what I'm going to eat the day before, and I ate everything I planned to eat all day for breakfast this morning. Maybe I will add something else for dinner, maybe not. I'm not sure how dangerous fasting is for someone with an ED...but it was a lot of protein and fat, so the cravings should stay away.

Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.

Souldanzer, what was a turning point for you? I read that you made a remarkably fast recovery this time around. What do you think was different this time?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T17:54:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32421>

> **Jennifer;32525 wrote:** Keep riding PaleoCowgirl!

Cute! Thanks, Jennifer! I read through parts of your journal and see TOM has returned for you! Congrats! I can't remember the last time I had TOM. I bet it was 2010. I got off birth control in Feb. and am hoping to see it's return soon. DO you think there was one element of your routine that helped you out? If so, care to share?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-20T18:22:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32430>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32535 wrote:** Cute! Thanks, Jennifer! I read through parts of your journal and see TOM has returned for you! Congrats! I can't remember the last time I had TOM. I bet it was 2010. I got off birth control in Feb. and am hoping to see it's return soon. DO you think there was one element of your routine that helped you out? If so, care to share?

LOL..we're thinking alike. Well, I've been on the elmination diet since June 2, so I've been eating really clean - no coffee, no alcohol, no caffienne, no eggs, no pork, no red meat, no dairy. This is all for my leaky gut. I added in sweet potatoes. And I eat a lot of salmon now. I stopped weighing myself except maybe once a week. I've stopped sweating the small stuff. Added the pregnelone, 7 keto, Phosphatidyl Choline and l-carnitine. I take a lot of fish oil. And the red clover which I read in Dr Lee's hormone book. He also suggested Vitex herb for ovulation but I never found that one so I didn't buy it. And I switched to armour thryoid and did some spot CT on carotids. Or maybe the estrodial has finally kicked in???

### Lyndra — 2012-06-20T19:06:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32441>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32534 wrote:**
>
>
>
> Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.

Maybe there is no "normal" behavior when it comes to junk food like that. How many people can look at a pan of brownies and not want to eat until they're stuffed or beyond? I gave in to GF treats a few weeks back and one begat another and another. Once I finally got them out of my system (brain & body), I'm not craving them anymore.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-20T19:23:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32445>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32534 wrote:** Yeah, I really need to dump my scale and my relationship with it. The bacon was a lot to eat, but you're right -- it didn't set off the crazy in the brain, I've got to eat more, more, more feeling that I get with carbs. Thanks for the reminder about AI. I was just pining away after a brownie recipe to take to the lake this weekend, but that would mean allowing eggs and almonds back in, and that, I cannot do.
>
>
>
> I usually plan out what I'm going to eat the day before, and I ate everything I planned to eat all day for breakfast this morning. Maybe I will add something else for dinner, maybe not. I'm not sure how dangerous fasting is for someone with an ED...but it was a lot of protein and fat, so the cravings should stay away.
>
>
>
> Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.
>
>
>
> Souldanzer, what was a turning point for you? I read that you made a remarkably fast recovery this time around. What do you think was different this time?

YES, dump the scale! I'm a lot happier now that I did. Of course, I gained and I'm not thrilled about that but I'm putting health first, so there you have it. I know that I've gained. My clothes can tell me that. Clothes are better than an actual number that you can use to berate yourself with.

And this may sound crazy, but just give yourself permission to eat the whole pan. See what happens...I gave in to all my cravings for about 10 days solid. I stopped trying to stop myself. Stopped beating myself up. And yes, I gained and it was ugly for awhile but now I seem to be able to stop myself. There are things that I still eat a bigger portion of than my super-uptight personality wants me to eat! But I can pull the plug. Even so, I always did the BAB and ate my fat and protein. It's the nuts that get me, every time. OH and the muffin cups. I could eat a lot of those.

I think another key is not to tell yourself anything that starts with "never" like "I can never have nuts again". At least for me, the more never something is, the more I want it. Set small goals. Like no bingeing this week. For some crazy reason, doing this elimination diet has been easy, even though I can't hardly eat anything. I think it's because I know it's just 28 days.

Also, I think realizing that you are normal! As normal as I am!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T19:31:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32446>

> **Lyndra;32555 wrote:** Maybe there is no "normal" behavior when it comes to junk food like that. How many people can look at a pan of brownies and not want to eat until they're stuffed or beyond? I gave in to GF treats a few weeks back and one begat another and another. Once I finally got them out of my system (brain & body), I'm not craving them anymore.

Agreed. For awhile, I felt I was too strict, and I needed to cut myself some slack, so I experimented with g-free treats and loved them, but I also noticed that I craved sweets more...I need to focus on just eating real food.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T19:33:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32447>

I added in sweet potatoes. And I eat a lot of salmon now. I stopped weighing myself except maybe once a week. I've stopped sweating the small stuff. Added the pregnelone, 7 keto, Phosphatidyl Choline and l-carnitine. I take a lot of fish oil. And the red clover which I read in Dr Lee's hormone book. He also suggested Vitex herb for ovulation but I never found that one so I didn't buy it. And I switched to armour thryoid and did some spot CT on carotids. Or maybe the estrodial has finally kicked in???

Ha! That's alot! Well, I'm on the 7 keto, but I may have to look into the other stuff. I had read up on Vitex. I also see a lot of people have success using metmorfin for ovulating and eventually achieving pregnancy. I see my doctor again in mid-July, so I may take a list of supplements that I have read up on and see what she thinks. Thanks!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T19:34:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32448>

> **Jennifer;32559 wrote:** YES, dump the scale! I'm a lot happier now that I did. Of course, I gained and I'm not thrilled about that but I'm putting health first, so there you have it. I know that I've gained. My clothes can tell me that. Clothes are better than an actual number that you can use to berate yourself with.
>
>
>
> And this may sound crazy, but just give yourself permission to eat the whole pan. See what happens...I gave in to all my cravings for about 10 days solid. I stopped trying to stop myself. Stopped beating myself up. And yes, I gained and it was ugly for awhile but now I seem to be able to stop myself. There are things that I still eat a bigger portion of than my super-uptight personality wants me to eat! But I can pull the plug. Even so, I always did the BAB and ate my fat and protein. It's the nuts that get me, every time. OH and the muffin cups. I could eat a lot of those.
>
>
>
> I think another key is not to tell yourself anything that starts with "never" like "I can never have nuts again". At least for me, the more never something is, the more I want it. Set small goals. Like no bingeing this week. For some crazy reason, doing this elimination diet has been easy, even though I can't hardly eat anything. I think it's because I know it's just 28 days.
>
>
>
> Also, I think realizing that you are normal! As normal as I am!

I've tried that method...just eat...and sometimes it resulted in a drop on the scale...other times it lead to a binge of non-paleo foods. You're right about not saying "never;" it seems to restricting. I'm trying to do the whole30...one day at a time.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T19:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32454>

> Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.

You could consider your difficulties a blessing.... to become Optimal - not mediocre, like those who continue to eat "cheats" and "get away" with it.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T20:12:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32459>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32534 wrote:** Yeah, I really need to dump my scale and my relationship with it. The bacon was a lot to eat, but you're right -- it didn't set off the crazy in the brain, I've got to eat more, more, more feeling that I get with carbs. Thanks for the reminder about AI. I was just pining away after a brownie recipe to take to the lake this weekend, but that would mean allowing eggs and almonds back in, and that, I cannot do.
>
>
>
> I usually plan out what I'm going to eat the day before, and I ate everything I planned to eat all day for breakfast this morning. Maybe I will add something else for dinner, maybe not. I'm not sure how dangerous fasting is for someone with an ED...but it was a lot of protein and fat, so the cravings should stay away.
>
>
>
> Sigh...sometimes I just wish I could be normal. Eat one gluten free treat without needing to eat the whole pan, and then not feeling guilty about that one bar and making my life miserable for days after to try to lose the weight.
>
>
>
> Souldanzer, what was a turning point for you? I read that you made a remarkably fast recovery this time around. What do you think was different this time?

I still have to eat when I get hungry pretty much. So if I ate everything in the AM for the day, if I was hungry for dinner, I would still eat. How do you even plan your food intake like that in advance? How can you predict how much and what your body is going to need exactly?

I made a very quick recovery from my last lapse for my own standards. If there is a next time, I will catch it even earlier and do the required corrections. I'm pretty sure this happened b/c I've been following AI and Lrx for long enough to get some pathways established in my brain. So while my AN pathways are still very functional and easy to slip into if I go off road with my program, if I correct the deviations, I think my new and improved pathways just kick back in quickly. It was like my body remembered what it was supposed to do. I'm not at my goal yet with my eating... still working on some things. I'm not afraid of relapsing any longer - b/c I know what to do to get out of it now.

### Lyndra — 2012-06-20T20:19:41+0300

I think you're right. Those of us who have issues are lucky in that it motivates us to change and improve. Those who eat SAD and think they are doing great are in trouble and don't know it.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32476>

> **Souldanzer;32574 wrote:** I still have to eat when I get hungry pretty much. So if I ate everything in the AM for the day, if I was hungry for dinner, I would still eat. How do you even plan your food intake like that in advance? How can you predict how much and what your body is going to need exactly?
>

>
>
>
> I made a very quick recovery from my last lapse for my own standards. If there is a next time, I will catch it even earlier and do the required corrections. I'm pretty sure this happened b/c I've been following AI and Lrx for long enough to get some pathways established in my brain. So while my AN pathways are still very functional and easy to slip into if I go off road with my program, if I correct the deviations, I think my new and improved pathways just kick back in quickly. It was like my body remembered what it was supposed to do. I'm not at my goal yet with my eating... still working on some things. I'm not afraid of relapsing any longer - b/c I know what to do to get out of it now.

Well, I do adjust if I'm hungry and depending on my activity levels; it's not so rigid...but I feel failing to plan is planning to fail, and I chart everything; I even plug in what I eat on binges, so I can look at exactly what I'm putting in my mouth. My hungry/satiety signals are so far gone that I don't trust myself. For example, even though I ate over 1200 calories for BAB, I could have kept eating. I have no idea when I'm hungry and full really, until I'm way past the uncomfortable point. On the flip side, it can be really easy to eat too little because the hunger cues don't really come like the blood sugar drops on a SAD diet.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-20T21:23:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32477>

> **Souldanzer;32570 wrote:** maybe stop being blinded by the goal and start to enjoy the journey....

True. There is a certain anxiety that comes with not cycling, especially because one day I would like to have children. It plays tricks with my mind. What's wrong with me? Am I not fertile? Will I have problems getting pregnant? Miscarriage? Developmental issues in my baby? Maybe all women have these fears; I don't know. But, you're right...I need to relax and let TOM come when it comes.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T21:26:35+0300

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T21:36:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32481>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32592 wrote:** True. There is a certain anxiety that comes with not cycling, especially because one day I would like to have children. It plays tricks with my mind. What's wrong with me? Am I not fertile? Will I have problems getting pregnant? Miscarriage? Developmental issues in my baby? Maybe all women have these fears; I don't know. But, you're right...I need to relax and let TOM come when it comes.

Think long and hard about when the right time is to have a baby. Or you might be doubling up on ED problems (and others) for the rest of your life and your child's life. If you have a child while you're still ED, you will set up your child to have the same problem.

This one is emotionally charged for me as my mom was AN right before she got pregnant with me. Same situation as yours... desperately wanted to get pregnant w/ HPA amenorrhea. If I was given the choice, I would have happily waited a few more years to join this world set up for success instead of the disaster that I have been so far. I keep wanting to say "it's not fair" and I know that won't change anything... what doesn't kill me makes me stronger.... but it's not a lot of fun, speaking as the future baby you might be having.

Yeah, I really need to get myself in shape before I bring a baby into this world. I read your journal entry about that and the point you made that people absolutely should not think about reproducing if they aren't optimal, and I cried to my husband about it. It's a really crappy feeling to think I shouldn't have a baby because I wouldn't be doing that child any favors. Then, I see my friends and cousins who have no problems with weight issues, have happy healthy babies and seem blissfully unaware that the cheerios, ice cream and other SAD goodies they give their babies are bad for them!

But, you're right...I would hate to be on binge/restrict mode while carrying a child. That's not fair to my unborn babies. And, it would be a terrible environment to develop.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-20T21:51:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32488>

Enjoy the process and success!

I'm sorry that was hard for you to hear. The truth isn't always what we want to hear.... and yet, you just made my day, as I am plugging along with my little steps out of the disaster that my life and body have become.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-21T00:39:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32532>

PaleoCowgirl, I have been reading your thoughts and think you're pretty inspirational yourself. You've been so honest and I think so much of our 'cheating' and 'hiding' is about dishonesty (mostly trying to keep things from ourselves!). Here is what I'm wondering. How would you eat/live if you were pregnant. Right now. I think you should eat and live that way at this moment. My guess is you'd nourish yourself, mind body and spirit. You wouldn't eat crap because you'd be motivated by life not to do so. You wouldn't restrict your calories because you'd want to nourish a growing being. You would enjoy each moment and love the journey because you knew each moment was precious and never-to-be-repeated. Well all that is true right now. Maybe if you live it - NOW - you will not only move out of the stuck place but also end up giving birth to a new you. And a baby.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T00:45:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32533>

I've seen people with ED's continue the detrimental behaviors throughout their pregnancies. Getting pregnant does not cure an ED. Many have tried.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T02:39:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32559>

> **KiwiLauren;32649 wrote:** PaleoCowgirl, I have been reading your thoughts and think you're pretty inspirational yourself. You've been so honest and I think so much of our 'cheating' and 'hiding' is about dishonesty (mostly trying to keep things from ourselves!). Here is what I'm wondering. How would you eat/live if you were pregnant. Right now. I think you should eat and live that way at this moment. My guess is you'd nourish yourself, mind body and spirit. You wouldn't eat crap because you'd be motivated by life not to do so. You wouldn't restrict your calories because you'd want to nourish a growing being. You would enjoy each moment and love the journey because you knew each moment was precious and never-to-be-repeated. Well all that is true right now. Maybe if you live it - NOW - you will not only move out of the stuck place but also end up giving birth to a new you. And a baby.

Wow, this is really powerful stuff. So true! I just sat down to have venison and wanted something sweet for dessert and realized I didn't have anything in the house I could have -- not a piece of fruit or scoop of coconut butter or nothing. I was just thinking about going to raid my mom's cupboard...that's becoming a habit during my binges since we live 15 minutes from town and a half mile from her kitchen...anyway, I read this note and realized, I don't WANT to hurt my body with sugary carbs. I don't want to binge in secret and have a hidden addiction. What you said is exactly how I should treat myself. Eat when I'm hungry, stop when I'm full, eat healthy, real foods, don't restrict and don't overdo it. I know it's easier said than done, but I need to find a place in my mind where this mantra actually sticks and works the next time I want a sugar high. Thanks so much for the advice!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T02:47:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32563>

> **Souldanzer;32650 wrote: I've seen people with ED's continue the detrimental behaviors throughout their pregnancies. Getting pregnant does not cure an ED. Many have tried.
>
>
>
>
>
> .... nobody needs a spare scale....**

**

I don't know why I've got baby on the brain so much right now. It started when my doc diagnosed me with PCOS and said I could have a hard time getting/staying pregnant. I've always been one to try that much harder when somebody tells me I can't do something. In reality, my husband and I don't want a baby for a few years yet. We have a land loan (cattle pasture) that will be paid off in 2015, so that gives us a lot more cushion and extra money for a new member of the family. In the meantime, if I stick to that goal, that means I have 3.5 years to get optimal.

**Between now and then, I want to:**

\- Get a regular TOM back

\- Settle at an appropriate weight for my height. I'm 5'3.5" I weighed 137 on my wedding day and everyone said I looked beautiful. To me, I looked fat...I know 109 is way too long, so I should settle somewhere in between and be happy about it.

\- Reduce my need for t3 and keep my hashis at bay

\- Heal my adrenals. I hate seeing my temps tank to 95 in the afternoon. No wonder I feel crappy!

\- Eliminate my cravings and urge to binge

\- Sleep through the night without waking up to go to the bathroom

\- Have a positive body image. Right now, when I look in the mirror, I see a skinny boy with a spare tire, thinning hair on my head and extra hair everywhere else (thanks PCOS) and acne (from my binges). Hot, huh?

\- Change the way I think about food. Food is medicine. It's not a treat. It's not a punishment. It's not a reward. It's fuel.

How am I going to do that?**

\- CT regularly. Get in the bathtub, pansy!

\- Eat fish regularly. Just because I live on a ranch and have a surplus of beef doesn't mean I can't eat more fish!

\- BAB. Fat and Protein. Fat and Protein.

\- Leptin reset. No snacking. No late-night eating. Plenty of space between meals.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32594>

PaleoCowgirl, you have a fantastic way of saying all the things I think and feel, but in a much more eloquent way than I ever can. I'm going through a lot of the same things you are right now (pretty much everything minus the baby brain) and I have many of the same goals. I just want to ditch this ED "crazy brain" (it's funny to me that you said that-that's how I refer to it too) and have a "normal" relationship with food. That's all. Eat food, be healthy. It kills me that it's so easy for so many people and so so hard for me. I've been a terrible detox buddy lately  I'll be back on the wagon again soon...I hope. Keep if up though, you're doing awesome and your journal is inspiring!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T04:50:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32605>

Thanks! Yeah the ED crazy brain is hard to understand unless you've experienced it. It's like a switch that can't be shut off...I know if I keep at the BAB and protein and fat and not restrict so much, the binging will go away. I just have to forget the scale for awhile, which is so hard because I DON'T want to be a fat girl again. 177 lbs. is way to much for this small frame of mine. Talk about crazy inflammation!

Why have you been a terrible detox buddy? Are you struggling? Binging? What's going on? You can get right back on the wagon right now. Forgive yourself of your slip-ups and be kind to your body. I always have to remind myself of that. The self-loathing after a binge can be almost more destructive than the binge itself.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T06:18:32+0300

I don't know why, but I ate bad foods again tonight. What's my problem? All these people on here motivating me and supporting me and wanting me to do well, and I mistreat my body again. And, again. (and a good leptin reset breakfast lol) I am so right with ya though!

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-21T16:39:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32692>

> **PaleoCowgirl;32748 wrote:** I don't know why, but I ate bad foods again tonight. What's my problem? All these people on here motivating me and supporting me and wanting me to do well, and I mistreat my body again. And, again. And, again. Oh, the squeaky wheel. I'm getting to the point where I don't trust myself to eat anything because I don't know if I will ever stop. Yet, not eating anything only exacerbates the problem. What is a girl to do?

I know - I hate that when I do it. I feel like two people - one is wise and loving and knows what she needs to do to treat her body with respect. The other is eating. All I can say, is you do it until you don't. If I can paraphrase MomaG (from memory so I hope this is the gist of it) if you have a history of restricting or doing counter-measures to "make up" for bingeing, your body doesn't trust that it will be nourished. So if you binge, you still have to BAB and feed your body and not do any corrections. Your making connections in your brain, the behavior you want will follow. May I also suggest that you stop identifying yourself with the "fat girl" who has to watch her weight and scrutinize herself. If you want to be thin, act like a thin person! Be a thin person in your mind and it will ripple down. I like what Kiwi said, be that person now.

And if all else fails, check this out:

[http://fastpaleo.com/chocolate-covered-bacon/](http://http://fastpaleo.com/chocolate-covered-bacon/)

Thanks for the pep talk and for making me laugh! Nothing better than chocolate and bacon!

I just woke up...I was up until about 3:30 am last night...could NOT sleep. My steak is on the grill. BAB. BAB. BAB. Repeat.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-21T18:08:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32717>

**\- Breakfast w/ 50gm of protein Yes, I had a HUGE chuck steak

**\- Breakfast w/i 30m of rising Yes

**\- 5+ hrs between Breakfast and Lunch No lunch. I slept in until late and didn't get done with BAB until 10 am

**\- 5+ hrs between Lunch and Dinner No Lunch.

**\- Finished dinner by 7 Ate dinner at 4:30 pm

**\- no snacking** Check

\- 3.5+ hrs between dinner & bedtime** Yep

\- exposure to morning temp & sun** No...

\- Darkening the house at sunset** Put my blue blockers on at 7:45 pm. Already feeling sleepy...

\-CT** Cold shower, face dunking and spot CT twice

\- 100% primal, whole 30 diet (no PUFAS)** Check

\-

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-21T19:11:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32750>

This one is for you - i found it facinating and so explains why know that I have stopped trying to stop myself, I feel so much better!

[http://www.gnolls.org/2342/restrained-eating-willpower-and-why-diets-fail-why-are-we-hungry-part-3/](http://http://www.gnolls.org/2342/restrained-eating-willpower-and-why-diets-fail-why-are-we-hungry-part-3/)

Why Dieting Makes You Fat: You’re Depending On Willpower

All this seems like an obvious, common-sense result. We know intuitively that willpower is a limited resource: we only have so much tolerance for denying our own wants. Furthermore, life is far more pleasant when we’re not living in a constant state of self-denial (or self-flagellation).

But there are even more reasons to minimize the role of willpower in a diet. These titles say it all, and I don’t even have to quote abstracts:

Am J Clin Nutr. 2001 Jan;73(1):7-12.

Cognitive dietary restraint is associated with higher urinary cortisol excretion in healthy premenopausal women.

McLean JA, Barr SI, Prior JC.

J Gerontol A Biol Sci Med Sci. 2006 Jun;61(6):628-33.

High cognitive dietary restraint is associated with increased cortisol excretion in postmenopausal women.

Rideout CA, Linden W, Barr SI.

Psychosom Med. 2010 May; 72(4): 357–364.

Low Calorie Dieting Increases Cortisol

A. Janet Tomiyama, Ph.D.,a Traci Mann, Ph.D.,b Danielle Vinas, B.A.,c Jeffrey M. Hunger, B.A.,b Jill DeJager, MPH., RD,d and Shelley E. Taylor, Ph.D.c

**In other words, restraint—using our willpower—doesn’t just use energy. It causes stress.

A Brief Note On Cortisol

Cortisol is a glucocorticoid—a class of necessary regulatory hormones whose functions and interactions are far too complicated to discuss here. However, we know that persistently high cortisol levels are strongly associated with increased stress, and that administration of exogenous glucocorticoids (the fancy term for “giving them as drugs”) actually produces increased stress, along with a litany of terrible side effects.

“Side effects of oral corticosteroids that are used on a short-term basis include: an increase in appetite, weight gain, insomnia, fluid retention, and mood changes, such as feeling irritable, or anxious.

Side effects of oral corticosteroids used on a long-term basis (longer than three months) include: osteoporosis (fragile bones), hypertension (high blood pressure), diabetes, weight gain, increased vulnerability to infection, cataracts and glaucoma (eye disorders), thinning of the skin, bruising easily, and muscle weakness.”

\-UK National Health Service

Persistently high cortisol not only makes you feel stressed, with all the nervousness, sleep disruption, and ugly side effects that entails…

…it makes you eat!**

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T19:25:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32754>

Some of us bingers on here noticed that we can't even handle very small amounts of carbs without it triggering a binge. Maybe try to eliminate the fruit if it sets you off. You now know more. No fruit will set you up for success next time.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-22T19:56:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-32972>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33099 wrote:** Which totally sucks because I LOVE that stuff...seriously. Mix it with Libby's pumpkin, a little stevia, cinnamon and coconut flakes on top...and it's my favorite dessert!

You can make your own - just throw some coconut flakes or meat of a coconut into the food processor. If you are lucky enough to have a Vitamix, it takes about 2 minutes. Otherwise it's a real PIA - you have to stop and scrape it off the side continuously. Although I just read that someone tilted their food process by using a book and then it works better. Then you can just make a little bit at a time and since it's such a PIA, you won't be tempted to make more! LOL

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-23T00:33:37+0300

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-23T18:31:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33166>

Good luck on your consult Cowgirl - wanted to let you know I am getting much out of your postings on all this. . . I look at your photo and see how cute (and tiny!) you look, and it just helps me deal with ME better seeing that even you have some of the same eating issues (can't get full!) as someone like me.

The more bricks that get put on the wall that prove it's not some kind of weak character help me believe it.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-23T18:42:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33172>

> **Cavemam;33298 wrote:** Good luck on your consult Cowgirl - wanted to let you know I am getting much out of your postings on all this. . . I look at your photo and see how cute (and tiny!) you look, and it just helps me deal with ME better seeing that even you have some of the same eating issues (can't get full!) as someone like me.
>
>
>
> The more bricks that get put on the wall that prove it's not some kind of weak character help me believe it.

Thanks for the kind words. I often relate to your posts, as well, so thanks for sharing yourself on the forum.

As far as my consult went....tough love from Dr. K but much needed. If anyone is doubting whether a consult is worth the money (I'm very conservative with my spending), just do it. No question, the best money I have spent on this journey so far.

Now, if I can only get my husband to understand what I'm going through. I tried to share with him some of Dr. K's thoughts, and we ended up getting in a fight. He thinks I get on the internet, read a bunch of voodoo and create health problems I don't have. I'm finally admitting to myself that I have an ED, and I tried to tell him I do and that's what I'm struggling with, and he doesn't see it. He says he sees me eat all the time. I'm so frustrated. Yet, I know if I can't get him on board to support me, I still have to do this fight for myself. And the first hurdle I have to get over is my weight. I need to gain weight; I don't want to. It's fun being this cute, tiny little girl, but at what cost? Not being able to have babies? Making my binge/restrict disorder worse? As Dr. K put it this morning via Skype, I'm my own worst enemy.

Next step: get some labs, narrow down my issues and eat ALOT of fat. Dr. K gave me permission to eat a whole stick of butter in a sitting. LOL!

Have a great weekend everyone! I'm off to a wedding.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-23T19:00:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33179>

Fighting w/dh is no fun. You do know you are doing the right thing and that's what is important. Nobody will take care of you but you. Perhaps when you can make some progress your dh will finally see or maybe not. No matter what, do what is best for you. Mine supports me but still doesn't get it as he still tries to feed me grains and doesn't do anything to keep my stress down. With my AF, he is slowly killing me and doesn't have a clue no matter how much I tell him. Stick up for yourself and do what you need to. You are worth it!!!!

Are you needing tests to see what is wrong or was he able to tell you from the labs you have? I wish my consult w/been longer. I choose the 1/2 hr and no one told me to write up a history. I feel like I was sorta short-changed. But on a positive note, I do have to have more labs so perhaps the 2nd consult will be much more enlightening and maybe a good thing it was only what is was. It was still good but certainly could have been better.

The thing with Jack's knowledge is - everything he has told me so far has been confirmed by a doctor here. HE IS THE REAL THING AND WE ARE SO LUCKY TO HAVE HIM. Perhaps your dh should have some labs and see where he stands. If he eats grains and sugar, he is probably not optimal either.

I hope you really really enjoy yourself today at the wedding.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-24T22:29:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33341>

sounds like some big thinking for you PaleoCowgirl, sending you a hug...I know your hubs is probably scared...I'll be praying he comes around for you! I guess being single has it's positives and negatives - I don't have to worry about anyone else but me right now. But I do take flak from friends and family about how restrictive my diet is right now when I'm "healthy." Well, they don't know what's going on with my hormones (except when I'm a bear)! And it was Dr K's blog on "avoiding the fates of Michael J Fox and Ronald Reagan" that got me. Only in my case, it's avoiding the fate of my maternal grandmother (Alzheimer's) and paternal grandfather (Parkinson's Disease). Yes, we "aren't that sick" right now, AND we're trying to keep it that way!

I know it must be hard to hear you need to gain weight. I know that I am very unhappy with the pounds that I put on BUT I got TOM again. I know in the end, we'll be able to have the figure we want and be healthy. But it might take some patience with our bodies in the mean time. I'm trying to be more loving and accepting of myself. If I can figure out my self-worth without being tied to skinny-minny me, I'll be all set! Keep going, PC!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T06:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33405>

To summarize my conversation with Dr. K:

1\. Do I have hashis officially? Antibodies present isn't enough to determine. **Officially test needed.

2\. How about PCOS? Just because my glucose test indicated elevated insulin doesn't mean I have it. An ultrasound would confirm yes or no.** However, because my periods are non-existent, not just inconsistent, it leads Dr. K to believe I have...

3\. HA (hypothalamic amenorrhea) Wtf is that? Dr. K sent me three sites to check out:

[http://noperiodbaby.blogspot.com/2009/06/hypothalamic-amenorrhea-recovery.html](http://http://noperiodbaby.blogspot.com/2009/06/hypothalamic-amenorrhea-recovery.html)

[http://halfasmanychances.blogspot.com/p/hypothalamic-ameno-what.html](http://http://halfasmanychances.blogspot.com/p/hypothalamic-ameno-what.html)

[http://forums.fertilitycommunity.com/unexplained-infertility/2020186789-need-support-hypothalamic-amenorrhea-anyone.html](http://http://forums.fertilitycommunity.com/unexplained-infertility/2020186789-need-support-hypothalamic-amenorrhea-anyone.html)

Basically, it's me in a nutshell. I busted my butt to get skinny, and in the end, it shut down my signaling to ovulate. I need to research this to further understand it, but I plan to visit with my primary caregiver this week about it.

4\. Direct quote from Dr. K, "You are one of the most messed up cases I've dealt with. This is serious \*\*\*\*. You may never be able to have a baby."

5\. His solution? Eat BAB...focusing more on fat than protein. Keto-paleo. No paleo treats. Absolutely no cheating, no gluten or sugar. Do for 12-18 months and see if period resumes. Period must be regular for 6 months before getting pregnant. I need to gain 10-15 lbs. So, basically my decision to get on hcg (which elevated my testosterone) is what helped me get thin but also caused me a whole lot of problems.

6\. Stop all supplements (except probiotics). He pretty much said it's like pouring buckets of water on a burning house.

7\. Beware of any doc who ever tries to get me on clomid.

How do I feel?

Angry. Depressed. Worried. How fat do I have to get? Will I ever be able to have a baby? If so, at what cost? The weekend was a bit rocky with foods, but I survived. It's time to get serious now about getting optimal.

Dr. K said I would be my own worst enemy on this journey, and I'm thinking he's probably right. I took notes from our meeting, and I need to dig into them deeper, but for now, these are preliminary notes.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-25T07:16:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33411>

>

>

Period must be regular for 6 months before getting pregnant. I need to gain 10-15 lbs. So, basically my decision to get on hcg (which elevated my testosterone) is what helped me get thin but also caused me a whole lot of problems.
>
>
>
> 6\. Stop all supplements (except probiotics). He pretty much said it's like pouring buckets of water on a burning house.
>

> 7\. Beware of any doc who ever tries to get me on clomid.
>

> How do I feel?
>
>
>
> Angry. Depressed. Worried. How fat do I have to get? Will I ever be able to have a baby? If so, at what cost? The weekend was a bit rocky with foods, but I survived. It's time to get serious now about getting optimal.
>
>
>
> Dr. K said I would be my own worst enemy on this journey, and I'm thinking he's probably right. I took notes from our meeting, and I need to dig into them deeper, but for now, these are preliminary notes.

Wow, PaleoCowgirl. That's a BIG consultation with some BIG revelations. What I hear is that you feel he is spot on - 'that's me in a nutshell' and that how this happened is making sense. I think this is really important. Because it now means that the plan is your plan, not someone else's, and it's for your specific health, not for someone else's idea of health or for weight loss.

You ask 'how fat do I have to get?' and I think that is the 'worst-enemy voice' talking. You said Dr K suggested you gain 10-15 pound. One, that is NOT fat. Two, you know how much to gain. Seems like a bit of fat on your body is important, just like fat in your food. Fat is equating with health here. And goodness. And life. Fat nourishes your body, your hormones, your baby-making abilities. There is nothing to fear here. Hurray. (You will still be beautiful with 10 extra pounds on you!)

I have just paid for my first consult (no time scheduled yet) and I can feel the nervousness in me about what may be revealed. So I can only imagine how hard it is to hear all this really tough stuff and how much is at stake. I don't have any easy answers or words of wisdom in that department, except be kind to yourself. Always. Treat yourself with the same kindness that you dole out to everyone else on this forum. Self-care is the route that will get you to optimal, not beating yourself for having so far to go.

Hang in there.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-25T14:37:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33418>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33537 wrote:** How much did you have to gain when before TOM made an appearance? I know this sounds vain...but I've bought so many cute new clothes and jeans to fit my small new body, and most I'm going to have to give away as I become a lard \*ss. Ugh. My husband was pretty supportive when I told him I needed to gain weight, as we has been telling me the same thing for months. He suggested I start lifting weights, so I don't just get squishy as I gain.

I feel really bad for you cuz I'm sure you are devastated over the thought alone. I just gained 5 lbs from the HC I've been on and I know how bad that is making me feel. But, just a couple of thoughts: 1.) Sometimes one preceives themselves differently than others. You probably do need to gain. Having to gain weight might actually make you more attractive especially to your dh (a little fat in the right places is very feminine). I always thought I wanted to be super thin but my sister is soo tiny now that I NEVER, EVER want to look like her. Can't even hug her appropriately as there is nothing to hug. 2.) Gaining some weight doesn't mean you will be a fat \*ss. Did he tell you how much weight you need to gain?

If your dh is telling you to gain, then he sees you different than you do and you probably do need to gain. I do understand about the weight gain though. No one wants to. Go slow and enjoy the ride! I can tell you that I have missed out on a lot of enjoyment because "I" thought I was too fat to wear this or that and kept telling myself when I lose a few lbs. I will dress like this. I am not fat still today but still have that screwed up mind. Why do we think no one will love us if we gain a few? I'm sure if you gain a few lbs. no one will probably notice and as for the cute little clothes, 1 size larger will still be very cute and you will still be awesome! Try not to stress over this cuz you know it will make you gain for sure, that whole cortisol thing. This really is a chance for you to get to know your body too. You will know what makes you gain and how much and how fast.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-25T14:50:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33419>

Ok, PCG, I didn't read your whole recap from your consult. WOW! If the good doc is telling you 10-15 lbs to gain, that is serious. I would not be looking at this from a weight gain stand point but more of a HEALTH standpoint. Sounds like you need to get it in gear cuz optimal isn't coming your way if you don't and worse things might be coming your way. How tall are you and how much do you weigh now? I am picturing very very thin. Fix yourself now before you get old. It is bxxxx to do it later. Trust me on that one. Still after a year, I am struggling. Hell, I am struggling to even find a doc who can deal with everything I have going on. So very frustrating.

As for how much you will have to gain? I think your body will tell you when TOM returns and stays. You have a journey but can make it. I wish you the best.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-25T15:48:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33426>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33537 wrote:** How much did you have to gain when before TOM made an appearance? I know this sounds vain...but I've bought so many cute new clothes and jeans to fit my small new body, and most I'm going to have to give away as I become a lard \*ss. Ugh. My husband was pretty supportive when I told him I needed to gain weight, as we has been telling me the same thing for months. He suggested I start lifting weights, so I don't just get squishy as I gain.

My advice is not to focus on the number. Just eat. Eat if you are hungry (Still do BAB and three meals max a day) but don't try to "diet" and do Leptin Rx. I know my head sometimes try to determine how much I should eat instead of my body. How it happened for me was I had an emotional trigger (my bff is my same age and told me she is pregnant with baby #5) and it sent me eating like crazy. Honestly, I couldn't tell you how much I gained because I stopped weighing, but it's in the 10-15 range. I def don't recommend a week long bender as your method to gain but eat. Eat fat and protein and fish and don't "push" yourself to do just two meals a day. If you need three, then eat three. I put the weight on in April. Finally got TOM in June. I could see signs of ovulation before TOM came, so figure that as a ballpark.

And I feel your pain. Believe me. I almost cry when I put on my new clothes and they are tight or I think I look like a blob.

Here is a great article on AM and it's written by that chick you like Paleo Pepper

[http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/](http://http://www.paleoforwomen.com/how-extremity-can-make-even-the-best-diet-fail/)

Also, I decided to follow the bulletproof exec's wife's diet for being healthy for baby. Do it now and in three years you will have rockstar baby. I KNOW it. Also she recommended this exercise called T-Tapp. I'm trying it now and I'll let you know how it goes.

[http://www.betterbabybook.com/](http://http://www.betterbabybook.com/)

And no, sweetie, you won't be a lard-arse. Be KIND to yourself. K?

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-25T16:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33431>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33537 wrote:** How much did you have to gain when before TOM made an appearance? I know this sounds vain...but I've bought so many cute new clothes and jeans to fit my small new body, and most I'm going to have to give away as I become a lard \*ss. Ugh. My husband was pretty supportive when I told him I needed to gain weight, as we has been telling me the same thing for months. He suggested I start lifting weights, so I don't just get squishy as I gain.

You'll have to change your thinking if you want this to work out.

None of this really has to do with "needing to gain weight" or whether you are "too thin" or not. It's about normalizing your hunger signaling, eating patterns and hormones along the way. Once they are re-established, the weight will settle wherever it needs to be for your body to be healthy.

I suggest you focus on eating properly and becoming healthy instead of the scale going up.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:35:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33451>

Question? How do I respond with multi-quotes? I clicked on the "quote +" button, but then what?

Before I respond to all of your kind words, here are a few more gems from Dr. K:

1\. I'm up to 300 mcg of t3/day, and he said the reason I'm on such a high dose is because it's all going to Rt3. **I need to get this checked.** I didn't take my evening dose last night and woke up to a temp of 98.6. Since I've been tracking my temps, I have NEVER seen my temp go that high. Not sure what that means, and I'm also night sure WHY it's all going to Rt3?????

2\. No CT. I have really struggled to do it, and he said it is only stressing my body further, so don't worry about it.

3\. He did say to focus on circadian rhythms, so I have to get more religious about getting to bed and wearing my blue blockers.

4\. Because I've been on reduced energy reserves and increased cortisol from exercising my whole life, my body is just plain tuckered out.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:37:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33452>

> **KiwiLauren;33547 wrote:** Wow, PaleoCowgirl. That's a BIG consultation with some BIG revelations. What I hear is that you feel he is spot on - 'that's me in a nutshell' and that how this happened is making sense. I think this is really important. Because it now means that the plan is your plan, not someone else's, and it's for your specific health, not for someone else's idea of health or for weight loss.
>
>
>
> You ask 'how fat do I have to get?' and I think that is the 'worst-enemy voice' talking. You said Dr K suggested you gain 10-15 pound. One, that is NOT fat. Two, you know how much to gain. Seems like a bit of fat on your body is important, just like fat in your food. Fat is equating with health here. And goodness. And life. Fat nourishes your body, your hormones, your baby-making abilities. There is nothing to fear here. Hurray. (You will still be beautiful with 10 extra pounds on you!)
>
>
>
> I have just paid for my first consult (no time scheduled yet) and I can feel the nervousness in me about what may be revealed. So I can only imagine how hard it is to hear all this really tough stuff and how much is at stake. I don't have any easy answers or words of wisdom in that department, except be kind to yourself. Always. Treat yourself with the same kindness that you dole out to everyone else on this forum. Self-care is the route that will get you to optimal, not beating yourself for having so far to go.
>
>
>
> Hang in there.

Thank you for the kind words. I do need to wrap myself around the idea of gaining weight. I liked how I looked last summer weighing about 5 lbs. more than I weigh right now, and I could have happily stayed there if I would have ever stabilized. Last summer, people commented on how good I looked; this summer, they seem more concerned and less congratulatory about my weight...so I guess there's my answer.

By the way, it's okay to be nervous about your consult. Dr. K asked I was I was doing, and I said I was nervous, and he said, "Well, you should be. I have some serious sh\*t to tell you." YIKES! Anyway, he does a great job of explaining that serious sh\*t, so at least I have a plan of attack. Good luck with your consult!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:44:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33455>

> **vkiernan;33554 wrote:** I feel really bad for you cuz I'm sure you are devastated over the thought alone. I just gained 5 lbs from the HC I've been on and I know how bad that is making me feel. But, just a couple of thoughts: 1.) Sometimes one preceives themselves differently than others. You probably do need to gain. Having to gain weight might actually make you more attractive especially to your dh (a little fat in the right places is very feminine). I always thought I wanted to be super thin but my sister is soo tiny now that I NEVER, EVER want to look like her. Can't even hug her appropriately as there is nothing to hug. 2.) Gaining some weight doesn't mean you will be a fat \*ss. Did he tell you how much weight you need to gain?
>
>
>
> If your dh is telling you to gain, then he sees you different than you do and you probably do need to gain. I do understand about the weight gain though. No one wants to. Go slow and enjoy the ride! I can tell you that I have missed out on a lot of enjoyment because "I" thought I was too fat to wear this or that and kept telling myself when I lose a few lbs. I will dress like this. I am not fat still today but still have that screwed up mind. Why do we think no one will love us if we gain a few? I'm sure if you gain a few lbs. no one will probably notice and as for the cute little clothes, 1 size larger will still be very cute and you will still be awesome! Try not to stress over this cuz you know it will make you gain for sure, that whole cortisol thing. This really is a chance for you to get to know your body too. You will know what makes you gain and how much and how fast.

Thanks for the kind words. Dr. K said to gain 10-15 lbs., which is frustrating because it's almost back to my pre-hcg weight...it's like, why did I mess with a good thing, and why did I think I was so fat? I wish I had never done it, if I had known then what I do now. The trouble wasn't the hcg though, it was the underlying problems I had before it that were made worse by the stress of losing so much weight in a year.

I know what you mean though about super skinny women. Two of my closest friends are size 0, and I was always the big, corn-fed, country girl around them, and FINALLY, I was the same size as them! It felt great. It's frustrating that they can be "healthy," or then again, maybe they aren't, and I have to gain weight and be larger again to be at my optimal... this is just me feeling sorry for myself though. I need to get over it!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:45:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33457>

> **vkiernan;33555 wrote:** Ok, PCG, I didn't read your whole recap from your consult. WOW! If the good doc is telling you 10-15 lbs to gain, that is serious. I would not be looking at this from a weight gain stand point but more of a HEALTH standpoint. Sounds like you need to get it in gear cuz optimal isn't coming your way if you don't and worse things might be coming your way. How tall are you and how much do you weigh now? I am picturing very very thin. Fix yourself now before you get old. It is bxxxx to do it later. Trust me on that one. Still after a year, I am struggling. Hell, I am struggling to even find a doc who can deal with everything I have going on. So very frustrating.
>
>
>
> As for how much you will have to gain? I think your body will tell you when TOM returns and stays. You have a journey but can make it. I wish you the best.

I'm not stepping on the scale these days, as I'm sure my weight is way up. I've been abusing my body with food, and the last time I weighed myself I was at 118. My lowest was at 109 before all the binging started back up again. I'm between 5'3 and 5'4.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T17:50:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33458>

> **Jennifer;33562 wrote:** My advice is not to focus on the number. Just eat. Eat if you are hungry (Still do BAB and three meals max a day) but don't try to "diet" and do Leptin Rx. I know my head sometimes try to determine how much I should eat instead of my body. How it happened for me was I had an emotional trigger (my bff is my same age and told me she is pregnant with baby #5) and it sent me eating like crazy. Honestly, I couldn't tell you how much I gained because I stopped weighing, but it's in the 10-15 range. I def don't recommend a week long bender as your method to gain but eat. Eat fat and protein and fish and don't "push" yourself to do just two meals a day. If you need three, then eat three. I put the weight on in April. Finally got TOM in June. I could see signs of ovulation before TOM came, so figure that as a ballpark.
>
> And I feel your pain. Believe me. I almost cry when I put on my new clothes and they are tight or I think I look like a blob.

>
> [http://www.betterbabybook.com/](http://http://www.betterbabybook.com/)
>
>
>

These are great links! Thanks! Paleo Pepper is so easy to relate to, at least to me. Keep me posted on the Better Baby Diet.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-25T22:55:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33526>

Turns out when you eat poorly, it makes you feel like crap. The last two months have been plagued with repeat binges, and as I detox from my latest, my head pounds, my clarity is shot, I'm unproductive, crabby and bloated. Yuck. My first instinct, of course, is to fast and eat little until I feel better, but I have a new focus on BAB with lots of fat, and I'm not stepping on the scale or worrying about it right now. My temp was up this morning and I observed some watery CM, so maybe I've ovulated. If so, I would have TOM would be right around the corner. Maybe?

Also, random question, I have some cabbage and a ton of radishes from my garden...anybody have good suggestions for recipes?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-25T23:03:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33528>

Look for the more egg white consistency to indicate ovulation. And great plan. To get different results, you gotta do something different.

As for the cabbage, ferment your own sauerkraut! So good for leaky gut! And radishes I like to use like potato. Either boiled or with eggs like hash browns (or in ground beef). [http://balancedbites.com/2012/01/easy-recipe-raw-sauerkraut-fermented-probiotic-food.html](http://http://balancedbites.com/2012/01/easy-recipe-raw-sauerkraut-fermented-probiotic-food.html)

My doc is great! I just called her up, and I'm going to get blood drawn to officially test for hashis, as well as checking my rt3 and iron levels. This should give me an idea of where I'm at and hopefully help me understand why I'm not absorbing the t3.

Once these results are in, I also want to see her and get an ultrasound to see if I have pcos or if its ha. I'm also looking at ordering blood spot/saliva labs to check my LH, FSH and a complete female hormone profile. Is there anything else I'm missing that might prove useful to know?
