### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-26T22:08:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33751>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33847 wrote:** I know in my heart you are right, Souldanzer. It's just a tough reality...and a bit depressing. But, if TOM comes, I know I'm on the right track. I actually want it to reappear more than I want to be a size 0. I need to focus on that.
>
>
>
> I'm going grocery shopping tonight, as our pantry and fridge are completely empty. Part of me wants to leave it that way, so I have nothing to binge on, but part of me knows I need to get some veggies and make some decent meals around here. Anybody have any favorite paleo dishes? I think part of my issue is I'm bored with my food, so bad stuff sounds so much better.

I went through it too...the fear of "will I ever stop gaining" and I DID stop gaining but it came on pretty fast in the beginning. Sometimes you have to go backward to go forward on the right track.

Recipes...yes, I agree when I get into a food rut it leads to eating off program. Eating AI can be so yummy! Do you have Dr K's cookbook?

I know lately it's been hard to keep up with trying recipes because life is so rushed, but I have a few I go to a lot. I'm eating a lot of avocado right now. I blend it with all kinds of stuff to make gauc or ice cream or pudding treats.

[http://www.thrive-style.com/2012/03/dark-chocolate-avocado-pudding/](http://http://www.thrive-style.com/2012/03/dark-chocolate-avocado-pudding/)

I love mustard in avocado with lime juice and some kickin spices. Pair that with fish and good to go.

I like this recipe for coconut curry and it's very adaptable, I've done it stovetop with shrimp and kale and squash. Mix it up:

[http://paleopot.com/2011/12/easy-paleo-crock-pot-chicken-curry-with-peppers-and-cabbage/](http://http://paleopot.com/2011/12/easy-paleo-crock-pot-chicken-curry-with-peppers-and-cabbage/)

And I love this spicy salmon

[http://everydaypaleo.com/2010/05/31/grill-it-up-salmon-two-ways/](http://http://everydaypaleo.com/2010/05/31/grill-it-up-salmon-two-ways/)

and this is a great little recipe for tuna or salmon salad, especially if you can eat eggs!

[http://everydaypaleo.com/2012/04/21/two-minute-tuna-salad/#more-4323](http://http://everydaypaleo.com/2012/04/21/two-minute-tuna-salad/#more-4323)

I also did pulled pork in the crock pot with Zevia Root Beer. Super simple

Last night I ground up liver and made meatballs. I modified this recipe according to the spices I had on hand:

[http://primalonadime.com/2012/04/22/easy-beef-liver-meatballs/](http://http://primalonadime.com/2012/04/22/easy-beef-liver-meatballs/)

(and I used a lot less liver, more like 4:1 beef to liver). I put cinnamon in some of them and liked them the best, but I love cinnamon in everything...LOL

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-26T23:15:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33760>

> **PaleoCowgirl;33896 wrote:** I do have Dr. K's book, but most of them aren't AI protocol friendly. Are there some out of there that you have tried and liked? Thanks for the recipe ideas. I'm thinking avocados sound really good right now, so I'm definitely going to try your suggestions. I just ordered two paleo cookbooks, too, for inspiration, so we will see if that helps.
>
>
>
> You always offer really kind words of support, and I appreciate it, Jennifer. From the time you started gaining weight until TOM reappeared, can I ask how much you had to gain and how long it took? I know my experience won't be the same as yours, but hopefully it might be similar.

I know you're scared, so I will answer you but I don't think you should focus on a number. Let your body go where it needs to go for healing....I gained about 2-3 pounds a week for a month/5 weeks. That was April. So roughly two months after gaining 10-15. Can't tell you for sure because after Dr K said it was ruining my adernals, I stoped the scale. Even so, you know. Your pants are tight. It sucks. But your health is more important. I believe Dr K completely when he says becoming Leptin Sensitive trumps every thing else. Just be gentle with yourself. I'm kind of confused about what the heck is going on with me now that I'm cycling again. Weird appetite and cravings. I guess, for so long I was trying to MAKE my body do what I wanted and do what I wanted at any cost. Don't war with your body. Your body will win, so better to just let it! Good luck.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-27T00:10:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33771>

HPA amenorrhea:

> Ghrelin and Agouti both stimulate hunger but in AN, they are uncoupled from their master controller leptin, while IL-6 levels are quite elevated and **fuel the conversion of T4 and T3 to make high levels of reverse T3. This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.
>
>
> **The high cortisol also directly affects the Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis too.** Cortisol directly blocks 5′ deiodinase enzyme that converts T4 to T3 (this occurs in the liver). Cortisol Releasing Hormone (CRH) (seen elevated in high cortisol production states) directly blocks TSH. The implication is huge because Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) and T3, the active thyroid hormone, are inhibited quickly in this process. Immediately, any excess T4 is then shunted to reverse T3. Reverse T3 is a COMPETITIVE inhibitor to T3, the active thyroid hormone.** This basically turns off the thyroid! (Youâ€™re welcome ladies.), This is a biological switch needed to shut metabolism off in starvation mode. This is precisely what happens in starvation or in anorexia. Once T3 is turned off, no fat burning can occur at the muscles with UCP3. Remember UCP3 activation requires T4, T3 and Leptin to be working well. With high cortisol it cannot. Shutting off these things could be good biologically, if youâ€™re badly starving or if youâ€™re morbidly obese.

Your level of T3 supplementation is suspect, I'm sorry to say. Plenty of people have abused thyroid meds in an attempt to lose weight. If you're dealing with HPA amenorrhea, I'm not sure there is a point in out-supplementing the underlying issue no matter how badly your thyroid is off.

Did your doc tell you to take that much T3? Or is this self-medicating?

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-06-27T01:50:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33802>

I'm so sorry you have to go through this PaleoCowgirl. I know how scary it might be to be told you have to gain weight, especially after putting so much effort in losing the weight. Once you'll gain a little healthy pounds, you'll feel better and probbaly won't have that urge to binge anymore. And best of all, your body will become baby ready. It's really true that our body knows best. It won't let you have a baby unless you are healthy enough. And once you'll be, you'll realize how silly it was to stress about 10 lbs or so.

I don't think you'll get fat with more fat and BAB. Sugar would make you fat, but good fats will help hormones and your brain rewire. Lack of fat in the diet often leads to sugar cravings...

I was talking with a friend of mine today about my journey with bulimia. I was so, so obsessed about my weight, I was sure I was FAT at 125 lbs (5'5''). I screwed myself up, and now almost 35 lbs later, I am really FAT. But I feel soooo much better. Ok, I'm not optimal, but I'm so much more healthier than I was, I'm free of binging (thank's God) and I don't have to fight against myself everytime I have food in front of me. The weight botters me, I won't lie to you, but I know it will come off at the right time. I was thinking of doing HCG, but reading your story and Jennifer's makes me a little scary.

You have to do this for yourself. And as V said, you better do it while your young, it's gonna be much easier even if it's gonna be pretty long as Dr K predicted.

And thank's for dharing your story, I hope you'll continue journaling as you go through this.

k

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-27T05:22:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33875>

I asked Stefani (Paleo Pepper) for some advice about what I'm going through, and I thought I would post what she had to say, so maybe her words could help someone else, too. She blogs at Paleo For Women and has gone through similar scenarios. I really encourage anyone struggling with ED to check it out.

....I think it's both PCOS and HA. I also think that 123 is a great weight for someone who is 5'4... even a few more pounds. Does that put you around a size 3-5? **Totally sexy. Totally!

The problem is: when women lose weight, they lose estrogen production. So your estrogen levels have dropped, and apparently they have dropped lower than your pituitary gland and ovaries are comfortable with. This means that your pituitary is not getting the estrogen signaling it needs to trigger menstruation, so you have stopped menstruating. But your body is still trying in fits and starts to make it happen, which has given you the cysts. At least, I think this is possibly what has happened to you. I've seen it a fair number of times before.

**One thing that will help you is reducing any kind of stress in your life. I mean this very, very seriously. And to recover and eat as many calories as your body is asking for-- as what you experienced is pretty dramatic weight loss, and very stressful to your metabolism. Another will be to make sure you include carbohydrates in your diet. I think you might find that if you eat a meal high in carbohydrate, you will feel a bit more sexual or fertile... however that might appear to your particular body (for example, having more vaginal discharge). This is because carbohydrates spike insulin which spikes leptin, which is the hormone our fat cells normally send to the hypothalamus to assure it that we are well fed. Eating carbohydrates "tricks" the body at least a tiny bit into thinking that it has more fat on it than it actually does.

But the thing about those carbs is that they are a short term fix. **What really is the only thing I have seen consistently work for women is putting on weight. Not a ton, mind you, but a few pounds here or there, and slowly. **If you can inch up your BMI and still be within the "normal" range-- as I expect you can-- this might be your most powerful fertility tool.** That's not easy for a lot of women to hear, but it is (possibly) your truth. It is, definitely, my truth. So I know what it feels like-- I know so well.

But putting on weight... that is also just a part of eating more calories and not being restrictive in general. I bet you have seen my post on fasting for women. It's a bit deal. Fasting convinces our bodies that we are starving. I would recommend to you, therefore, to eat more, smaller meals throughout the day. Definitely. I have read multiple times on hypothalamic amenorrhea forums that women need to eat consistently throughout the day in order to recover their fertility.

So: possibly put on weight, definitely eat more calories, don't exercise more than a couple of times each week, and eat carbohydrates, at least 100g/day.**

Those are my general prescriptions, in any case.

And finally, how to stop bingeing/restricting. Well. That would take a novel, and several years. But I can tell you what the most powerful tools are: forgiveness, and love, and and a focus on the future**. Let go of the idea of a 'perfect' body, and look in the mirror as little as possible. Recognize that your eating behavior is a demon that has happened to you-- not something that you have made happen yourself-- and that when you overeat, it's not your fault.** You must forgive yourself for eating. It's not your fault. So let it go. **Think of a binge as something hurtful that has happened to you, and move forward with nourishment and forgiveness and sympathy for yourself and love rather than anger and hate. And do not restrict after a binge. I know-- it's hard not to. Let yourself eat less if you feel like eating less. But don't if you don't. **The thing about bingeing and restricting is that restricting has to stop first. Restricting necessarily begets bingeing, so unless you stop restricting first, there's a fairly good chance they will persist in you longer than you would like.

The same for bingeing goes for cravings. Forgive yourself for having them, and let them roll off, or even laugh their way off, of your shoulders. Try transferring your cravings and overeating behavior to progressively healthier and more natural foods over time. If you normally binge on cake, for example, try moving first to fruits, and then to sweet potatoes, and then carrots. If you crave cake, think: "how would a few pounds of sweet potatoes go down instead?" and see if that satisfies you just as much. It's surely just a bandaid on the overeating problem, but it's helpful for women trying to navigate these complicated waters. One step at a time.

And focus on nourishment rather than deprivation. Think of food in terms of "I can have more. Do I want more?" rather than "Will I let myself have more?"** And think of your body as the great, beautiful thing that gives you life, and that deserves your love, rather than something that should be punished for not fitting into society's norms. That's silly. Society is dumb, but your body is brilliant.**

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-27T20:07:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-33998>

> Ghrelin and Agouti both stimulate hunger but in AN, they are uncoupled from their master controller leptin, while IL-6 levels are quite elevated and fuel the conversion of T4 and T3 to make high levels of reverse T3. This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.
>
> The high cortisol also directly affects the Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis too. Cortisol directly blocks 5′ deiodinase enzyme that converts T4 to T3 (this occurs in the liver). Cortisol Releasing Hormone (CRH) (seen elevated in high cortisol production states) directly blocks TSH. The implication is huge because Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) and T3, the active thyroid hormone, are inhibited quickly in this process. Immediately, any excess T4 is then shunted to reverse T3. Reverse T3 is a COMPETITIVE inhibitor to T3, the active thyroid hormone. This basically turns off the thyroid! (Youâ€™re welcome ladies.), This is a biological switch needed to shut metabolism off in starvation mode. This is precisely what happens in starvation or in anorexia. Once T3 is turned off, no fat burning can occur at the muscles with UCP3. Remember UCP3 activation requires T4, T3 and Leptin to be working well. With high cortisol it cannot. Shutting off these things could be good biologically, if youâ€™re badly starving or if youâ€™re morbidly obese.
>
> > My brain isn't getting this 100% yet. But I am very interested in this since I know my T3 ratio is 6%, so I was interested in supplementing T3 but if its all gonna go to rT3...hmmm...testing,right?

This biochemical maneuver completely uncouples the thyroid, and it can no longer work well. When this occurs it directly affects the pulsatile release of GnRH and shuts off the ability to have a menses. It also destroys the LH surge that is normally associated with oocyte release monthly.
> >
> > The high cortisol also directly affects the Hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis too. Cortisol directly blocks 5′ deiodinase enzyme that converts T4 to T3 (this occurs in the liver). Cortisol Releasing Hormone (CRH) (seen elevated in high cortisol production states) directly blocks TSH. The implication is huge because Thyroid Stimulating Hormone (TSH) and T3, the active thyroid hormone, are inhibited quickly in this process. Immediately, any excess T4 is then shunted to reverse T3. Reverse T3 is a COMPETITIVE inhibitor to T3, the active thyroid hormone. This basically turns off the thyroid! (You’re welcome ladies.), This is a biological switch needed to shut metabolism off in starvation mode. This is precisely what happens in starvation or in anorexia. Once T3 is turned off, no fat burning can occur at the muscles with UCP3. Remember UCP3 activation requires T4, T3 and Leptin to be working well. With high cortisol it cannot. Shutting off these things could be good biologically, if you’re badly starving or if you’re morbidly obese.

**

"Because optimal is within your grasp....if you choose it."**

That's the key point here in my journey, isn't it? I could choose to stay stick thin and meet society's standards, or I could follow your advice and get to optimal and have a baby. Sometimes the right path isn't the easiest path, and I have a lot to learn along the way. Your post really means a lot to me, as I've been grappling with my self-worth as I feel somewhat like a failure...I failed my body. I failed to lose weight AND stay healthy. I failed my future babies. Basically, I've been having a little pity party with myself, but I have been eating, not restricting and not binging, just eating! And, it feels right. For the first time, even though my jeans are tight, my diet feels right.

I know I need to change my mindset first and foremost and know that I'm making positive changes with your RX. You fuel my fire, Dr. Jack Kruse. I'm making baby steps with my husband, and my doctor is willing to order the labs I request, so I'm on my way.

If you stop back, could you recommend which labs I should order? I'm thinking HGH, FSH, LH and a hormone and cortisol panel. Anything else? I will order from ZRT. This week, I'm getting blood drawn to test my ft3 and rt3 and for hashis. I will post in my journal my results.

### BJK77 — 2012-06-28T03:21:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34084>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34014 wrote:** I asked Stefani (Paleo Pepper) for some advice about what I'm going through, and I thought I would post what she had to say, so maybe her words could help someone else, too. She blogs at Paleo For Women and has gone through similar scenarios. I really encourage anyone struggling with ED to check it out.

PCG,

Thank you for sharing this with us. I just started reading about your journey and could see myself in your posts. It actually just brought me to tears. I'm not overweight (never have been), but I've always felt like I was never thin enough, never good enough, just never enough.

I never really dieted or exercised, but I got to my thinnest a few years ago before I crashed hard. I started eating a vegetarian diet and doing an extremely intense workout program when I suddenly started to develop all my food intolerances and my health just went downhill extremely fast and yet I was happy that I was so thin! Of course I was upset and scared about the new health issues I found myself dealing with, but ironically I finally felt like my body was perfect. I was 5'6" and only about 106 lbs!! Looking back on it now I can see that I was sick in so many ways - mentally, physically, emotionally. I wasn't restricting food really. I didn't go hungry, but I didn't necessarily eat what I knew I should have been eating.

I have gained weight since then and I'm trying to look at it as a blessing, that my body is strong enough to put on some weight now and I'm hopeful this is a sign of good things to come. I know I gain super easily if I eat more than 30-40 grams of carbs in a day, so I generally limit my carbs, but I always tell myself I'm doing it to be in line with the leptin rx. Truthfully it's probably more because I don't want the weight gain.

I had my consult with Dr. K last week and he believes my main problems are my adrenals and thyroid. I just ordered some T3 to start since I was on nature-throid / synthroid previously. If I'm being honest with myself, I'm hoping that the T3 will take off a little of the weight I've put on . . . I don't want to think that way, but it's constantly there! I had a friend tell me last week that I looked great. She confided in my that last year she feared for me because I "looked like a skeleton." We have such a skewed perception of our own reality. Scary stuff!

Sorry - I'm rambling now! Just wanted to share with you that you are so not alone. I hate that I would ever think of something as superficial as weight being more important than overall health, but that's my reality. I'm desperately trying to get over that and I think I'm making headway as I know you will too. Actually just writing this out has been so therapeutic!

You will be fine PCG! You will find your way to optimal

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-28T03:29:43+0300

If I'm being honest with myself, I'm hoping that the T3 will take off a little of the weight I've put on . . . I don't want to think that way, but it's constantly there! I'm desperately trying to get over that and I think I'm making headway as I know you will too. Actually just writing this out has been so therapeutic!

> You will be fine PCG! You will find your way to optimal

I'm sorry for your struggles! Thanks for sharing your story. Losing weight is somewhat of an addiction I'm discovering. Since I've been big my whole life, I don't know any other way than to diet! Be careful with the t3. I lost 8 lbs. in a month on it, which is how I got to my crazy low at 109. You're taller than me; I have a feeling 118 is a nice spot to be. That's what I was weighing when Dr. K told me to gain 10-15 lbs. Please be strong and don't fall into that trap again.

I'm gaining weight now, and while I know it's for the best, part of me hates it and hates myself. I feel gross and puffy. I liked feeling my ribs at night and thinking, at least I'm thin. I know that sounds sick, and I guess it is, I was in denial for so long...but if I'm honest with myself, I didn't really look good at that weight.

I'm choosing health not skinny. It's a tough choice (actually it should be an easy one), but I'm going to conquer my beast, and so can you!

Cheers to our road to optimal! Thanks again for stopping by.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-28T03:41:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34088>

Advice from Abetterme:

Don't want to lose this!!!

It's still a work in progress, and I guess it will always be, but if I could tell you what helped me, it would be those points:

1) BAB. At first, it was 85g of protein to make sure I would have any cravings. Then it was 70-ish grams, and not it's somewhere between 50 and 60g, but sometimes higher.

2) Enough fat. I can feel it when I don't have enough. I can't find any lard here, and sometimes I'm just tired of CO. I do my own ghee too, and the BPC if the best way to get my fat. When I was still craving sweet at the beginning, I was having CO even if that wasn't meal time. It's better than sugar even if I was off with the schedule...

3) I can't have nuts, coconut manna in the house. Everytime I buy some telling myself I'll be fine this time, I'm not. I bought almonds last week-end and ate them in 2 sittings. I don't consider it as binging (because before I would have ate tons of food after without being able to stop myself), but I know it's a no-go. I'm getting better with dark chocolate. I don't feel the urge to eat it all when I have some. But if I do, I don't feel bad, it's sugar free and full of fat

4)I also have a difficult time with fruits and sweet potatoes. When I buy berries, I eat them all in no time. I bought cherries yesterday. I choose the smallest size to make sure, but they're all gone. Everytime I tell myself I shouldn't but any anymore. Same with sweet potatoes. If I start and have a few, I'll want more and more.

5)Over the last year and a half, I've learned to listen to satiety and fullness signs. This one is difficult and it takes times. Most of the time I still eat 3 meals a day, but I listen to myself especially at lunch time and dinner. Lunch is always pretty small (protein and avocado), but I have it anyway because if I get too hungry in the afternoon, I might eat whatever come in sight and eat beyond fullness. You're hungry when you feel it in your stomac, not when you want to eat. If you are not sure, you can have water and wait a little. If you are not hungry for a steak, you probably are not hungry, period. When you eat and food doesn't sound as tasty as it was when you began, it's a sign you are not hungry anymore. You have to listen carefully and not eat too fast.

6) I'm not as hard on myself as I used to be. That feels good I'm not perfect, and I don't have to be. Sometimes I eat stuff I shouldn't, but that's ok, I'll go back on track on the next meal. I've always had a hard time not eating at night when not sleeping. Those last 2 weeks, I can't go to sleep before 3 o'clock in the morning, and I get hungry at midnight or one. Sometimes I eat, sometimes I don't. I now I shouldn't, but I don't beat myself up with that and I get up with my BAB the next morning.

7) I stopped weighting myself. Scale is only a number, I don't want to focuss my life on that anymore. I want to be healthy and strong. I go by how I feel, and not by how I weight.

8) Learning that binging is not psychological or emotional, but physiological. If I want to binge, it's not because I'm stressed/sad/angry. It's because there is something unbalanced inside me. I wish I knew that before!! Usually if I want to binge it's because I lack sleep, I haven't had enough fat or I had sugar and I want more and more!! Understanding what is happening is helping me a lot!

9) Journaling. It help me a lot. And couting the binge-free days too when I started. I wa smotivated by the number going up and didn't want to go back to zero. Now I've lost count, I have to go to previous messages to do the count. I know I'm approching the 100 days mark, and I'm so proud of it. I know however that I'm still vulnerable to a binge, and I'll probably will be for a while, but seeing the progress is really great.

It's not vain to want to fit in tiny clothes or be as thin as your friends. I know you didn't chose that over health and having a baby. But I know it's a constant battle. When I was in that circle, I thought I was having the control on my body. But at the end, I was always losing. I wasn't happy even if I was thin, because I was obsessing with food, the scale, staying away from social events, isolating myslef from my friends and familly. I was always feeling tired, stressed, puffy and gross. Talk about having control! I was also ao angry at myself, I coudn't see the end of that. I just wanted to be normal again! I sometimes wonder where I would be now if I didn't start the leptin reset protocol. I would be in some miserable place beating myself up and praying for some help. If you change your goal from "being thin" to "being healthy", at some point the size of your clothes won't matter to you anymore because you'll feel good about yourself and your body.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-06-28T19:04:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34188>

> **Jack Kruse;34300 wrote:** your thinking improves your body awfully fast when you let your cells heal your thought via your new actions..........cellular health dictates your thinking.......always.

Just read this over at Whole 9 - pretty much the same thing Dr K is saying - our gut flora is protective of our ability to deal with stress! BIOLOGY influencing psychology

Prevention of gut leakiness by a probiotic treatment leads to attenuated HPA response to an acute psychological stress.

Translation: Improving gut barrier integrity by improving the balance of gut microbiota (bacteria) leads to reduced reactivity when faced with psychological stress. In other words, a happy, intact gut improves your stress "resilience". The gut, the immune system, the neurological system, the adrenals... it's all the same story.

[http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306453012001291](http://http://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0306453012001291)

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-29T18:26:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34371>

Good morning, everyone! I'm battling some demons yet...I don't know why I'm so in denial about fruit, but I CAN'T have any, plain and simple. Same for carrots...it triggered my sweet tooth yesterday...go figure. I gotta stay away from stevia, too. And, I had requested coconut manna at my health store a long time ago, and they called me yesterday to let me know it was in. Yeah, I'm NOT going to go get that either, even though I love it, unless I love binging. Which, I don't. I've got to kill the sweet tooth and never invite it back into my life. It's trouble.

I've been feeling pretty down on life, not very impressed with the hand of cards God has dealt me. I've been depressed, crabby and not myself since my consult with Dr. K...probably because the light finally clicked on, and I finally listened to advice that has been given to me over and over again that I never wanted to hear. Anybody ever do insanity workouts or p90x with Shawn T? Shawn T is a motivator in the tough love kind of way, and it works for me....Dr. K is the Shawn T of the health world, and apparently I needed a kick in the butt.

I recently read the book, "Heaven is for real," and the Dad fights with God while his son was being operated on in a life or death scenario. He whined to God and yelled at him and asked why he deserved any of this, and the son told him that God heard him and answered his prayers. I've been wrestling with God this week, and while part of me wants to quit this fight completely, I know I can't.

So...I'm putting my boxing gloves on, and I've going to kick this battle square on its a\*\*. I've made some poor food decisions lately in my anguish, and that's probably why I'm so miserable...so it's back to keto-paleo....no messing around...but I'm not going to restrict either.

I want optimal, and it starts today.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T18:33:17+0300

you do P90X and you will hurt yourself and your brain.........you need to back off. Discuss this with your Reproductive Endo and I bet you'll get read the riot act.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-29T18:38:29+0300

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T18:41:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34376>

I couldn't eat carrots either during certain parts of my recovery. Don't sweat it. You now know more. Keep collecting information.

Practice Ahimsa with yourself.... been reading and thinking about this today myself. Hard exercise qualifies as self-harm in your case. Gentle walking can sometimes take the edge off the need to move hard for me.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-29T19:05:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34387>

Good luck PCG! You do sound determined to knock the negative thinking & behaviour in the butt! You will do it. You mentioned p90x. I used to do it a couple of years ago & am reminded of it everytime I walk into my living room (hence the chin-up bar in the doorway)! I actually love the fact that I \*can't\* exercise during the reset. Absolutely love it!!! I hope when the time is right I'll \*want\* to again but for now I am savouring every moment of the Reset Rx that prescribes No Exercise!!!

As I think about it, some of my thinking has gradually been changing. Like I just mentioned, I love not exercising, love eating rib steak as often as I want, love eating my fatty BAB's, etc. I may not be seeing results on the outside or on the scale because there's probably a mess inside of me that's I'm not aware of yet but I know things are going in the right direction. I think the more you \*do\* the right things the more your thinking will align and vice versa. You will get there!!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T19:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34393>

> Shawn T is a motivator in the tough love kind of way, and it works for me....Dr. K is the Shawn T of the health world, and apparently I needed a kick in the butt.

Contemplate that you and your body might need the exact opposite.... gentleness. You don't feel well, you now know that you \*are not\* well.... maybe it's time to stop fighting and relieve the dissonance within yourself. Who are you really kicking/hurting when you put on those boxing gloves?

You've got to let it all go.... "going to pieces without falling apart".... surrender to what is..... start where you are. You don't need to fix it all in one day. You don't need to be perfect today. Just set one little goal that you know you can reach successfully. It takes a while to build a stable base to build your life on.

Be gentle. Learn to be in unison with your body again. It's giving you a whole lot of information about what it needs every day. Learn to listen to what it is telling you again. Gentle. You've already tried the forceful way and it didn't get you to where you want to be....

>
> You've got to let it all go.... "going to pieces without falling apart".... surrender to what is..... start where you are. You don't need to fix it all in one day. You don't need to be perfect today. Just set one little goal that you know you can reach successfully. It takes a while to build a stable base to build your life on.
>
>
>
> Be gentle. Learn to be in unison with your body again. It's giving you a whole lot of information about what it needs every day. Learn to listen to what it is telling you again. Gentle. You've already tried the forceful way and it didn't get you to where you want to be....

Jennifer told me the same thing last night...quit fighting it and just let your body do the talking. Perhaps you're both right...I just need to relax and go with the flow...but what I'm fighting is the part of me that just wants to quit altogether, that tells myself that I'm not worth it, and I'm tired of dealing with all this. That part needs to go away.

### PaulaRichards — 2012-06-29T19:35:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34407>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34559 wrote:** Thanks for the kind words! I doubt I will ever do those workouts again. They were meant to be intense, and I don't want to raise my cortisol. I think lifting weights (in my case...throwing square bales on the farm LOL) or walking are great forms of exercise that don't stress the body out.

Awe! I grew up on a dairy farm; a family farm type, not a factory farm kind! We drank lots of raw milk back then!!!

### MartiD — 2012-06-29T19:35:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34409>

Mandy, I know you can pull though all this! Find something that makes you happy or at peace. I'm so envious that you are on a cattle ranch. I've told you that I was raised on a cattle ranch in OK, and although I don't think I appreciated it at the time, looking back I know that is what helped me battle family issues. I would get on my horse or take a walk with my dog and wonder through acres and acres of our land. I would play with the baby calves, enjoy the outdoors, smell fresh (mostly!) air, play in the pond, etc. I never watched TV, mostly because we couldn't get any channels where we were so my best friends were my irish setter, my white horse and a goat named Billy! What I would give for those days again. What I would give to have my kids have that type of a life!

Everyone is right about letting go of the anger and disappointment. Re-direct your energy on something positive. You will heal much faster I believe. Stay strong girl!

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-29T19:37:18+0300

You want to DO something and sometimes there is nothing to do but BAB, protein and fat and wait for the healing to work things out. Kind of boring...LOL

### KiwiLauren — 2012-06-30T06:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34541>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34560 wrote:** Jennifer told me the same thing last night...quit fighting it and just let your body do the talking. Perhaps you're both right...I just need to relax and go with the flow...but what I'm fighting is the part of me that just wants to quit altogether, that tells myself that I'm not worth it, and I'm tired of dealing with all this. That part needs to go away.

How about not fighting any part of you? Like Souldanzer says, the anger has intelligence. So does the part of you that wants to quit. No one 'part' of you has all the answers, but they all have a voice. When you fragment yourself and listen to only part of you, you end up in a mess (eg listening to the part that says being thin is the prize you're aiming for). You can allow any part of you to speak up. Then the greater you, the whole you, can then make a decision re: your next step. Personally, I don't think being perfect is ever the goal. Being whole and healthy and completely you is the goal - as soon as you are untrue to or unkind to yourself, you stop leading your life fully.

Dr K gave you the kick in the pants that actually pushed open the doors so you could get enough light to see the big picture. Seeing the whole thing - in its full chaotic glory - is scary. Which is why so many people would rather follow bad advice or have small goals. You've got bigger fish to fry. But you don't have to do it all at once.

Every choice you make - every one - either drains your energy or adds to it. Sometimes the simplest things are the biggest because they contain the blueprint of your whole life. I think it's easy to overlook that and think you must have some big plan all figured out. We're all muddling though finding our way. Hang in there.

A question I have, and maybe it's naive, but humor me: if I'm not cycling, what happens to all the build-up inside? If the eggs never flush, I assume I do get cysts, but what are the repercussions of these cysts, and how can they be flushed out?

### allison09 — 2012-06-30T23:16:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34676>

> **PaleoCowgirl;34753 wrote:**
>
>
>
> A question I have, and maybe it's naive, but humor me: if I'm not cycling, what happens to all the build-up inside? If the eggs never flush, I assume I do get cysts, but what are the repercussions of these cysts, and how can they be flushed out?

I'm no expert, but I haven't been cycling as well, for a while. I went to the Dr. a while ago and got a bunch of test done. She saw the ultra sound and told me that there was no build up. She then went on to tell me that because there was no build up, there's no reason to be concerned that I'm not cycling. I'm not sure I buy that, but maybe you're not accumulating any build up as well?

### Sem — 2012-07-01T01:36:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34696>

PCG - I've been a lurker here and just wanted to say hi. I can relate to some of your struggles. I've never had the best relationship with food myself and my hormones have been a mess for years. I did have every big shot fertility doctor in Beverly Hills tell me I would never get pregnant again after my first son was born due to premature ovarian dysfunction. I went to Accupuncture and took some DHEA ( totally willy nilly so not necessarily recommending it). I ended up pregnant the old fashioned way. When my doctor delivered my second son via c-section she called him a "miracle" due to the state of my ovaries. You are probably much younger than I was then (I was 37) so I feel really good that you can get it together for yourself and any future babies. Good luck.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-01T21:38:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34856>

I'm not sure I buy that, but maybe you're not accumulating any build up as well?
>
>
> Thanks for sharing! I'm going to request an ultrasound at my next appt. on July 18... in the meantime, I'm doing a full thyroid and hormone panel to see where I'm at. I suppose there's no use picturing the worst until you know for sure what you're dealing with.
>
> I would agree that not cycling at all is a cause for concern; I wonder why your doc wouldn't recognize that?
>
>
>
> Sem;34856 wrote:** PCG - I've been a lurker here and just wanted to say hi. I can relate to some of your struggles. I've never had the best relationship with food myself and my hormones have been a mess for years. I did have every big shot fertility doctor in Beverly Hills tell me I would never get pregnant again after my first son was born due to premature ovarian dysfunction. I went to Accupuncture and took some DHEA ( totally willy nilly so not necessarily recommending it). I ended up pregnant the old fashioned way. When my doctor delivered my second son via c-section she called him a "miracle" due to the state of my ovaries. You are probably much younger than I was then (I was 37) so I feel really good that you can get it together for yourself and any future babies. Good luck.

Hey Sem, thanks for the reassurance. I've heard of accupuncture for fertility but have never tried it. Can you tell me more about what they do for you? This could be something I would think of doing possibly. I've also heard of docs doing fertility massages; I would be up for that!

This weekend has been one of healing. My husband and I had a really good heart-to-heart about my struggles, he's loving that my curves are returning (even though none of my clothes fit), he can't keep his hands off me. I'm trying to feel sexy in my new skin (I'm at 125 now...so there's definitely a change...come on estrogen in my fat cells! Make something happen!) It's really hard to do when I got so used to being rail thin and everything that I tried on looked good. We went to a wedding last night...thankfully it's the last one until Aug. (We have 14 total this summer YIKES!) It's nice to be social, but then I'm out late, the food is never paleo-friendly, and I get out of my routine. I'm happy we have ZERO commitments in July, except for one cattle show at the end, so I can focus on healing.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-01T23:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34882>

> **PaleoCowgirl;35016 wrote:** This weekend has been one of healing. My husband and I had a really good heart-to-heart about my struggles, he's loving that my curves are returning (even though none of my clothes fit), he can't keep his hands off me. I'm trying to feel sexy in my new skin (I'm at 125 now...so there's definitely a change...come on estrogen in my fat cells! Make something happen!) .

Awesome! Try seeing yourself through his eyes! And honestly, I know this sounds crazy but I think my full length mirror in my house makes me look short and wide. I mean, I think it is warped or something 'cause when I pass some glass or a mirror when I'm not a home, I think, geez, I don't look that bad. So, when in doubt, blame the mirror! LOL

Oh, I so know how you feel - like, come on body, DOOO SOMETHING! LOL There was a point where as gross as it sounds, I wanted to eat an ovary or something, anything. Any way, I think once your hormones start coming around, you are going to feel so good that there is no way you'll want to go back to being skinny but miserable. I know that's how I feel. Thank god, it's like I'm alive again and almost the full and real "me." Nothing better than that.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-02T00:10:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34889>

found another good blog...she writes about PCOS and how it drives food cravings

[http://www.eat-real-food-paleodietitian.com/paleo-diet-and-pcos.html](http://http://www.eat-real-food-paleodietitian.com/paleo-diet-and-pcos.html)

She says insulin resistance is to blame (NOT YOU).

LIttle snippet:

PCOS needs to be taken seriously.

The good news is that there is A LOT you can do to improve your fertility, regulate your menstrual cycle and manage your PCOS symptoms… and it starts with what you put on your plate. The Paleo diet is the perfect template for improving PCOS.

Diagnosis

To be diagnosed with PCOS, you need to meet at least 2 of these 3 criteria: 1) irregular menstrual cycle, and/or; 2) high testosterone levels or symptoms of high testosterone levels, such as hirsutism or acne, and/or; 3) polycystic ovaries as seen on a vaginal ultrasound. (2)

The name polycystic ovarian syndrome implies that cysts on the ovaries are the underlying cause, but not all women with PCOS have polycystic ovaries. And some women have polycystic ovaries without having PCOS or any fertility problems. Actually, the name PCOS is not very accurate and should probably be changed…

Why? - Insulin Resistance and PCOS

Not all women with PCOS have the symptoms and not all cases of PCOS have the same root cause. However, for a majority of women with PCOS, not only those struggling with their weight but lean women as well, insulin resistance is to blame. Insulin resistance is a form of prediabetes and can disrupt your hormone balance.

Insulin is a very important hormone required to keep your blood sugar levels in check. Your pancreas produces insulin in response to carbohydrates to prevent your blood sugar levels from rising too high and allow the extra sugar derived from carbohydrates to be taken from your blood into your cells to be stored as fat. Without enough insulin, your blood sugar levels would go sky high, as it does in untreated type 1 diabetics.

The problem with women with PCOS dealing with insulin resistance is that their body becomes resistant to the action of insulin. The cells are supposed to “listen” to insulin when it says to do something about your blood sugar levels. With insulin resistance, it is like if your cells had become deaf and can’t hear insulin anymore. To try to make itself heard, the pancreas cranks up the music by producing more and more insulin. The hyperinsulinemia, or high insulin levels, associated with insulin resistance can affect your whole body, including your ovaries by triggering the production of more testosterone which affects your whole hormonal balance and lead to PCOS.

What Can You Do About Your PCOS?

For most women with PCOS for which high insulin levels and insulin resistance are behind their hormonal imbalances, improving your health, PCOS symptoms and fertility start by managing your blood sugar levels. Taking care of your blood sugar levels will automatically help your insulin levels, insulin resistance and ultimately restore your natural hormonal balance.

It is harder for most women with PCOS to achieve and maintain a healthier body weight

both because of the high insulin levels

and because of the strong cravings

caused by the hormonal imbalances.

The Paleo diet can help you control your weight

and manage your PCOS symptoms.

### Sem — 2012-07-02T01:22:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-34903>

Accupuncture was amazing! I went twice a week. She put a bunch of needles in me. It doesn't hurt at all and then I would pass out. She would come back an hour later and take out the needles. Every session started with her taking my pulse and looking at my tongue. I was about a week pregnant and she was able to tell by my pulse. She was also able to tell the sex of the baby by my pulse. Crazy. I also had to drink a really foul herbal tea mixture a few times a day. I have referred and others have referred many, many people to her. Most of them got pregnant. My one piece of advice is to make sure you go to the real deal. This woman was an OB in China. She came from a family of acupuncturists going back generations. Her husband was also an acupuncturist. I really should keep going to her for my other issues but she's quite a drive for me and it's not cheap - or covered by insurance!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-02T19:39:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35066>

> **Jennifer;35049 wrote:** found another good blog...she writes about PCOS and how it drives food cravings
>
> [http://www.eat-real-food-paleodietitian.com/paleo-diet-and-pcos.html](http://http://www.eat-real-food-paleodietitian.com/paleo-diet-and-pcos.html)
>
> She says insulin resistance is to blame (NOT YOU).
>
> LIttle snippet:
>
> PCOS needs to be taken seriously.
>
> The good news is that there is A LOT you can do to improve your fertility, regulate your menstrual cycle and manage your PCOS symptomsâ€¦ and it starts with what you put on your plate. The Paleo diet is the perfect template for improving PCOS.
>
>
>
>
>
> Diagnosis
>
> To be diagnosed with PCOS, you need to meet at least 2 of these 3 criteria: 1) irregular menstrual cycle, and/or; 2) high testosterone levels or symptoms of high testosterone levels, such as hirsutism or acne, and/or; 3) polycystic ovaries as seen on a vaginal ultrasound. (2)
>
>
>
> The name polycystic ovarian syndrome implies that cysts on the ovaries are the underlying cause, but not all women with PCOS have polycystic ovaries. And some women have polycystic ovaries without having PCOS or any fertility problems. Actually, the name PCOS is not very accurate and should probably be changedâ€¦
>
>
>
> Why? - Insulin Resistance and PCOS
>
> Not all women with PCOS have the symptoms and not all cases of PCOS have the same root cause. However, for a majority of women with PCOS, not only those struggling with their weight but lean women as well, insulin resistance is to blame. Insulin resistance is a form of prediabetes and can disrupt your hormone balance.
>
>
>
> Insulin is a very important hormone required to keep your blood sugar levels in check. Your pancreas produces insulin in response to carbohydrates to prevent your blood sugar levels from rising too high and allow the extra sugar derived from carbohydrates to be taken from your blood into your cells to be stored as fat. Without enough insulin, your blood sugar levels would go sky high, as it does in untreated type 1 diabetics.
>
>
>
> The problem with women with PCOS dealing with insulin resistance is that their body becomes resistant to the action of insulin. The cells are supposed to â€œlistenâ€￾ to insulin when it says to do something about your blood sugar levels. With insulin resistance, it is like if your cells had become deaf and canâ€™t hear insulin anymore. To try to make itself heard, the pancreas cranks up the music by producing more and more insulin. The hyperinsulinemia, or high insulin levels, associated with insulin resistance can affect your whole body, including your ovaries by triggering the production of more testosterone which affects your whole hormonal balance and lead to PCOS.
>
>
>
> What Can You Do About Your PCOS?
>
> For most women with PCOS for which high insulin levels and insulin resistance are behind their hormonal imbalances, improving your health, PCOS symptoms and fertility start by managing your blood sugar levels. Taking care of your blood sugar levels will automatically help your insulin levels, insulin resistance and ultimately restore your natural hormonal balance.
>
>
>
> It is harder for most women with PCOS to achieve and maintain a healthier body weight
>
> both because of the high insulin levels
>
> and because of the strong cravings
>
> caused by the hormonal imbalances.
>
>
>
> The Paleo diet can help you control your weight
>
> and manage your PCOS symptoms.

This is really good information and so true that I feel best on the Paleo diet. I have deviated since my consult with Dr. K and the bloating, fatigue, irritability, acne, etc. is SO not worth it.

My question is...if gaining weight will supposedly help me get TOM back, I'm curious how that's going to work. I'm skeptical, as I wasn't cycling at 150 or 140 anymore than I was at 109. I'm not willing to get that heavy again. I don't think that's healthy for me either.

I have been wanting to get it! Let me know what you think. I put sweet potatoes back in the diet. Japenese are the best. And low and behold, I've had two in the fridge for days and haven't gotten around to eating them. I'm not bingeing on things like I used to. ### Souldanzer — 2012-07-02T22:29:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35110>

> **PaleoCowgirl;35226 wrote:** This is really good information and so true that I feel best on the Paleo diet. I have deviated since my consult with Dr. K and the bloating, fatigue, irritability, acne, etc. is SO not worth it.
>
>
>
> My question is...if gaining weight will supposedly help me get TOM back, I'm curious how that's going to work. I'm skeptical, as I wasn't cycling at 150 or 140 anymore than I was at 109. I'm not willing to get that heavy again. I don't think that's healthy for me either.

B/c you can have HPA amenorrhea at any weight if your cortisol is out of whack. It's just that if you're losing weight unhealthily or keeping it artificially low, your cortisol is definitely out of whack.

Stop focusing on the weight and start focusing on the real issue.... which you will find out via the testing you are doing. There's AN's that never lose their period even at ridiculously low weights, and there's AN's that lose a pound from their goal weight and their cycle goes bye bye. All unique to your body. All unique to their particular stress/inflammation response.

Were you coming from above or below when you weren't cycling at 140/150?

I've always been in weight loss mode, ever since I can remember, but I was definitely losing when I wasn't cycling at those weights. But, I was also on the pill, and my doc said to expect my periods to just go away. Hopefully, my body is adjusting and figuring things out and I can stay at a stable spot where it doesn't feel stressed out.

Very true. I'm sure I went into hcg a mess, so doing the diet only created a larger mess! Yikes! I've been rough on my body.

I love the book so far. It explains your body's response to glucose, fructose, etc. and what happens over time. I haven't gotten into the lab hacking part yet, but I'm really interested in learning from that section. The recipes look great, too. I'm excited to try the sweet potato cole slaw and the grilled sweet potato with lime and cilantro dressing. Yum!

That's great you no longer have the urge to binge and that carbs in the house can go unnoticed. I had placed an order for coconut manna a while back, before I declared I shouldn't have it in the house, and it just arrived today. I just stuck it in the back of the cupboard for a day when I won't have such a hard time stopping after 1 spoonful.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-03T17:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35282>

I woke up this morning CONVINCED that it's going to be a great day. The inflammation from my week of poor eating choices is gone, the urge to binge is not around thanks to lots of fat with my BAB, I feel good in my skin, and luckily my sister who is a few sizes larger than me has really cute clothes for me to borrow. I have a few writing deadlines to make this morning, but then my sisters and I are going to town for a day of complete relaxation.

1\. Pedicures

2\. Massage (I'm getting a sea detox wrap)

3\. Magic Mike

When we get home, I plan to open up a good book, lay on my deck and get some Vitamin D the natural way.

I'm not sure where the optimism comes from. It's amazing how eating clean, respecting the light cycles and getting a good night's sleep can do for you.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-04T01:58:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35392>

My sister had basketball this afternoon, so we did get our massages/pedis in, but Magic Mike will have to wait until later this week. That's okay though -- we'll spread out the fun. We grilled an early supper and will shoot fireworks tonight. My family talked me in to going to the lake for at least tomorrow, so I'm packing a burger to grill and I'm taking this shrimp, avocado tomato salad (recipe here: [http://www.theblackpeppercorn.com/2012/05/shrimp-avocado-and-tomato-salad/](http://http://www.theblackpeppercorn.com/2012/05/shrimp-avocado-and-tomato-salad/)) as my contribution to the potluck. There are nightshades, which I usually avoid, but my HDL is 97, so other than not following AI protocol, I'm hoping it's okay.

I'm doing really, really well today. Weighing in at 125, which is a pretty big jump from 109...but it's a weight I think I will stick with for awhile and see what shakes loose. I've really upped the fat and moderated my protein intake, which seems to help with satiety a lot. I even have a couple spoonfuls of manna and had the urge to eat the whole thing, but I listened to my stomach, knew I was full and stopped. Major victory I think.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35416>

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-05T18:28:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35649>

I'm embarrassed to write this, but I spoke too soon on the coconut manna. It lead to a fruit binge, which lead to an ice cream binge. I stayed HOME yesterday from the lake, too, to avoid temptation, and what did I do? Get into my husband's stash! I wish he would go paleo, so I wouldn't have to buy this garbage anymore. I'm so bloated and gross. I start out so well, too...keto paleo, lots of fat, but then I grab a piece of fruit or a spoonful of manna for dessert, and it's all over.

I'm up a whole 20 lbs. from my lowest weight, and I didn't gain it the healthy way. Gosh, I have to be in a wedding in a month, and I'm a puffy, bloated whale.

I ate BAB this morning, even though I didn't want to. I don't really trust myself to eat any other meal but that. I made plans for a bbq with a friend, and I don't want to blow her off, but I really don't want them to come over here either. I'm so stressed, depressed and bloated, and not cycling. I'm so messed up. I'm just really, really frustrated. It seems whatever tight control I had over myself is completely broken, and now I have no control.

End of rant.

How do I fix me?

### PaulaRichards — 2012-07-05T18:38:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35655>

I can sense your frustration. I wish I had words of wisdom for you! It is awesome that you ate your BAB (even though you didn't want to!)! This is one of things that helped me. Even when I woke up the day after my all-out-ice cream binge I ate the BAB!!! This is going to come together for you. I can feel it. Get up. Dust off. BAB. Keep following as many rules as you can and it will get easier! \*hugs\*

### colleencoble — 2012-07-05T18:55:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35662>

First off realize that you are not a fat, bloated mess. You are at a more normal weight now. Try to look at yourself realistically. You're beautiful and you don't have to be a bag of bones to be beautiful. Hang in there, Mandie! praying for you today.

Most of us feel "messed up" on some level, we all have demons to fight. That said, it is always easier to give an advice and to console others.

This is a journey on which nothing will get fixed immediately. How I wish this were not true!! On this journey it is a step forward, one step back and sometimes two back! I pray for all of us that a bit by bit our journey is successful.

This is one of things that helped me. Even when I woke up the day after my all-out-ice cream binge I ate the BAB!!! This is going to come together for you. I can feel it. Get up. Dust off. BAB. Keep following as many rules as you can and it will get easier! \*hugs\*
>
>
> A binge usually is followed by great restriction, so I was happy I got up and did a big BAB. Thanks for the encouraging words. I keep hoping everything will fall into place, but maybe things have to get worse before they get better.
>
>
>
>
> > **colleencoble;35825 wrote: First off realize that you are not a fat, bloated mess. You are at a more normal weight now. Try to look at yourself realistically. You're beautiful and you don't have to be a bag of bones to be beautiful. Hang in there, Mandie! praying for you today.
> >
> >
> > In my heart I know you're right. In my head, I'm wanting to bury myself in a hole and not come out. If I'm honest with myself, that's why I didn't go to the lake yesterday -- not that I was worried about temptations, but because I was embarrassed to be in a swimsuit. Earlier this week, I made plans to grill out and have a bonfire with a couple of our friends -- one who is rail thin, size 0 without trying, has a healthy, happy baby, and the world is her oyster -- and part of me wants to cancel because I know I will compare myself to her and feel like a failure. I don't know where all this insecurity is coming from. I'm not going to cancel because it seems the last 6 months, that's all I do is bail out. But, it's going to be rough. And, I don't know why I've developed such social anxiety, but I have it really, really bad. It's more stress than it's worth, but it's pretty lonely having no friends, too.
> >
> >
> >
> >
> > > **Souldanzer;35827 wrote: Hon, get off the scale.... I know it's hard but you gotta do it. Fix the eating not the weight. Fixing the eating means fixing the inflammation/cortisol.
> > >
> > >
> > > You're right. I need to stay away from the scale. It just feels like I'm letting myself go....
> > >
> > > Destiny;35828 wrote:** Most of us feel "messed up" on some level, we all have demons to fight. That said, it is always easier to give an advice and to console others.
> > >
> > >
> > >
> > > This is a journey on which nothing will get fixed immediately. How I wish this were not true!! On this journey it is a step forward, one step back and sometimes two back! I pray for all of us that a bit by bit our journey is successful.
> >
> >
> >
> > So true! Right now it feels like one step forward, a mile back. Thanks for the words of encouragement.
> >
> >
> >
> > Lyndra;35844 wrote:** All true. I find so much inspiration from reading what you all are going through and relating in my own way. Nobody ever said this would be easy, or if they did, they were wrong. My only goal these days: become a bit more patient with myself and realize that the body heals in its own time.
>
>
>
> I really do need to remind myself that it took awhile for my body to get in this mess; I can't expect it to bounce right back. I pray for more patience....
>
>
>
> Jennifer;35848 wrote:** sweetie, control is the issue. you gotta let go and let god. you have no control. and it's OK. the harder you push that beach ball under the water, the more force it pushes back on you. just let go and buy spanx...that's what i'm doing! LOL and please have fun

Spanx, hot weather and extra rolls does not equal fun in my book. I wish I was gaining weight in the winter, where I had more layers to cover things up, and I wasn't sweating my butt off all the time. So uncomfortable. My dress for this wedding I'm in in a month is a size 6, and was way too big...but I haven't had it altered yet. I actually don't know what to do because how do I know what size I will be in a month? I guess I better just wait until the last minute to do alterations and see where it goes...

> **Souldanzer;35850 wrote:** Boulder lives in spandex. Why did they ever make clothes that do not stretch? Like we're the same every single day. Like we don't got hormones.

Is it all really cute little people wearing spandex, showing off their rock hard bods, or what? Maybe I need to go shopping for new clothes that fit. Maybe it will be the confidence booster I need. (I think I own a wardrobe in every size!)

I'm feeling a little better than I was this morning. I'm cleaning like mad...dusting, vacuuming scrubbing, and drinking water like crazy while spot CT'ing. I need to buck it up and know my friend will love me no matter what my size, my husband thinks I look way better now than before, and no matter how hard I try, it's not healthy for me to be the same size as my friends. I've always, always been the fat girl who made up for it by being smart and driven. I was never the fun girl, or the pretty girl, or the popular girl... In fact, my friend just blogged about 6 of us girls who kind of grew up together, and I got the description of practical, sensible. How fun is that? Well, there I go feeling sorry for myself again. Chin up. There's only one place to go from here, and that's up.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-05T21:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35703>

Question, I have my lab orders for a blood draw for ft3, antibodies and rt3, plus my zrt labs came to test for hormones and cortisol. I don't want to mess them up, and I haven't gone yet because I haven't been eating clean and don't want that to skew my results. I'm thinking of going in tomorrow. I know to fast before the blood draw. Anything I need to keep in mind for the saliva test?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-06T01:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35732>

> **PaleoCowgirl;35863 wrote:**
>
>
>
>
>
> You're right. I need to stay away from the scale. It just feels like I'm letting myself go....

You don't need to get off the scale... you need to pick it up and throw it out the window. Let the cows trample it. Drive a tractor over it. Put it in an outdoor bath to rust and cool the water down. Hang it from a tree and use it for target practice (you could throw zucchini at it). Dig a hole and bury it and let the future bone collectors wonder how humans in 2000 weighed themselves while underground. Get a sledgehammer and whack it (sounds like LHT to me). Just get it OUT of your house, out of your head, out of your life. K?

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-06T02:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35738>

> **PaleoCowgirl;35863 wrote:** I was never the fun girl, or the pretty girl, or the popular girl... .

Ok, news flash - YOU ARE THE PRETTY GIRL NOW. Adorable! Believe me, no amount of thin or cool or fun will ever be enough to make up for yesterday. If you want to be different, then be different. Ten years from now, you'll think, geez why didn't I just let it all go and enjoy myself?

We have so much in common, only I'm a lot further out than you. I always wanted to be skinny and popular and cool. But you know what's not cool, staying home because we feel uncomfortable in our own skin.

You follow me on pintrest and I saw your boards and thought you have such a unique style and you know what you like and you're fun and bubbly and you have really good taste. Change your mind about yourself, and watch the world reflect it back to you.

I'm glad you are just going to keep going out. I'm gonna keep wearing a bikini even though my belly is pretty bulgy. I gotta go on vacation with my YOUNGER brothers and their skinny wives and babies. But dang it. I'm going and I'll have fun. When I think about the past, I think I was always so caught up in worrying about what other people were thinking that I never was myself and I never had fun. The true you will come out and shine if you drop the rest. Ok, wow...I need to get off the soapbox already. I think I'm really talking to myself over here. I needed a pep talk...LOL

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-06T17:08:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35847>

Ouch, I stressed my body out last night...we went to the late movie, which totally messed me up on bedtime, and I hadn't eaten since that morning -- stupid. I was so hungry that I came home and ate 2 eggs at midnight. I ended up tossing and turning all night long. Felt a little nauseous eating BAB this morning -- I had steak tips and one egg (I'm really craving eggs lately, and I know it's not AI protocol, but it's good protein -- maybe I need the folic acid or something).

In my restricted eating yesterday, I realized I need to feed myself, but it doesn't have to be crap. I don't have to become a blog, and I suspect I won't, eating keto paleo, but I've been on such a self-inflicted binge lately I haven't given it a chance.

Here's to some perspective, a little reality check, a goal of getting my period back and staying off the stupid scale.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-07T01:21:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35980>

I am not fat. I am not ugly. I am getting stronger and healthier each and every day. I am beautiful. I am whole.

Repeat.

### nicebeanst@yahoo.com — 2012-07-07T01:37:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35985>

If that is you in your avatar you sure as \*\*\*\* aren't fat or ugly. Stop that!

No really, don't say "I am **not** XYZ" you're just going to register the XYZ part of it. Make it positive, as in "I am beautiful. I am powerful." which, of course you are. As well as happy, healthy, and perfect JUST AS YOU ARE. Just keep saying it. I have been saying I love and approve of myself over and over for a month now and I am starting to believe it. Silly big brain of ours!

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T01:45:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-35987>

Mandie, is this you now? You are still extremely thin. I would say too thin even.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-07T02:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36002>

The more feedback you guys are giving whether or not she is thin or too thin or normal or whatever, the harder it is for PCG to let go of that source of self-worth. You are feeding her ED.

JMHO.

PCG: I asked everybody to stop commenting on my body while I was recovering. I wanted to find myself and develop my own body image and others had no say in it any longer.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T02:40:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36009>

Here's what I know from experience. When you've lost a lot of weight like she has (and I have so I'm speaking from experience) you don't see yourself clearly. You still see the fat girl you were. So it's extremely helpful to realize that our thinking is skewed. That's one reason I always tell the gals to take pictures of themselves so they can actually SEE on a photo that they aren't the fat girl they see in the mirror. There is something about a photo. . .

I'm constantly asking my husband, am I about the size of that girl? Because I don't see myself clearly even after 2 years at my new weight. It takes time and some help from others. Her worth shouldn't be in her size, but it's helpful to realize she's not the size she thinks she is either. Then we self talk ourselves into trying to see things more rationally.

### Lyndra — 2012-07-07T02:50:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36010>

> **colleencoble;36174 wrote:**
>
> I'm constantly asking my husband, am I about the size of that girl? Because I don't see myself clearly even after 2 years at my new weight. It takes time and some help from others. Her worth shouldn't be in her size, but it's helpful to realize she's not the size she thinks she is either. Then we self talk ourselves into trying to see things more rationally.

My sis used to ask me the same question - "am I the size of that girl?" I didn't know how to answer. I wasn't perceptive enough to tell whether they were really about the same size. I never analyzed my sister's butt enough to know how it compared to someone else's. Maybe if they had been standing size-by-side, then I could have rendered an opinion, but that was never the case. I was also wary of making her feel bad; if she saw the other girl as bigger than she wanted to be, it would sour her mood. Thankfully, it sounds like your husband is far better at giving you useful feedback than I was for my sis.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-07T02:51:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36011>

Colleen, there is a point in recovery where you will deal with finding the correct body image and it is true that the brain lags behind in that.... I went through it. But for someone with an ED that comes after finding self-worth outside of body size. Size shouldn't matter at this point.

I don't need to get into it but what is happening here is eating disordered, and it is helping PCG none (even if she doesn't agree with it). You will not find one ED therapist who will ever comment on the body size of a patient. Rule #1 in ED treatment.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-07T02:53:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36013>
