I wasn't perceptive enough to tell whether they were really about the same size. I never analyzed my sister's butt enough to know how it compared to someone else's. Maybe if they had been standing size-by-side, then I could have rendered an opinion, but that was never the case. I was also wary of making her feel bad; if she saw the other girl as bigger than she wanted to be, it would sour her mood. Thankfully, it sounds like your husband is far better at giving you useful feedback than I was for my sis.

nice description of the uncomfortable place this kind of thing puts the bystander in.... and not everybody even looks at some other woman's butt to determine if it's smaller or bigger.... more interesting things in life.

### colleencoble — 2012-07-07T03:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36015>

yes my husband is good at reassurance. LOL

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-07T17:21:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36090>

body image is coded for in a different part of the brain than actually looking at a pic of oneself......so there is a biologic reason what colleen is reporting actually works........Its two separate brain regions.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-07T18:20:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36099>

Wow, I didn't mean to start a huge debate. The picture I posted is 20 lbs. heavier than my lowest when I realized I have a problem and 10 lbs. heavier than my avatar, so I think that's progress and don't think I need to gain anymore. Is it enjoyable gaining that much weight after working so hard to lose it? Absolutely not. Do I want to have a baby one day? You bet, so if that's what I've got to do, then that's what I'm going to do. I'm just trying to love myself along the way instead of hating what I'm becoming.

I've mustered up the courage to spend the rest of the weekend at my family's cabin. That means I'm rocking my bikini even if I feel like I shouldn't, and I'm not go to give in to temptations of bad food or worry about what other people think about my body. I'm going to enjoy the sun, relax with a good book, drink my lemon water (my new favorite) and just be.

### Sueville — 2012-07-07T18:30:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36102>

Good Attitude PCG. Enjoy your time making memories with your family. Time is a precious commodity and shouldn't be wasted wallowing in negative emotions. (Spoken from someone who wished she'd learned this lesson in her 20's or 30's instead of her 60's. I missed out on a lot of life because of poor body image). So grab life by the horns and live it to the fullest.

### Owl — 2012-07-07T19:10:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36111>

Cowgirl. good for you!

And go buy some clothes that fit - you don't want to be always uncomfortable in clothes that are too small.

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-07T19:50:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36117>

I agree with Owl, go shopping girl! You'll feel much better.

You unfortunatly can't decide if you need to gain more...Your body knows better. You look awesome on the pic, and IMO, you would look even better with more curves!! You might gain some more, your body will adjust, and once your hormones are balanced, you might lose part of what you gained. And maybe not.

It took me a while to stop focussing on my weight. I'm not happy with it, but obviously for now I have to live with it, so better accept it....When I was caught in the buliemia trap, I was the first one to judge people according to their weight. And now I'm the fat one. Ouch!! I learned to be kind to myself. There is some day that I just hate my body and all those love handles. But I come back to more positive thoughts and remind myself that I'm still beautiful (men stop me in the street to tell me I look good! Never happened in my skinny suit when anyway I had no self-confidence) and that my goal is to sleep and be healthy, what ever the scale says.

I know you want it all right now. It will come, but the more you'll try to control everything, the less it will happen. But you already know that

Have a good week-end, have fun and enjoy time with your familly and friends, that's what really matter in life

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-08T04:00:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36166>

Yay! Vacation w family and bikini!! Good for u. I likey

### nuttmegs17 — 2012-07-08T19:43:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36208>

I've been out of the loop due to travel (conferences) so am catching up...hugs to you PCG! you are on the right track

> **PaleoCowgirl;32534 wrote: Yeah, I really need to dump my scale and my relationship with it.
> yes for sure! if anything weigh yourself less. For instance, i was glutened twice (bacon was on a bed of toast but they told me it was "gluten free" sigh) last week due to conference food - def blew up due to inflammation for the following week (finally going down now) i have avoided the scale bc i know it will reflect a high number but its not "real" weight. its water/inflammation/bloating. no sense in getting on it and upsetting myself.
>
>
>
>
> > **Souldanzer;32596 wrote:** Think long and hard about when the right time is to have a baby. Or you might be doubling up on ED problems (and others) for the rest of your life and your child's life. If you have a child while you're still ED, you will set up your child to have the same problem.
> >
> >
> >
> > This one is emotionally charged for me as my mom was AN right before she got pregnant with me. Same situation as yours... desperately wanted to get pregnant w/ HPA amenorrhea. If I was given the choice, I would have happily waited a few more years to join this world set up for success instead of the disaster that I have been so far. I keep wanting to say "it's not fair" and I know that won't change anything... what doesn't kill me makes me stronger.... but it's not a lot of fun, speaking as the future baby you might be having.
>
>
>
> i agree that you want to be emotionally ready to have a child, but am not 100% sold you will have any effect on whether your future kid will have an ED. my mother has a great relationship with food, i never once overheard negative comments about her body or saw her dieting...in fact my family LOVES food....a bad experience with handsy soccer coach is what spiraled me down into ED. My husbands sister never had an issue with food and yet their mom was ALWAYS (and still is!) on a diet...always....she talks horribly about her body and her struggle with food all the time. However, my Sister in law - besides just showing a lack of interest in food in general - never went through what I went through (we grew up together so i've had 15 plus years to observe). I dont think one is ever 100% "ready" to have a child and am trying to avoid having to have a "perfect" life before taking the plunge myself (I get caught up in "i need to have more savings, need my health to be 100%, need to have my debt lowered" etc). If you wait around for perfection, you'll never be ready to have a child.
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
>
> PaleoCowgirl;33591 wrote:**
>
>
>
> I know what you mean though about super skinny women. Two of my closest friends are size 0, and I was always the big, corn-fed, country girl around them, and FINALLY, I was the same size as them! It felt great. It's frustrating that they can be "healthy," or then again, maybe they aren't, and I have to gain weight and be larger again to be at my optimal... this is just me feeling sorry for myself though. I need to get over it!

i feel you on this one. i live in a big city and the women here are SUPER tiny. I used to think they were just genetically lucky (and no doubt some - a very small percentage - are) but after spending a ton of time with them i've realized what the secret is: they just dont eat. its really sad.

my boss works out (heated power yoga - hours and hours a day) and is vegetarian (i know! - however, i suspect its more ED related than compassion related....its just a way to restrict right?) and after spending morning noon and night with her, she prob takes in 500 calories. literally. dinner was 3 bites of a salad (while i dug into a steak...yum!) i was shocked (but even more shocked when I realized eating dinner is not usually part of her diet). I'm coming to find out that is pretty typical of these super svelte ladies. even the ones that appear to eat normally when i have dinner with them, i usually find out that they did not eat anything at all the entire day. honestly, i've been shocked. however, realizing this is making it easier to not compare myself to them bc that is not normal. society's image of what "women" should look like is sick...the projected ideal is the body of a 12 year old GUY. sick and twisted. for anyone who grew up watching 90210, i was really taken aback by the reboot: the girls on it are anorexic looking. it's amazing how the "ideal" has changed in such a short time.

by best friends are pretty tiny. one is naturally so. she doesnt eat a ton but she is very petite. the other works at it. she works out, and she basically has a cup of yogurt for breakfast, a salad with cucumber and tomato and fat free dressing for lunch and a super tiny supper - she eats like this so she can go all out on the weekend. if you find yourself comparing yourself to other tiny women, remember that they are not as pulled together as you think. MOST women have some sort of hang up with their body, food, etc. and most women are working hard to be "ideal". i am quiet certain many of the tiny women you see walk around in a constant state of denial - denying their hunger signals, denying themselves of pleasure and in general not being kind to themselves....in the case of big city women, most are heavily medicated as well (adderall, uppers, downers, etc)

i constantly have to watch myself around these women bc its easy to fall into their mindset. i am actually starting to look forward to moving to a smaller town where the pressures to look a certain way may be a little less. i am so sick of hearing my co-worker eat a piece of dark chocolate and then obsess about it and schedule an emergency spin session so she can work it off....ugh! NOT HEALTHY

i struggle with body image issues and not being able to see myself the way others see me as well. i'm a work in progress. my hormones are still being optimized so i'm trying to go with the flow as much as possible. its amazing how much weight inflammation/bloating can cause. crazy. i'm still figuring out what works best for my diet. i generally eat low carb but eating sushi the other night with rice left me feeling amazing same thing with chocolate (sometimes i think chocolate is the one thing i've been able to eat throughout all of this that has never made me feel worse after consuming, hehe), same with french fries (i know they aren't primal, but cooked in duck fat they should be ok ....i dont make it a habit but they tend to leave me feeling good and not bloated) i go back and forth with carbs but sometimes worry not getting enough is stressing out my adrenals/thyroid. esp as i ride my bike to work almost daily, but i digress

> **colleencoble;33917 wrote: 300 mcg of T3? Holy cow. I've never seen anyone take that much. Did someone tell you to take that much? The highest I ever got was 150 which is pretty common with thyroid resistance.**

**

yes that seems high! i am only on 10, but i also take a grain and a half armour so maybe that's why

**PaleoCowgirl;34196 wrote:**

"Because optimal is within your grasp....if you choose it."**

That's the key point here in my journey, isn't it? I could choose to stay stick thin and meet society's standards, or I could follow your advice and get to optimal and have a baby. Sometimes the right path isn't the easiest path, and I have a lot to learn along the way. Your post really means a lot to me, as I've been grappling with my self-worth as I feel somewhat like a failure...I failed my body. I failed to lose weight AND stay healthy. I failed my future babies. Basically, I've been having a little pity party with myself, but I have been eating, not restricting and not binging, just eating! And, it feels right. For the first time, even though my jeans are tight, my diet feels right.

I know I need to change my mindset first and foremost and know that I'm making positive changes with your RX. You fuel my fire, Dr. Jack Kruse. I'm making baby steps with my husband, and my doctor is willing to order the labs I request, so I'm on my way.

If you stop back, could you recommend which labs I should order? I'm thinking HGH, FSH, LH and a hormone and cortisol panel. Anything else? I will order from ZRT. This week, I'm getting blood drawn to test my ft3 and rt3 and for hashis. I will post in my journal my results.

> **PaleoCowgirl;35238 wrote:** I just got the book, "Sweet Potato Power," in the mail. Anybody else read it yet? What did you think?

great book! i am also reading "it starts with food" and "the perfect health diet" they are not really telling me much that I haven't picked up either and they kinda contradict each other in terms of carbs, but i find reading these things helps reinforce healthy behaviors...reminds me why i eat the way I do

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-09T03:59:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36275>

> **AbetterME;36283 wrote:** I agree with Owl, go shopping girl! You'll feel much better.
>
>
>
> You unfortunatly can't decide if you need to gain more...Your body knows better. You look awesome on the pic, and IMO, you would look even better with more curves!! You might gain some more, your body will adjust, and once your hormones are balanced, you might lose part of what you gained. And maybe not.
>
>
>
> It took me a while to stop focussing on my weight. I'm not happy with it, but obviously for now I have to live with it, so better accept it....When I was caught in the buliemia trap, I was the first one to judge people according to their weight. And now I'm the fat one. Ouch!! I learned to be kind to myself. There is some day that I just hate my body and all those love handles. But I come back to more positive thoughts and remind myself that I'm still beautiful (men stop me in the street to tell me I look good! Never happened in my skinny suit when anyway I had no self-confidence) and that my goal is to sleep and be healthy, what ever the scale says.
>
>
>
> I know you want it all right now. It will come, but the more you'll try to control everything, the less it will happen. But you already know that
>
>
>
> Have a good week-end, have fun and enjoy time with your familly and friends, that's what really matter in life

Yeah, it sucks going back to being the fat girl...I hate it. But, I suppose things have to get worse before they get better.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-09T05:39:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36287>

> **colleencoble;36445 wrote:** Quit saying you're the fat girl! I saw your picture. You look gorgeous! You need to speak reinforcements to the truth. Say I am beautiful inside and out. Eventually you'll believe what we all see.

It's like trying to rationalize with a drunk alcoholic. Quit reinforcing her ED (which is talking here) and she will stop making these statements. What you pay attention to grows. Help her take the attention \*off\* her body weight - no matter what it is!

Every time you react to a statement like that you are encouraging her (ED) to do it again. Draw the attention to something desirable instead.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-09T05:43:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36289>

adventures are good... seek one out? You might discover something new.... about yourself. Clothing is a very safe way to try something new. I went down this path while discovering the real me  It was fun!

### chocolate — 2012-07-09T19:51:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36351>

Paleo, I do a little here and there and know you have to wear form fitting for safety's sake. At least you could get a couple pair of jeans that feel like home. Its all about the pockets for me. That's where my style lay... my fon and my money need to be easy to get to and not fall out. You could just get some coveralls for when you go outside. I have some state prison neon orange ones. I didn't know that til I went to the bank in them. It makes you very popular with the criminals. They don't care how fat you are.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-11T00:15:43+0300

> Thanks, Grammy. I think I hit rock bottom on Sunday while we were tinning the barn roof. (A bad place to be when you're in a deep depression...) There is really only one place to go from here, and that's up. If anything, I think going through this is making me closer to my mom. I shared with her that I'm scared I will never get pregnant, and she has been sharing stories about her own struggles to get pregnant and how she understands how depressing it can be to feel like it will never happen. Obviously, I'm not actively trying to make this happen for me now, but it's lingering in the back of my mind that it may never happen for me.
>

>

>

I can't always avoid mirrors and what I look like. I really don't care if other people comment about my looks or not. But, I do have to come to terms with my size, especially now that it's a bigger size. I don't know how to love myself with my looks, and I know I have to find worth in other ways...but I still need to find a way to be comfortable and confident in my own skin...or I'll never leave the house.

Haha! I have plenty of "fat" jeans from my former big days, so I have plenty of chore clothes that fit to wear outside. It's going out in public that's a different story. I'll get there though.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-11T00:21:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36644>

No updates here. I keep eating bad (gluten and sugar), so I have been putting off my blood draws. I've been eating clean now for a few days, so I think I'm going to go in tomorrow. I also have a saliva test for hormones and adrenals, plus a blood spot test for LH and FSH to do. I really need to get on it, so I can take the results to my doc appt. next week.

I really hit rock bottom this weekend. I just didn't want to try anymore. I wanted to give up. I felt sorry for myself, hating the hand God had dealt me. I couldn't understand why other people have it so easy. Why they can eat what they want and not feel/look bad after. I keep reminding myself though that feeling sorry for myself is only reinforcing the depression, the bad eating, the who cares? attitude...and it needs to stop.

I have serious goals that need addressing:

1\. Sleep all the way through the night.

2\. Get rid of the inflammation from my poor eating choices.

3\. Get my period back.

4\. Have babies one day.

5\. Become LS.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-11T01:01:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-36663>

I like one of Dr K's mottos (also one of Carolyn Myss's btw)... embrace paradox. When you're feeling sorry for yourself, get grateful. One of my favourite quotes is from Brother David Steindl Rast who says "In daily life we must see that it is not happiness that makes us grateful, but gratefulness that makes us happy."

He says it far better than I can (and I watch this regularly)....

[http://www.gratefulness.org/brotherdavid/a-good-day.htm](http://http://www.gratefulness.org/brotherdavid/a-good-day.htm)

Mandy, I just took myself shopping and was texting pics to tennie leek..lol.. I need a shopping bud. But I was making myself nuts because I tried on a Lauren Conrad dress size 14! And it fit! I woke up this morning with a temp of 101.4...yikes! I have a sore throat and a headache but not much else. Could my immune system be weak from eating crap foods in my recent binges? Could I finally be utilizing the t3 I'm taking, instead of it getting dumped into rt3 as Dr. K suspects? I didn't take my t3 this morning because I didn't want to elevate my temp further.

I would. When you are healthy and have a good T3 level and get a temp, you can't lower your t3 because you body makes it.

I would keep it steady.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-12T15:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37124>

How ya feeling today lovey?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-12T19:58:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37214>

> **Jennifer;37291 wrote:** How ya feeling today lovey?

Much better. I was in bed by 9:30 pm and slept until 9:30 am this morning....my husband said I was so sweaty I soaked the sheets last night, but the fever has broke, thank goodness. If that wasn't a wake-up call to eat better and take care of myself, I don't know what is!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-12T20:08:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37217>

Day 2 Binge Free (wow, that sounds sad, but you gotta start somewhere!)

I finally went to town and did my blood draw this morning. Working from home and living 15 miles out of town, I rarely make trips to town. I got out of my basketball shorts and t-shirt and put on real clothes to get my thyroid lab done. I'm going to wait until next week to do my saliva and blood spot test. Tomorrow, I have a speaking engagement in town for work, Saturday I have to go to a meeting with my husband and Sunday we are going to the lake, so they aren't typical days to test my cortisol levels. I will probably wait until Tuesday, so I'm good and recovered from all my running around this weekend.

I tried on my bridesmaid dress for Aug. 4, and a couple of months ago, it was atleast 3 inches too big...now I can barely get it zipped. Talk about depressing, but I know I'm inflamed my from gluten binges recently, so if I eat clean (yet eat enough), I'm sure the damage will go away, and I will find a size that doesn't make me feel like a balloon.

While I was in town, I stopped at the spa and did a $25 Derma Steam. It's not CT, but it really helps take away the bloating and extra water weight that I've been carrying from my binges. It's pretty affordable and super relaxing.

My sister owns a clothing company, and she is having a photo shoot (complete with hair and makeup lady) tomorrow at my house. She has about 5 of us models to wear her clothes, and a professional photographer to capture all the outfits. It should be fun; I'm going to embrace my new size and try to feel confident in my skin.

I'm cleaning house for the rest of the day and then helping my husband tin the barn roof later tonight.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-12T21:30:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37244>

That sounds so cool! All of it ! The treatment and photoshoot. Igloos to see pics! And great attitude

### MartiD — 2012-07-13T00:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37309>

Mandy, sounds like you are headed in the right direction! Be sure and post your photo shoot pico on FB...I love seeing your sisters clothing line! I know you will look great!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-13T02:15:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37335>

Tut tut tut! That's not sad! That's the beginning of something awesome. That's the first steps leading you to a better and healthier you. Every success is made of baby steps. Don't forget that.

Hug,

Katy

Katy, this is the kind of stuff I wish my mother had said to me. SO nice to hear it (and know you mean it!). Lauren

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-14T07:08:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37704>

I am so broken.... I'm screaming at the top of my lungs for help and nobody sees I'm drowning. Binging is out of control. I'm out of control. I'm in a game I can't win.

What do I do? Where do I turn?

I'm lost.

### Sem — 2012-07-14T08:39:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37709>

Hi. I can completely relate. I have definitely been there. You sound in real despair. Is there someone you can turn to for help? Your mom? Sister? Close friend? You sound very depressed and despairing. It's a scary place to be. When I have felt like that, I want to isolate myself but you can't do it. I had a very bad depression in my late 20s. I ended up on meds. I wouldn't recommend going down that road because it has it's own set of issues. But try looking into some other things to help balance your mood. Have you read the book The Mood Cure? Keep the connection with the people who love you. Sending bug hugs.

### spinnsugar@hotmail.com — 2012-07-14T15:38:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37724>

I am so sorry to hear this, Big Hugs for you! I know how it feels and wish I could help you. It does get better. I hope you find your path soon.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-14T16:15:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37735>

> **PaleoCowgirl;37871 wrote:** I am so broken.... I'm screaming at the top of my lungs for help and nobody sees I'm drowning. Binging is out of control. I'm out of control. I'm in a game I can't win.
>
>
>
> What do I do? Where do I turn?
>
>
>
> I'm lost.

Why do you feel so broken? You are not broke yet, just not down the right path just yet. If you were broken the path would be easy. So you are binging, pick yourself up and try again. Shoot for 1 meal at a time if that's what it takes. I realize I don't understand ED but if you want to fix yourself, then you have to keep on trying. One day it will click. I still struggle every day even after a year. They have a candy dish at work with things I still enjoy but I have to walk past it and tell myself I will not put that crap in my body. I deserve better. It's kinda funny that the tongue (ok, maybe the brain) has so much control over what the body really needs. I look at that damn candy dish about 20 or more times a day and I know how good those stupid reeses peanut butter cups taste (yes, I love them still). Some say shoot for the moon, but that is not always possible. Just keep trying and keep adding better things to get you to optimal so even if you slip you are still making progress. Sounds like maybe you need to do something fun and maybe you are focusing too much on all of this. Smile and try again today!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-14T16:28:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37742>

Oh Mandy, I'm so sorry! What brought that binge? The photo shoot with sister and friends?

There's only one thing to do: start all over again. BAB and fat. And then more fat. Get down as much food as you can for breakfast. It's the only way you won't have cravings at night. If you're hungry between meals, eat more fat. After a few day you'll focus on not eating between meals, but right now you have to get rid of the cravings.

Please don't give up. You can continue screaming, but nobody will come and help you. People around you are their for you, but they can't do it for you. YOU have to do it. YOU have to be there for yourself, YOU have to believe in yourself and love yourself enough to have the desire to heal those body and mind. Take responsability for yourself. If you binge again, just forgive yourself and start over the next morning. It will get easier with time.

I know you'd prefer an easier and quicker fix. Especially when you feel that bad and so desperate. Yes, there's an end to all that.

Are you still focussing on your weight and how you look? Are you restricting yourself or feeling deprivated? Those are big stressor that easily lead to binge...Please, please, please, stop focussing on how you look. I know it's easier said than done, I know. People around you will still love you even if you gain another 20 lbs. They will still think you are lovely and beautiful. Why wouldn't you?

Now get some fat, take it easy on yourself today, don't stay by yourself, and try to enjoy the day. Hugs,

Katy

Sending the same thoughts and hugs to you, Paleocowgirl!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-14T22:02:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37820>

Thanks everyone for the support and kind words. I need to do some soul-searching and figure out what's important to me in life. Needless to say, the photo-shoot didn't go well for me, at all. It was not what I needed yesterday, but it was a wake-up call that can't be ignored. I'm ashamed at what I've become, and I pretty much had a melt-down with my husband last night. Today, he boxed up all the garbage food in the house -- his beloved ice cream, ranch dressing, tator tots, corn dogs, pop tarts -- and told me we were going to get healthy together, not lose weight, but be healthy. He told me he loved me no matter what size I am (I believe him, he met me when I weighed in the 170s!) but he realized I was unhappy and freaking out about not fitting in this bridesmaids dress for the wedding I'm in in 3 weeks. The last year, I've either been eating like a bird or gorging, and neither path is healthy. So, he took all the junk food over to my mom's -- yes, it's just taking the poison down the road -- but at least it's a sign of solidarity and support in a time where I feel very, very alone.

Hugs back to all of you who responded to my late-night episode. It really means a lot.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-14T22:09:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37824>

For your hubby to do this with you is huge. That is a big sacrifice on his part. He obviously loves you very much!

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-14T22:28:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-37832>

> **PaleoCowgirl;37987 wrote:** Thanks everyone for the support and kind words. I need to do some soul-searching and figure out what's important to me in life. Needless to say, the photo-shoot didn't go well for me, at all. It was not what I needed yesterday, but it was a wake-up call that can't be ignored. I'm ashamed at what I've become, and I pretty much had a melt-down with my husband last night. Today, he boxed up all the garbage food in the house -- his beloved ice cream, ranch dressing, tator tots, corn dogs, pop tarts -- and told me we were going to get healthy together, not lose weight, but be healthy. He told me he loved me no matter what size I am (I believe him, he met me when I weighed in the 170s!) but he realized I was unhappy and freaking out about not fitting in this bridesmaids dress for the wedding I'm in in 3 weeks. The last year, I've either been eating like a bird or gorging, and neither path is healthy. So, he took all the junk food over to my mom's -- yes, it's just taking the poison down the road -- but at least it's a sign of solidarity and support in a time where I feel very, very alone.
>
>
>
> Hugs back to all of you who responded to my late-night episode. It really means a lot.

Ok, Husband is on board. Put that in the "things I'm grateful for" category.

Next - do that soul searching. What ever you decide that is important - own it. It looks to me like choices are to opt for fitting into that dress at whatever size OR re-focusing on your overall health.

It seems that we can get over focused on the details and this rabbit hole - and perhaps some of it isn't positive. You know, even if you don't like you right now because what you R E A L L Y want is a short term win of that damn dress - decide to like that girl anyway. Then that is what it is. . .what would be a shame is for you to realize that is what you want and not learn/grow from it and proceed on fresh after the wedding.

Changing, real change on all the levels of change required is not linear. If you are not "failing" you are not progressing. Perhaps this is just going to be a fail. Perhaps you will restrict and exercise and do what you can do lose those pounds and fit in the dress. Just make sure along the way to be cognizant that you are choosing that right now - and figure out if there is anything along the way that you can learn or do to help you become something different than you are.

NOBODY is perfect, everyone has tons of skeletons in their closets. In the big picture of life - not being perfect on your optimal wants for three weeks isn't the end of the road and in reality nobody gives a \*\*\*\* anywhere close to the way you do.

A couple of things I had to realize before I could let the old me go. Number one is so important:

1\. Other people are not thinking of you. They are not in your head and they are not judging your reasons for/against something. Even if other people talk/comment about how you look or what not - it's conversation. conditioned conversation. They are sitting there in their own heads with their own inner dialog trying to figure out how they fit in the world and what is up next for THEM.

True connection to another person on an intimate mental level is rare, and it's not going on all the time. Sometimes the middle ground of comfortable by observing standards of behavior is good enough.

2\. Give to others to let the drama go. You could sit in the drive through munching down on yummies and beating yourself up the whole time - or you could embrace that choice. Go be nice to the clerk, go open doors for people, go smile and have a conversation with someone who needs it - enjoy the \*\*\*\* out of that damn yummie and get out of the cycle of beating you up for it. If you don't leave the farm - do something unexpected and nice for your husband. Neighbor. Dog - whatever. You learn to give to yourself by understanding the pleasures and joy of giving to others.

If you

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-15T15:49:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-38028>

Lots of stuff to really think about and digest. Funny there are a lot of metaphors for thinking related to food. Clue?

Any way.

The more you think about and try to control yourself , the worse it gets. My gut tells me your avoiding something and all this is a good distraction. Cavmam has good advice there. Time to clear out the BS voices in your head so you can hear the small voice inside of you that's trying to get your attention. I think it's great your hubby has stepped up. Your having a war of control with yourself. Maybe it's time to lean on him. Either way. I know how horrible it is to feel out of control. IMHO All you can do is hottie your knees and admit you can't do it alone. And fat and protein

Xoxo

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-15T15:49:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-38029>

And eat more seafood!!

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-16T11:43:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-38318>

> **PaleoCowgirl;37987 wrote:** Thanks everyone for the support and kind words. I need to do some soul-searching and figure out what's important to me in life. Needless to say, the photo-shoot didn't go well for me, at all. It was not what I needed yesterday, but it was a wake-up call that can't be ignored. I'm ashamed at what I've become, and I pretty much had a melt-down with my husband last night. Today, he boxed up all the garbage food in the house -- his beloved ice cream, ranch dressing, tator tots, corn dogs, pop tarts -- and told me we were going to get healthy together, not lose weight, but be healthy. He told me he loved me no matter what size I am (I believe him, he met me when I weighed in the 170s!) but he realized I was unhappy and freaking out about not fitting in this bridesmaids dress for the wedding I'm in in 3 weeks. The last year, I've either been eating like a bird or gorging, and neither path is healthy. So, he took all the junk food over to my mom's -- yes, it's just taking the poison down the road -- but at least it's a sign of solidarity and support in a time where I feel very, very alone.
>
>
>
> Hugs back to all of you who responded to my late-night episode. It really means a lot.

Great step to remove the trigger foods from your home. My now ex-husband had to live with these challenging circumstances for a while. I also gave up all credit cards and cash for a year or so and periodically thereafter so I could not go to the store and buy junk.

None of this eliminated the binging but it established the right environment for me to take other measures to stop it. I had to think before I acted.

I still occasionally binge these days and I choose to focus on the good days I had in between instead. There is many more healthy days now than bad days and the bad days are starting to fade. Cold and seafood was the most important thing for me to stop the binging along with Leptin rx. Binging takes a while to stop. Make sure you keep eating right and when you do binge, it's helpful to try to stick to AI foods as to not mess up your gut again.

You won't fix this overnight. An ED takes 10 years to recover from for most. Hopefully, Dr K's work might speed this up a bit.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-16T16:35:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-38350>

I showed him the sugar on the label. It's weird because my mom was never really accommodating with my lifestyle when it was just me, but now that she knows Tyler is on board, she called to ask what kinds of foods we could eat.

>
> 1\. Other people are not thinking of you. They are not in your head and they are not judging your reasons for/against something. Even if other people talk/comment about how you look or what not - it's conversation. conditioned conversation. They are sitting there in their own heads with their own inner dialog trying to figure out how they fit in the world and what is up next for THEM.
>
>
>
> True connection to another person on an intimate mental level is rare, and it's not going on all the time. Sometimes the middle ground of comfortable by observing standards of behavior is good enough.
>
>
>
> 2\. Give to others to let the drama go. You could sit in the drive through munching down on yummies and beating yourself up the whole time - or you could embrace that choice. Go be nice to the clerk, go open doors for people, go smile and have a conversation with someone who needs it - enjoy the \*\*\*\* out of that damn yummie and get out of the cycle of beating you up for it. If you don't leave the farm - do something unexpected and nice for your husband. Neighbor. Dog - whatever. You learn to give to yourself by understanding the pleasures and joy of giving to others.

Thanks! I love your two pieces of advice! So true! What started my binge last Friday before my sister's shoot was stress. I had a speaking engagement that day, plus all these people coming to my house for the shoot, and my sister was acting like a diva. I was also invited to help can string beans with the Hutterite women who live close to us, and they offered me an iced coffee with whipped cream on top. I haven't had a coffee beverage like that in 6 months, so I was in heaven. (In their culture, it's really really disrespectful to turn down their gifts. They sent me home with rolls, fresh jelly and some beef...which of course, I slathered some jelly on the rolls and it all went down hill from there.) By the time the photo shoot came around, my stomach was distended, and I was miserable. So, I put on some spanx and sucked it up, watching other girls put on dresses that used to fit me and no longer did. Of course, I'm incredibly inflamed from the gluten. I checked the scale and saw I dropped 10 lbs. of water weight over night. Once I saw the pics from the shoot though, I was immediately depressed. I looked so puffy and gross. Now, I'm admitting I need to gain weight, but not the way I have. I recommitted myself to the reset over the weekend, with my husband to back me up, and once this inflammation is gone, I have a feeling I will have a whole new healthy outlook on life and a chance to put on weight the right way. I have a feeling that the dress for my wedding will fit too once I take away all these inches of bloat.

> **Jennifer;38197 wrote: Lots of stuff to really think about and digest. Funny there are a lot of metaphors for thinking related to food. Clue?
>
> Any way.
>
> The more you think about and try to control yourself , the worse it gets. My gut tells me your avoiding something and all this is a good distraction. Cavmam has good advice there. Time to clear out the BS voices in your head so you can hear the small voice inside of you that's trying to get your attention. I think it's great your hubby has stepped up. Your having a war of control with yourself. Maybe it's time to lean on him. Either way. I know how horrible it is to feel out of control. IMHO All you can do is hottie your knees and admit you can't do it alone. And fat and protein
>
>
>
> Xoxo
>
>
> Yeah, I'm not sure what I'm avoiding, but I do know this ED has made me completely irrational about social situations with friends. I had a heart to heart with one of my girlfriends to tell her what I was going through, and having had an ED herself, she really understood and suggested that we get together for a movie or something instead of going to eat. I reached out to another friend too, and we plan to go to the rodeo this weekend. I WILL NOT BAIL!
>
>
>
>
>
> **Jennifer;38198 wrote:** And eat more seafood!!**

**

Fresh-caught crappie is on the menu tonight. I've also got seafood around, and I'm going to try to eat it at least every other day, even though I would MUCH prefer pork and beef.

Souldanzer;38489 wrote:** Great step to remove the trigger foods from your home. My now ex-husband had to live with these challenging circumstances for a while. I also gave up all credit cards and cash for a year or so and periodically thereafter so I could not go to the store and buy junk.

None of this eliminated the binging but it established the right environment for me to take other measures to stop it. I had to think before I acted.

I still occasionally binge these days and I choose to focus on the good days I had in between instead. There is many more healthy days now than bad days and the bad days are starting to fade. Cold and seafood was the most important thing for me to stop the binging along with Leptin rx. Binging takes a while to stop. Make sure you keep eating right and when you do binge, it's helpful to try to stick to AI foods as to not mess up your gut again.

You won't fix this overnight. An ED takes 10 years to recover from for most. Hopefully, Dr K's work might speed this up a bit.

Yesterday I ate more than I probably should have, but it was all okay foods -- coconut flakes (which I threw out to make sure I didn't overindulge on them again) but also pork loin and bacon. I wouldn't call it a binge, but I did eat past the hunger signal. The fact that I didn't go over to my mom's and plow through her junk food though is a step in the right direction.

I ended up having a great weekend with my husband -- laughing, watching movies, holding hands, working in the garden together, etc. It was so good to reconnect and know I have someone to support me through it all. Even if he doesn't get it completely, he knows I've been hurting, and is finally starting to come around and listen.

Today, I'm grateful for the return of my libido, my great husband, the sunshine and my positive attitude.

I'm praying for a shot of rain to help the crops and the hay fields, strength in my food decisions and to continue to feel good about myself.

I went to the doc today, and she wants to test me for diabetes. She also ordered a pelvic ultrasound to be done to confirm IF I actually have PCOS or not. If I do, she wants to put me on metformin for fertility and controlling my insulin levels. Anybody have experience with this?

Here are my latest labs. Hackers, can you take a look?

FEIBC Panel

Iron 53 (37-170 mcg/dL)

TIBC 401 (250-460 mcg/dL)

FE Sat% 13 LOW (15-40%)

TSH 0.00 LOW (0.49-4.67 uIU/mL)

FT4 0.60 LOW (0.7-1.85 ng/dL)

FT3 3.2 (2.4-4.2 pg/mL)

RT3 63 Low (90-350pg/mL)

Ratio .05079 (Doc says needs to be .2 or greater)

I was expecting my rt4 to be sky-high because I was taking 300 mcg of t3 with no ill effects, and dr. k indicated it was probably all going to rt3. Not sure what this all means.

Help?

4 Days binge free. This week is going great. I think I've got a handle on things...seafood (which tastes more like medicine than food to me) is really changing my thinking about the fuel I put in my mouth.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-20T17:06:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-39674>

I'm thinking more about my doctor's appt. yesterday and wanted to also mention that I told her about my consult with Dr. K and how he suspected HA, not PCOS. She said she was going to read up on both the HA and Dr. K...I think this will be really good, if she can check out his stuff and see where I'm coming from.

On another note, she said I'm at a much better weight now to get my period back and conceive. This is positive reinforcement that I'm on the right track, even though I'm super bummed at the way I put the weight on. My fertility friend chart predicts my period will come on Monday...I'm not holding my breath, but here's hoping.

Also, I've been lifting 5 lb. dumbbells whenever I walk by them (I leave them on the dining room table, so I see them often) Anyway, I'm trying to regain some of the muscle I lost, and my husband saw me doing it, and he sat down with me and showed me all kinds of lifts from his high school days. It's fun to be eating with him and now "working out" together. It's nice to have a teammate.

Not nosey at all! I've been off birth control pills since February. I know I probably should use backup, but I would be thrilled to get pregnant (yes, I have things to work on, but it could take decades to get to optimal...I'm not waiting that long). ### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-23T23:25:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-40470>

I went and did a DermaSteam treatment again today. I must be seriously detoxing things because my skin just smells today. Gross. I know the skin is the biggest organ, and I can definitely tell I'm detoxing some of the nasty frankenfoods I've eaten lately. I'm so glad I'm on the straight and narrow now. Even though I feel like I'm back to square one, eating keto-paleo with lots of seafood and lots of nourishing fats feels right.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-07-24T01:22:01+0300

There is never a perfect time, and if nature lets you get knocked up - go with it! I know it's not PC to say you make changes for other people - but nothing inspired me to change things more than my child. Yes, it would have been nice "to do it for me" but who cares! What's wrong with doing for others?

Anywhoo - eat epi paleo and let nature decide!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-24T17:28:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-40685>

Every time I want to make a bad eating decision, I now ask myself if my future baby will be better or worse because of that choice. For some reason, thinking about that child and not just me makes the choice a lot easier to make. I would always choose optimal for baby...not sure what it means that I'm willing to be less than optimal when it's just me.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-24T22:18:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-40817>

I wonder if it's my ED that caues me to be so emotionally insane or what it is. When I'm on a binge, even my family notices that I'm on a sugar high...lots of energy, lots of bubbling personality...then the crash comes. And, I'm in a deep depression, nothing matters, I don't want to go anywhere or do anything. I'm in that funk right now; my husband asked me today when I'm going to get my zeal for life again.

Then, when I'm firmly in ketosis, I have no emotions at all. Not happy, not sad, just there. Is that the way to live? Yet, as soon as I add carbs into my diet, I get cravings and want more, more, more. I'm to the point where I don't even trust a little vegetable like cucumbers. How lame.

What is the solution to this? I'm reading the Mood Cure right now, but I'm not keen on trying to fix my problems with tons of supplements. I've been supplementing like crazy the last year, and it seems like there has got to be a more natural solution...I know epi-paleo, but that still leaves me emotionally flat. Anyone else experience this?

> And the research he reported in his masterwork, “Nutrition and Physical Degeneration”, indicated that fish – especially fish eggs – rank very high among the whole foods associated with human health and fertility:
>
> “The eggs of the salmon are dried and stored as an important item of nutrition for both children and adults. They are also used to increase the fertility of the women. From a chemical standpoint they are one of the most nutritious foods I have found anywhere. (Price WA, p 70)
>
> “In many of the primitive tribes living by the sea we found emphasis on the value of fish eggs … for insuring a high physical development of growing children, particularly of girls, and a high perfection of offspring through a reinforcement of the mother’s nutrition.” (Price WA, p 264)

> **Jennifer;41724 wrote: How's things
>
>
> Things are going okay. Thanks for asking. I seem to be having a lot of highs and lows emotionally and physically. I have finally dumped the scale after saying I was going to do so a million times. I'm just sick of relating my self-worth to the scale. I'm in a wedding this weekend, so it's crunch time to fit into that dumb dress. I'm hoping I don't fall into AN tendencies this week in preparation for it, but on Friday I binged and purged again and haven't eaten since, so it's fair to say I'm in ED mode again, which certainly isn't going to help me get my cycle back, help me get pregnant, help me heal this issue I have...sigh.....I should really go eat a bucket of shrimp and get over it. Even though I'm having some trouble s though, I do feel better at a higher weight. I've accepted it and am starting to be okay when I look at myself in the mirror. I'm working on confidence issues, but I will get there.
>
>
>
> Souldanzer;41780 wrote:** I'm thinking busy writing affirmations, looking in the mirror and telling herself she is beautiful, making collages of her future life full of kids, and dreaming the perfect boyfriend..... oops, that was us  Cowgirl already got that one going for herself

Haha! I do enjoy reading about your boy adventures! I live vicariously through you single ladies. I know a lot of cowboys if you're interested in the ranch life....unlimited access to beef, lots of grassy pastures, no neighbors, Wrangler jeans... ;

Affirmations: This is exactly what I've been working on. Trying to find what's beautiful...what I like about myself, the great things about my body, the fact that I'm getting stronger...

I do a little photography on the side, and I took maternity pictures this morning of a couple. It's exciting for them, but there is still this fear in the back of my mind that it's not in the cards for me. Let's hope I'm wrong. I WILL have a healthy baby someday. I WILL get my period again. I WILL maintain a healthy weight that allows my body to be at optimal. REPEAT. REPEAT.

Yeah, I have no idea if that's indicative of hashis or not -- that's why I posted to see if I could garner some insights from all of you! I will definitely stay on AI protocol just in case. What really concerns me is the A1C levels of 5.1. It seems kind of high after eating only keto.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-31T22:19:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42106>

For some people A1c goes up after going very low carb for a while. Mine is at 5.4 and I do consider myself pre/diabetic. It appears though that with continued following of Kruse's program, my fasting BG has finally come down (last was 76! very proud of myself!!) this will eventually go away. It's called physiological insulin resistance.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-07-31T22:44:38+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42114>

any idea what your fasting blood glucose is, Mandy?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-31T23:02:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42124>

> **Jennifer;42366 wrote:** any idea what your fasting blood glucose is, Mandy?

When I test on my dad's glucose reader after fasting, I'm between 85-95. I am going to do a two-hour glucose test to see how insulin resistant I am. We will see what those results look like. I still have the saliva test for adrenals and fh and lsh, but I've been eating so poorly lately, I don't really think it's worth measuring right now. I'm going to wait until I've been working at optimal for awhile, with no mess-ups before I measure those.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-07-31T23:04:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42126>

> **Souldanzer;42357 wrote:** For some people A1c goes up after going very low carb for a while. Mine is at 5.4 and I do consider myself pre/diabetic. It appears though that with continued following of Kruse's program, my fasting BG has finally come down (last was 76! very proud of myself!!) this will eventually go away. It's called physiological insulin resistance.

This is very good to know. I think I should just consider myself pre-diabetic and avoid spiking my insulin whenever possible.

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-08-01T21:47:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42333>

Holy smokes! Talk about hormone imbalance. When my thyroid was not functioning, I would get so angry and upset at the little things. I would throw a temper tantrum and cry. I would get so mad I could hardly see straight. Thankfully, I haven't had that in a really, really long time...until today. We were outside working with cattle, and my sister let a calf back that she wasn't supposed to. I immediately got so mad I was seeing stars, and I started bawling and had to leave. Of course, I realize it's ridiculous behavior, but jeeze, where did that come from? I think this happens when I don't have enough fat in my diet. Better increase it. That and fish. Blech, wish I liked it as much as I like my beef, pork and lamb.

As I sip some ice tea and try to relax (and get my work done today), I thought I would write today's goals:

1\. Laugh. Seriously. I need to lighten up. I hardly smile or joke around or enjoy myself anymore. I have put in an old video -- Blue Collar Comedy Tour. Maybe that will make me chuckle. Anybody have any secrets to laughing? Any good movies that just crack you up?

2\. Do yoga. I want to be flexible, strong and lean again. I don't want to be pudgy. I want to be at a healthy weight, but I also want muscles. I've got Jillian Michael's yoga DVD, and I'm determined to do it.

If I can accomplish laughter, relaxation, increased fat and yoga today, I'm going to call it a win.

### nosnhojjennifer@yahoo.com — 2012-08-02T03:27:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42412>

Full moon is wacking me out too! I'm all over the place. Hang in. Like ur list

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-08-02T05:09:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42437>

> **Jennifer;42668 wrote:** Full moon is wacking me out too! I'm all over the place. Hang in. Like ur list

You think it's the full moon? Haha! Honestly, I think what's whacking me out is restricting my eating this week. I'm about 2 inches away from zipping my dress up all the way for this dumb wedding. I should just go get the bridesmaid dress altered, but I'm really looking forward to just eating and not worrying about it and let my weight fall where it may. This wedding is causing too much stress, which is really dumb.

On a plus note, my yoga session was sooo relaxing! And, the redneck jokes made me laugh. ANDDDDD......it's RAINING! This drought is killing our crops and pastures! HURRAY for rain!

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-08-03T23:18:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42761>

I'm 5 days binge free!

I'm on day 3 of yoga, and it feels great to stretch, meditate and relax. I'm going to keep doing it several times each week.

I stocked up on some fertility supps: maca, red clover, red raspberry leave...I plan to take those supps with a B complex, vitex, DHA and probiotics. I've increased by prog. and estriol creams, too.

I'm adding a lot more fish to my diet. I just bought scallops, shrimp, salmon and a treat...lobster tail!

My bridesmaid dress ZIPS up for tomorrow. Yippee! Here's hoping I don't crash and burn tonight at the rehearsal supper or anytime tomorrow. Going to get back on track once I'm home from this event. Wish me luck!

### vkiernan — 2012-08-04T15:26:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/paleocowgirl-battles-hashis-adrenal-fatigue-pcos-binge-eating-with-ct-keto-paleo.1936/post-42827>

Robb Wolf talked about this...apparently when you are keto/paleo, your blood cells do not die off as quickly like they do when you eat a lot of carbs and sugar...so the A1C actually goes up as those blood cells are not dying off...so you have to look at other things like fasting blood glucose etc to know where you are at....

# Thread: showering after ct
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### msclman — 2012-06-19T00:21:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32019>

did 80 minutes in 56 degree water yesterday. my wife used to be on the zone and we have all those reusable ice packs so i loaded up the tub with those and all the ice in my freezer. water got down to 55.6. i tried holding the packs against my stomach but that was a little brutal

anyway when i get done is it bad to take a hot shower after? just feels so good. if i get out without doing that it takes forever to get my temp up again.

### kathylu — 2012-06-19T02:27:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32049>

> **msclman;32128 wrote:** did 80 minutes in 56 degree water yesterday. my wife used to be on the zone and we have all those reusable ice packs so i loaded up the tub with those and all the ice in my freezer. water got down to 55.6. i tried holding the packs against my stomach but that was a little brutal
>
>
>
> anyway when i get done is it bad to take a hot shower after? just feels so good. if i get out without doing that it takes forever to get my temp up again.

Believe it or not, I use the time after the bath to do a cold shower! You are already acclimated, so it actually feels comfortably warm. That way I can extend my cold exposure a bit. Call me crazy.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T02:29:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32050>

crazy smart is more like it

### msclman — 2012-06-19T04:00:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32061>

> **kathylu;32158 wrote:** Believe it or not, I use the time after the bath to do a cold shower! You are already acclimated, so it actually feels comfortably warm. That way I can extend my cold exposure a bit. Call me crazy.

how long have u been ct'ing?? i dont dip my head in the tub so a cold shower would be tough but i will try it. and report back

### msclman — 2012-06-19T05:58:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-32092>

so i did a 65 minute in 57 degree tonight and took a cold shower after. not as bad as i though it would be.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-26T08:20:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33617>

You get to the point where it just feels great to be in a cold shower. Like drinking a nice cold glass of water feels on a hot day, but on the outside all over you, like aaaaahhhhh (in a good refreshing way, not a holy smokes that's cold kind of way!)

Cold water on my head to wash my hair always felt good before, but my skins couldn't take it running down my back until I worked at cold-adapting. Now I don't enjoy hot showers anymore, but oh how I enjoy cold ones!!

### MartiD — 2012-06-26T19:46:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33708>

I usually start with a cold shower, wash my body/hair, etc and then slip into a cold bath. I find it much easier that way! I have been putting in frozen 2 ltr bottles to help lower water in bath. After I get settled I usually hold the bottle in between my legs for a few minutes at a time. It's really cold being directly on skin so I don't leave long but I figure it might help with inner thigh fat!

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-26T21:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33734>

> **MartiD;33846 wrote:** I usually start with a cold shower, wash my body/hair, etc and then slip into a cold bath. I find it much easier that way! I have been putting in frozen 2 ltr bottles to help lower water in bath. After I get settled I usually hold the bottle in between my legs for a few minutes at a time. It's really cold being directly on skin so I don't leave long but I figure it might help with inner thigh fat!

Good idea! I'll try doing that... I have bottles of water frozen waiting in my freezer...

As to cold shower, that's the only kind of shower I can do now. Can't tolerate warm showers any more. Right now the tap water comes out at 62F. In March and April it was 52F. Not sure about Jan. Will find out this coming winter. Cold shower is very good for facial skin... if you want another incentive to do it.

### msclman — 2012-06-27T07:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/showering-after-ct.1939/post-33885>

I guess im not at the point where it feels good to be cold. i put ice in the tub so when i take the shower the cold water is warmer than the water i was sitting in so it feels "good"

# Thread: 7 Months Pregnant and doing Leptin RX-Am I crazy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 11
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T01:02:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32031>

I'll be 28 weeks on Wednesday. About two years ago, I started seeing a naturopath and had my labs done including sex hormones. They showed that I had very low in Free T3,T4, high TSH, Low Vitamin D, and slightly elevated CRP. I started Paleo/Primal 80/20 a few months after that, along with Vit D supplements, Naturothyroid 1.5grains, Omega 3, and Vitamin C. My next set of labs 6 months later were GREAT. Everything within normal limits, EXCEPT Thyroid. I was still too low, so we upped my naturothyroid to 2 grains. The following labs were even BETTER. I felt wonderful, but still suffered from major sugar benders and then...I got pregnant.

My first trimester I ate EVERYTHING! I was binging on SUGAR and GRAINS 3x a week. Gained 12 lbs in the first three months. I have always fought sugar binges and I think I just let loose and gave myself some sort of free pass! So DUMB..But I moved on. About 4 months in, I began Paleo/Primal again.. Started feeling so much better. Anyways, I started the Leptin RX 2 weeks ago. I definitely had a few days of "carb flu" but it only lasted a few days. The problem is, I just had my labs done one week into my reset. I have never had a problem with Cholesterol, but my total cholesterol is 273, triglycerides 164, free T3 1.7 (low) Free T4 .5 (low), CRP 1.9 (Higher than ever). My non-fasting glucose was 79, with A1C 5.4. Another random low number was Bilirubin at .1 (Low). My question is, should I be doing the LR now? I just want what's best for my unborn baby girl and both or our long term health. Thanks for your input. My blood pressure 105/65 or within 5 or that.

Stephanie

### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T01:10:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32033>

I also forgot to mention that my WBC were high-11.2 (normal 4-11).

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T02:26:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32048>

You need to elimate all grains and all sugar......now. The last trimester is where your baby gains its weight to support its brain. Make sure your Vitamin D level and progesterone are solid......this determines how good or bad your babies synapse are.......and my best advice. Wear seafood out until you child is 2 and make sure they are connected to the tit for a long time to offset that first 28 week maternal diet.

### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T07:20:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32103>

Thank you Dr. K! I upped my Vit D to 5000 today. Grains have been out for about 2 months. Honey has been out for only 2 weeks. Does this mean I should lower my fat intake at my BAB? I have been doing Bacon, eggs, sausage which has kept me full until 1 or 2pm when eating at 6 or 7am. I definitely plan on breastfeeding as long as I can(1 + year, hopefully).

Question about seafood. I love it, but am wary. I had my mercury levels tested about 1.5 years ago, and they were elevated. I am a dentist and was questioning amalgam exposure. It was elevated, so I cut out fish, added lots of Vit C and fish oils, and got into a low range. Those were the only changes I made. I don't think amalgam was the culprit, I think it was the amazing sushi diet I was eating once a week. I have added in Salmon/tuna during my pregnancy sparingly, but am afraid to go full throttle..what are your thoughts? Thanks so much.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-19T14:46:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32115>

Eat the fish....not to worry.....and eat the fat. Iw ould back off on the bacon for the baby.......the baby needs DHA fat first and foremost

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-19T18:44:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32162>

> **walkers345;32213 wrote: Thank you Dr. K! I upped my Vit D to 5000 today. Grains have been out for about 2 months. Honey has been out for only 2 weeks. Does this mean I should lower my fat intake at my BAB? I have been doing Bacon, eggs, sausage which has kept me full until 1 or 2pm when eating at 6 or 7am. I definitely plan on breastfeeding as long as I can(1 + year, hopefully).
>
>
>
> Question about seafood. I love it, but am wary. I had my mercury levels tested about 1.5 years ago, and they were elevated. I am a dentist and was questioning amalgam exposure. It was elevated, so I cut out fish, added lots of Vit C and fish oils, and got into a low range. Those were the only changes I made. I don't think amalgam was the culprit, I think it was the amazing sushi diet I was eating once a week. I have added in Salmon/tuna during my pregnancy sparingly, but am afraid to go full throttle..what are your thoughts? Thanks so much.
>
>
> Jack Kruse;32227 wrote:** Eat the fish....not to worry.....and eat the fat. Iw ould back off on the bacon for the baby.......the baby needs DHA fat first and foremost

My MW recommended I eat at least 6 cans of tuna/week or other fish when pregnant w/both kids... The only seafood I could not eat was smoked oysters... all other fish was on the table!

### walkers345 — 2012-06-19T19:06:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32174>

Thank you Dr. K and Shijin, I will back off on the bacon. I am cooking my eggs in coconut oil, and taking DHA and Omega 3's. I will up the fish. I love it! Still doing great with a BAB. Will have to change it up to keep full for as long as I am and keep the cravings down. Maybe, by just upping the coconut oil, adding some tuna and taking out the Bacon and sausage, I can still be satisfied.

I do notice even if I have 1/2 a sweet potato, like last night, I am hungry after dinner. Should I go above 50 carbs during this last trimester so as to not be in ketosis? Is is bad for the baby?

### PaleoCowgirl — 2012-06-19T22:31:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32237>

I'm loving this thread! Thanks for asking such great questions, Walker. Although I'm not pregnant yet, I'm hoping making a family is in my future in the next year or two. So, I appreciate you asking these tough questions; it's great to learn!

Congrats on your baby and best of luck in the remaining months of your pregnancy.

### nonchalant — 2012-06-19T22:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32238>

Walkers, do you like brazil nuts? They are high in selenium to offset the mercury.

### walkers345 — 2012-06-20T00:28:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32259>

Thanks Cowgirl! It's funny how things get way more serious when you're pregnant. For a year I was 80/20 paleo, but would have binge days where I would eat whatever I wanted. I started out about 2 years ago doing 4hour body(which allowed one free day a week) I used it to the nth degree. I've tapered this by finding MDA and realizing lots of ways 4hb is unhealthy. I also was not fixing my BED, just allowing it one day a week. (more in my first trimester! and around the holidays). I was working out 4x a week doing spin and cardio kickboxing and weights. It's harder now because I have less energy and can only do spin/weights. ANYWAYS, my point is, when it comes to a baby, things get REAL! That one day binge I knew was hard on my heart and all my other organs. I just don't want this to affect my unborn child. So better late than never I guess. I just hope this is a long term solution and I have faith it is. I am doing well so far, and I think the key is the BAB. I also can NOT eat my trigger foods, including almond butter, and honey! I hope sometime in the future I will be able to without it triggering my sweet tooth, but maybe not!

Nonchalant, I was just reading about Brazil nuts and looked for them at Fresh and Easy, but couldn't find them. I'll have to go to Sprouts because they may have more options. I've heard one nut a week is sufficient? Forgot where I read that one. I had no idea they could offset the mercury. THANKS!

### nonchalant — 2012-06-21T17:55:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/7-months-pregnant-and-doing-leptin-rx-am-i-crazy.1940/post-32713>

Stephanie, Dr. K recommends one brazil nut every other day, but I usually eat one per day. I sometimes buy them at Sprouts, but you have to smell them first. Often they are a bit rancid, sitting in those large buckets at room temp. I've had good luck at Walmart, who sells them in a small sealed bag. Keep them in the fridge.

Also you might want to check into cilantro. Evidently it will help "escort" the selenium/mercury combo out of your body. Jack recommends cooking it as little as possible. I sprinkle it in my scrambled eggs.

Welcome to the forum!

\-Sally
