### janagram — 2012-06-21T00:38:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32531>

does anyone know the Kruse opinion on calcium supps? I don't take them anymore, but forget why....is that a good thing? (it' not good that I am losing my memory...)

### Melinda — 2012-06-21T02:28:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32553>

I think he says no to calcium. Vitamin D and K2 make bones strong and keep calcium where it belongs in the bones, not lining the arteries.

### Destiny — 2012-06-21T02:45:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32561>

> **Melinda;32671 wrote:** I think he says no to calcium. Vitamin D and K2 make bones strong and keep calcium where it belongs in the bones, not lining the arteries.

If I remember correctly he also liked magnesium.

### Melinda — 2012-06-21T02:53:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32565>

> **Destiny;32679 wrote:** If I remember correctly he also liked magnesium.

Yes, yes, I forgot about mg. Thanks Destiny.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T03:09:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32572>

> **Jack Kruse;32685 wrote:** Big no...........

Dr Kalish is asking me to supp calcium for the co-factors for NT support.... my exatest came back with elevated calcium without any calcium supps in months. What to do?

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-21T04:01:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32586>

> **Souldanzer;32690 wrote:** Dr Kalish is asking me to supp calcium for the co-factors for NT support.... my exatest came back with elevated calcium without any calcium supps in months. What to do?

If your exatest came back w/high Ca I'd skip the Ca. And push the mg. But that's me. Hope fully Jack will weigh in.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-21T04:02:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32587>

you dont need it if your exatest says your RBC are loaded......that means your Mg deficient and need Mg for NT formation. You should email him for clarity

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T04:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32591>

I'm trying to figure out how to post my results.... there's ratios involved and according to the ratios I am low in mag but it's b/c ca is elevated. Mag alone is borderline low. Doc took me off calcium (which I wasn't taking anyway) and told me to take mag.

Apparently elevated Ca can have to do with bone formation/break down in process? It says in the interpretation guide.

Dr Kalish never saw this test. I'll ask him anyways.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-21T04:07:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32592>

if he did not see it he likely wont recommend you take more of it. Dan is a great guy.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T04:08:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32593>

> **Shijin13;32707 wrote:** If your exatest came back w/high Ca I'd skip the Ca. And push the mg. But that's me. Hope fully Jack will weigh in.

I can't take any more mg. I'm doing bowel tolerance plus oil for 7 months now. I don't even want to know where I must have started....

### Lyndra — 2012-06-21T20:25:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-the-kruse-opinion-anyone-who-knows-it-on-supplementing-with-calcium.1969/post-32766>

>

# Thread: Endless Appetite Frustration
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bigknitwit — 2012-06-21T02:31:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32554>

I have been leptin Resetting for 14 weeks now. I have been doing CT for 10 weeks. I am still struggling with endless appetite. This morning I ate 1.2 pounds of salmon with cabbage, sauteed in 1/3 cup coconut oil, and would have continued eating. It's always this way. I am always sad when meal time finishes. This process would be much easier if I actually felt satiety after a meal. What's going wrong that's preventing this? I have dropped weight on the reset, and I'm within 7 pounds of my ideal weight, but it can change very quickly when eating the wrong foods (eggs, dairy, nuts, etc, which I haven't touched).

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-21T04:17:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32596>

I wonder if you are still hungry as your body processes the hormones (CT driven). My appetite rose when I started CT and then after 3 1/2 months finally started to subside. Now I eat just two meals a day and that is plenty. My body took a while to process the excess estrogen.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-21T04:19:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32599>

You have cytokines.......that is why. Keep going. A life time of damaged caused this and ten weeks you expect a miracle? WOW on you.

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-21T04:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32603>

Would you mind pointing to key post that help understand this? I think I'm missing some key pieces of info on how this prompts hypothalamus.

Also - I find it curious that only some hormones elicit resistance but not others.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-21T04:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32606>

Its coming in this series.......Brain Gut is a massive tie in of the Quilt.......go back and look at the quilt......I have spent close to yr on leptin........now we are at levee 5.......Brain gut.......Next blog post will be one of more radical ass kickers.......I promise. Its time to tear apart of what really makes us what we are.......Im going after Darwin, Einstein, Cordain Wolf, and most of evolution bone collectors......and Richard Dawkins.........how is that for a blog post?

### bigknitwit — 2012-06-22T16:54:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-32939>

> **Jack Kruse;32720 wrote:** You have cytokines.......that is why. Keep going. A life time of damaged caused this and ten weeks you expect a miracle? WOW on you.

Heh! Well, when you put it that way, I do sound a lot like a whiner lol! I will stay the course as long as the course needs to be stayed. Thank you for reassuring me that I am on the right one - much appreciated.

### janagram — 2012-07-01T08:48:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-34752>

> **Jack Kruse;32727 wrote:** Its coming in this series.......Brain Gut is a massive tie in of the Quilt.......go back and look at the quilt......I have spent close to yr on leptin........now we are at levee 5.......Brain gut.......Next blog post will be one of more radical ass kickers.......I promise. Its time to tear apart of what really makes us what we are.......Im going after Darwin, Einstein, Cordain Wolf, and most of evolution bone collectors......and Richard Dawkins.........how is that for a blog post?

Whoa.......! Uh...can't wait to hear this!

### Shijin13 — 2012-07-02T16:35:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endless-appetite-frustration.1972/post-35004>

> **bigknitwit;32672 wrote:** I have been leptin Resetting for 14 weeks now. I have been doing CT for 10 weeks. I am still struggling with endless appetite. This morning I ate 1.2 pounds of salmon with cabbage, sauteed in 1/3 cup coconut oil, and would have continued eating. It's always this way. I am always sad when meal time finishes. This process would be much easier if I actually felt satiety after a meal. What's going wrong that's preventing this? I have dropped weight on the reset, and I'm within 7 pounds of my ideal weight, but it can change very quickly when eating the wrong foods (eggs, dairy, nuts, etc, which I haven't touched).

FWIW I didn't start eating the Post LR until close to 9mths after being on the LR. after my last sets of labs, specifically my HIGH BG?insulin/HbA1C readings - Its pretty evident I still need to eat following the LR. It takes time. you will get there. Consider how long it took you to get to a point where you wanted to make a change - for me it took a serious medical reaction to drugs i was on, for me to start looking for alternatives - I went gluten free in 2004, it took me another 6yrs, to go 100% grain free. guess what I've got close to 36 yrs that I need to reverse. It won't take me 12wks to reverse this... it will take a couple of years - I'm OK w/that... be patient, let your body heal itself. trust it... it works...

# Thread: Freezed my fat first time since CT.. :-/
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 39
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Inger — 2012-06-21T10:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-32636>

Yesterday I laid in the sun on my stomach and had an gel-icepack under my abdomen.

After a while I just intuitionally checked how the skin looked on the iced area.. and I got a shock. It was partly, in long thin stripes, totally frozen, white and hard. I got so scared I run downstairs and tried to shower my abnominal area with warm water but it hurted real bad so I quit. I decided to just warm my skin up in the sun instead. After a while the frozen parts thawed and got red and swollen and hurted by touch. I then went to work. When home again I noticed it was less swollen already but still red. Today the day after, nothing more! My skin is totally healed, nothing to see!!! Wow that was a fast recovery (maybe my raw mackerel-head-smoothie helped the healing.. HA). Thanks God. I was so scared my stomach will look awful for days and weeks and I love to use my bikini all the time..

Inger

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-21T11:43:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-32638>

Some gel packs will get colder than ice when frozen. I wish I had the guts to have a raw mackerel head smoothie.

### Inger — 2012-06-21T14:41:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-32651>

> **Souldanzer;32763 wrote:** I wish I had the guts to have a raw mackerel head smoothie.

You have... you actually need no guts whatsoever for doing that Souldancer! Just close your nose and you taste nothing! Right after put something nice tasting in your mouth.

If your health is important for you, really important (think about this for a while..), you are able to do the most crazy things, believe me. And it is not hard, not a second. Because you know, it does your body so very good and helps the healing tremendously.

I am stone hard with what I let in my body.. I choke down raw elk lungs and other organs and they makes me feel great too.

Food is for our healing.. so very important too!

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-06-21T15:35:32+0300

### Cowboy — 2012-06-21T17:16:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-32702>

I started CT with cold packs and this freaked me out the first time it happened to me. Only on the belly, never anyplace else, but it is a very odd feeling to find your fat stiff and frozen. I had no pain or rash. It may not be as controlled as Zeltiq, but then again Zeltiq sounds like it is a bit of "flying by the seat of the pants" also. Just because they charge a pile for it doesn't mean it is foolproof. Google Zeltiq problems and you will find lots of unhappy customers.

### PaleoMom — 2012-06-23T06:31:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33119>

### Inger — 2012-06-23T08:59:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33124>

Ohh.. THANK YOU!!!!

It feel so very good to be with people that have the same view as I.. it is hard because mostly - also in the paleo-word - people do not want to see the benefits of eating certain body parts that are not the norm in our society.

But yes, raw organs and fisheads are nothing short of amazing when it comes to make you feel great. They kind of makes me fly, really!!

I eat the mackerel-head-smoothie only a few time / month, because they have whole mackerels that seldom here. Sad but true.

But always when they have them, I do the smoothie! I eat the roe and the fishmilk too.

I ate the brain of one of my parents lambs too. Raw. That tasted great BTW. Creamy and mild. I did not needed to hold my nose then ;-).

It amazed me how focused I was the time after eating the brain, I had a huge test in school and I had no time to learn before. But sitting there with the papers in front of me.. the answers just popped up.. everything I needed!!!

I wish I had access to fresh lambsbrain more often but it is aganst the law to sell here sadly. But - mackerel-heads will do it too!

Yes, I just toss the heads in a blender with water, enough to make it drinkable.

Thats it! Very convenient.

Wow, you are a brave woman!! I don't think I could eat ANY brain!!

### AKMan — 2012-06-23T20:43:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33196>

My grandfather ate brains of every kind. We all laughed at him. He ate lard sandwiches. We all said it would kill him. He ate 'head cheese' and raw hamburger. We all said, 'gross'. He died 15 years ago at age 95, wish he was still here...we'd have a picnic.

### SeaHorse — 2012-06-26T00:20:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33538>

I seem to be the only one of my sisters who inherited my mother's taste for innards. She and I would fight over kidneys, hearts, liver and brains and would be sitting at the table chewing on the ends of bones long after everyone else had finished their meal. I kind of held back for a few decades when I was in my teens and twenties from fear of ridicule....but now I'm proud to make the cashiers roll their eyes when I unload my cart and my own family just gives me all their bones without comment.

This summer I will try a trout smoothie, fresh from our pond. That's a promise! I can't believe I just wrote that....

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-26T03:15:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/freezed-my-fat-first-time-since-ct.1974/post-33563>

>
>
>
> I think I'm in for the challenge  Now where to get mackerel heads.... my dog loves the whole thing, gut, head and all.

Okay! Got the mackerel! I cut off the head and stuck the rest in the oven.

What's next?

### Inger — 2012-06-26T17:52:28+0300

Oh no, I think you want need that with mackerel! I eat not frozen mackerel heads every time! It is as safe as sashimi in a sushiplace.

The parasites you can get from fish are not really dangerous eather, you get rid of them easy with a pill if you get an issue. I never had and I have been eating raw fish for about 7 years! I have eaten fish that had maggots too, but I just realized it after I ate the fish.. so too late. I often look in the toilet to check if there are anything moving, but nothing there. Also I feel great. So I think everything is fine!

I would only worry if the fish is from lakes (not saltwater) or very polluted areas. Also such fish I have eaten (that was a mistake) raw and have had no issues after that to this day.

Mackerel is just great! Do not worry Souldancer! Eat your heads as soon as possible, they are best fresh! But a few days in the fridge is fine for sure.

### Dan in Utah — 2012-06-21T18:00:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-32714>

[http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;KkdFkPxxDG8\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;KkdFkPxxDG8) Dr Stephen Phinney, MD, PhD

### Caroline Cooper — 2012-06-22T15:39:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-32925>

> **Dan in Utah;32840 wrote:** [http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]\]\[video=youtube\_share;KkdFkPxxDG8\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkdFkPxxDG8&feature=related\[/video\]](http://[video=youtube_share;KkdFkPxxDG8) Dr Stephen Phinney, MD, PhD

Hi Dan,

That was a very interesting video. I decided to look up his book and reserved a copy from the library. I sat wondering about the cover of his book which has a picture of vegetable oil being poured on a salad. What? You have to wonder what type of marketing editor came up with that picture. Where's the hunk of fat and meat?

After learning as much as I have about industrial vegetables oils I would skip the recommended safflower oil or canola oil. Ugh. Stick with an organic virgin olive oil.

I have been wondering about the traditional ketogenic diet used for treating epilepsy, before the modern use of vegetable oils. Before we had anti-convulsants the ketogenic diet was used to treat children and adults with seizure disorders. I would love to see some long term studies of ketogenic diets without vegetable oils. I am sure it has already been done. Do you know anything about the long term use of a ketogenic diet with children with epilepsy?

### Michael — 2012-06-24T18:39:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33300>

> **WannaBeOptimal;33154 wrote:** It's the seed oil issue, Ray. When he recommended canola oil, I had a bird.

I don't think that's what Jack meant.

Steve Phinney did say he thought Canola Oil acceptable. So have plenty of others -- Loren Cordain, for example. Cordain has now withdrawn the recommendation, having got what looks like a lot of of pushback from Paleo people coming out a WAPF background. (Mary Enig, the fats expert at the WAPF, thinks Canola Oil pretty bad, especially because while it may be mostly monounsaturated fats, which is better than polyunsaturates, there's erucic acid in it (although they tried to breed it out).) It's a side issue.

I think Jack simply meant that he thinks that very low carb diets work best in cold environments.

### Michael — 2012-06-24T19:08:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33310>

> **Caroline Cooper;33123 wrote:** I have been interested in the topic for some time. It is hard to find information about ketogenic diets before modern vegetable oils.
>
>
> There are probably some 19th-century books gathering dust in libraries, if they haven't been thrown out.
>
>
>
> I'd suspect they'd have made extensive use of animal carcase fats. I say that, because in those days everyone did. In England, for example:
>
>
>
> Dripping from beef has always been approved for cooking, and until the arrival of margarine (coinciding with the banishment of the cottager's pig), most children ate bread and dripping instead of bread and butter. Even in the opulent houses of late Victorian days, dripping cakes and dripping toast were considered excellent, whereas the mutton fast was the cooks's perquisite.

I have a cookery book on Norman Cuisine (from the 1980s, I think) and that has a recipe for Graisse Normand:

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Norman-Table-Traditional-Normandy/dp/0684183196/](http://http://www.amazon.com/The-Norman-Table-Traditional-Normandy/dp/0684183196/)

That's a cooking-fat you make with beef and pork fat and flavoring vegetables. The text seems to suggest that, although we associate Normandy with dairy products, and although most recipes in the book do use butter, extensive use of butter is part of a relatively recent gentrification of Norman cooking.

The chapter in _TSLCL_ is by Eric Kossoff. I don't know what fats and oils he's using. He is also using what they call a Modified Atkins Diet, which has been around since 2002, where possible. This is because the children suffer fewer side-effects on that than on the ketogenic diet. In Boston they've used a low-glycemic index diet, and apparently that has worked, too.

Kossoff has got a book out, and he's been interviewed here;

[http://www.thelivinlowcarbshow.com/shownotes/1972/dr-eric-kosoff-treats-epilepsy-with-ketosis-episode-367/](http://http://www.thelivinlowcarbshow.com/shownotes/1972/dr-eric-kosoff-treats-epilepsy-with-ketosis-episode-367/)

### jonnyh — 2012-06-25T14:59:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33423>

Jack

Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?

Thanks

### Michael — 2012-06-25T15:47:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33425>

> **jonnyh;33559 wrote:** Jack
>
>
>
> Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?
>
> Thanks

What's your reference for that, Jonny?

I've got a copy of the _Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living and nowhere do I recall their saying that.

I do recall their saying that the brain requires approximately 600 kcal per day. Furthermore, that it could run on either glucose or ketones. But where_ the brain might get 600 kcal of glucose seems to me be another matter. There's gluconeogenesis. Also, there could be other demands on glucose, depending on what you're doing and how efficient you are at using different substrates.

In practice, there are, of course, people eating, say, 70 g or 100 g or 120 g of dietary carbohydrate a day, and their brains don't close down ... although they rmight prompt their owners to go get a snickers bar in certain circumstances!

### jonnyh — 2012-06-25T16:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33430>

> **Michael;33561 wrote:** What's your reference for that, Jonny?
>
>
>
> I've got a copy of the _Art and Science of Low Carbohydrate Living and nowhere do I recall their saying that.
>
>
>
> I do recall their saying that the brain requires approximately 600 kcal per day. Furthermore, that it could run on either glucose or ketones. But where_ the brain might get 600 kcal of glucose seems to me be another matter. There's gluconeogenesis. Also, there could be other demands on glucose, depending on what you're doing and how efficient you are at using different substrates.
>
>
>
> In practice, there are, of course, people eating, say, 70 g or 100 g or 120 g of dietary carbohydrate a day, and their brains don't close down ... although they rmight prompt their owners to go get a snickers bar in certain circumstances!

Hi Michael, it's in the performance book.

Just found this, it is the exact quote from the performance book, I copied this from a review I read:

'If you eat a reduced carb diet of less than 150 grams but more than 50 grams, you body does not become Keto-adapted and you are starving your brain of both glucose and ketones. End result – the body converts protein to glucose or - you go on a carb binge'

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-06-25T20:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33490>

> **jonnyh;33559 wrote:** Jack
>
>
>
> Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?
>
> Thanks

Oooh, I've never heard this theory - interesting. I wonder if the switching from keytones/carbs can actually be regulated by the body very swiftly - or perhaps that is part of why sleep is so important at those levels. You need that time during sleep to generate the keytones.

### Michael — 2012-06-25T21:08:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33496>

> **jonnyh;33566 wrote: this ...is the exact quote from the performance book, I copied this from a review I read:
>
>
>
> 'If you eat a reduced carb diet of less than 150 grams but more than 50 grams, you body does not become Keto-adapted and you are starving your brain of both glucose and ketones. End result – the body converts protein to glucose or - you go on a carb binge'**

**

Thanks. Good to have the authors' words.

So that would be from this book:

[http://www.amazon.com/The-Art-Science-Carbohydrate-Performance/dp/0983490716/](http://http://www.amazon.com/The-Art-Science-Carbohydrate-Performance/dp/0983490716/)

I don't think that says quite the same. But we have exactly what they said now, so thanks.

I guess their point there is that an athlete - this is from the book written for athletes - is likely to pretty soon burn through 600 kcals of dietary carbohydrate (even if the activity is mostly anaerobic). So, if he hasn't got ketones, since his brain requires the one fuel or the other, he must either get glucose from gluconeogenesis or "go on a carb binge" ... or, presumably, collapse outright.

It doesn't seem all that different to what was said in the book for a more general audience. The difference, I guess, is that they say this will** happen to the athlete. The person on the high-carb, low-calorie diet (1200 kcal's, 50% of which is carbs) that they gave as an example can, of course, just cut the exercise, which is not an option for a professional athlete.

I can't see there's a point of contention there. ...

... Unless it's the suggestion they make that keto-adaptation just will not happen unless you get below 50 g of carbohydrate a day, so that there is no smooth transition.

Is that the issue you were thinking of?

Mark Sisson _seems_ to speak as if he thinks it's a seamless process where the level of the one goes down and the other up. I say "seems" - I don't want to put words in his mouth, but that seems to be where he's coming from. Here he is taking about ketosis:

[http://www.askthelowcarbexperts.com/2012/02/5-ketosis-devil-or-angel-mark-sisson/](http://http://www.askthelowcarbexperts.com/2012/02/5-ketosis-devil-or-angel-mark-sisson/)

i don't know. I should think V & P have worked with enough patients, and also subjects in clinical settings, and taken enough measurements while doing so to know whether keto-adaptation is a smooth or discontinuous process.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-25T21:19:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33499>

> **jonnyh;33559 wrote:** Jack
>
>
>
> Not sure if this has ben asked elsewhere, apologies if it has, but whats your view on V & P's theory that if your between 50g(or enough carbs to NOT be in ketosis), and 150g carbs, your not supplying your brain enough glucose?
>
> Thanks

I dont believe you need any exogenous glucose for the brain period. Our liver makes the endogenous amounts we need implicitly.

### jonnyh — 2012-06-25T22:25:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33515>

> **Michael;33632 wrote:**
>
> Unless it's the suggestion they make that keto-adaptation just will not happen unless you get below 50 g of carbohydrate a day, so that there is no smooth transition.
>
>
>
> Is that the issue you were thinking of?

Yes that was the issue I was thinking of, if you look at the link below, look for the 10th post down by Barry, he refers to the same thing:

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories)

Thanks for the link, I'll have a listen when I get the chance.

Cheers

### Michael — 2012-06-25T23:22:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-showing-why-vlc-really-means-high-fat.1981/post-33531>

> **jonnyh;33652 wrote:** Yes that was the issue I was thinking of, if you look at the link below, look for the 10th post down by Barry, he refers to the same thing:
>
>
>
> [http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories](http://http://forum.jackkruse.com/showthread.php?1092-Fasted-State-Training-Stories)

Thanks.

It's interesting, isn't it? My understanding is that the brain comes in because it's the limiting factor, since it can only burn two of the three possible energy substrates. If it's running on glucose, it grabs what's around -- which might, for example, have been made endogenously in the liver -- and there can be nothing left for the muscles. This is when cyclists "bonk".

If your brain could run on a mixture of glucose and ketones in any proportion, then it wouldn't grab all the glucose when there wasn't much around, and there'd be more glucose left for the muscles. But it seems that doesn't happen, because production of ketones, while it varies between individuals, doesn't usually ramp up until dietary carbs are not just low but very low (and dietary protein moderate, too).

Mark Sisson mentioned Peter Attia -- an athlete who admits to low carb. (Some athletes are probably doing low carb but keeping it a secret to gain an edge.) I just tried googling his name, and I reckon this could be worth a look:

[http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/the-interplay-of-exercise-and-ketosis-part-i](http://http://eatingacademy.com/nutrition/the-interplay-of-exercise-and-ketosis-part-i)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-21T22:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-reverse-t3-ratio.1987/post-32789>

Your reverse T3 is 23, and this implies that your TSH is not that accurate.....and your free T3 is low.....that means you have pregnenolone steal and you LDL's are probably high and your hormones are likely not optimal. You need to look a bit deeper into your labs to cull more info with your doc.

### JanSz — 2012-06-21T22:51:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/help-reverse-t3-ratio.1987/post-32798>

> **fitness@home;32913 wrote: Hi,
>
>
>
> I'd like to ask y'all for some assistance in figuring out what my Reverse T3 **Ratio** is. Got some labs back today and still looking for answers. Had to attach results as a jpg because the copy/paste method resulted in a jumbled mess.
>
>
>
>
> http://www.jackkruse.com/forum/convert\_files/attachments/32913=253-Thyroid Labs.jpg
>

> harry Says:
>
> June 8th, 2011 at 3:43 pm
>
> If reverse T3 is the key to leptin status, what should we be looking for? Is the ratio of rT3 to fT3 what is important, and what range for rT3 or the rT3/fT3 is desirable?
>
>
>
> admin Says:
>
> June 8th, 2011 at 4:20 pm
>
> @Harry you are looking for a problem in the ratio between the RT3 and the Free T3. i.e dividing the Free T3 by the Reverse T3 (Free T3 ÃƒÂ· RT3). For healthy amounts of RT3, The ratio result should be 20 or larger. If it happens to be lower, then you have an issue. If you use the total T3, you are looking for a ratio greater than 10. If lower, you have an issue. The real problem is that the units that rT3 and T3 are measured in are often different! Why I have no idea but it creates a calculation problem. I usually ask my lab to calculate it for me so I dont have too. Then I give the news to the patient.**

High LDL is a sign of a few things.....one is your LDL receptor status......bad genetically or damaged from glycation or lipoxidation or you have pregnenolone steal syndrome for some reason.......the causes of that vary and are substantial. This is why people do consults with doctors.......to find out why they in particular have it. There are no blanket causes.......contrary to what the non doctor bloggers tell you on the internet. This is why internet forums are littered with people who remain clueless why they are broken......and then they blame us. If you ask the people who talk to me or who see me........I cut straight to the heart of the matter so you can see your problem in a crystal clear light. And If I don't know.......I will tell you that too.......Im not afraid of telling you I dont know.....and I also want everyone questioning me too........in fact, I usually mention that in my educational consults.......I give you a blueprint or framework within for you to rebuild your self with your docs help.......and I give you bits of knowledge that you can put together to create your own personal wisdom.......so that you become your own inner guru by adjusting your thinking. Always question and never settle..........that is my dogma.

# Thread: Hunger:  Leptin or Mind Tricks?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AKMan — 2012-06-22T00:58:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-32835>

46yo male here.

Back in the day, I ate breakfast every day. Usually something crappy, like donuts, hashbrowns, biscuits and gravy.

Shortly after doing the reset last summer, I stopped eating breakfast altogether. If I eat before lunch, it's a can of sardines. I have a pretty good appetite for lunch and eat a big salad and more fish or some other meat.

For the past 2 months, I have been fasting one day a week--usually on Thursday. I eat a normal dinner on Wednesday and nothing until noon on Friday, about 42 hours. I am doing this for the puported health benefits of fasting, not to lose weight. I am at my ideal body weight, and have been for 6 mo or so.

On a normal day, I start getting hungry around 1030 and eat my lunch between 11 and 1130. Then, no snacks until dinner at 6, and I'm usually pretty hungry by then.

On my fasting days, I don't experience hunger at all. In fact, the thought of eating kind of turns my stomach.

So, what is going on? On the days I eat normally, is it the leptin telling me I'm hungry? Or just my brain because it's nearing lunch or supper time? What happens to the leptin signals on my fasting days?

The first couple times I tried fasting, I could only make it 20 hours. Now 42hrs is nothing and I could probably go 72 if I wanted.

### excitablestate@gmail.com — 2012-07-08T20:01:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-36212>

You have a couple of things going on. One is the fact you are in an extreme northern environment. This plays a big part on energy needs. Being in such a cold environment and on such short light cycles, should maintain high levels of BAT. You don't need to eat as often to maintain energy levels.

Second is the body only thinks in feast or famine modes. When you don't eat your body goes in reserve mode and assumes food isn't going to be available anytime soon (your external environment plays a huge part in this perception). So when you do eat your body figures it is a one chance deal that it can get energy and it wants to feast.

### chipmeister — 2012-08-17T07:49:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-45309>

Kind of on the same track, I'm finding myself getting hungry later in the evenings, even after eating a big dinner. For example, tonight we had a great sashimi dinner with a big ol' poki salad. I ate like it was my last meal. Then about two hours later I was ravenous. The problem for me is that I know we shouldn't eat within 4 hours of going to bed/sleep.

My question is this: is it normal to get hungry again after only a couple of hours when your letpin is reset? I remember when I was younger and my leptin was probably still good, I could eat 2 dinners and think nothing of it...you know the old "hollow leg". I drank a big water and tried to convince myself that that would be enough...not working so well.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-08-17T15:07:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-45324>

No it is not normal and means something is amiss.......inflammation likely causing hypothyroidism is an issue maybe......free T3 low?

### excitablestate@gmail.com — 2012-08-18T04:11:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hunger-leptin-or-mind-tricks.1990/post-45485>

Like Jack said, it's not normal. I have the same problem, but for me it only takes about 30 minutes for me to be hungry again. I got my lab work done about five months ago when I started fat adapting. Going to have them done again in a month to compare. That hopefully will give me a better idea on how to tackle the problem.

That sucks that lots of water doesn't work for you, it seems to help me. Maybe trying adding some sea salt to the water. Or try a fatty snack (dark chocolate bar.)

**Posts:** 19

### Sem — 2012-06-22T07:57:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32900>

What do you do when you are following the Leptin Reset and are on vacation? I'm going to be in a hotel for about five days and I am not sure how to do the BAB while there. I am following the AI protocol so don't do eggs. How do I start a big protein breakfast within 30 minutes? What have you done? Any tips and tricks are appreciated!

### Patty Cakes — 2012-06-22T18:46:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32958>

I pre-cook burgers, bring liverwurst, small cans of salmon, coconut oil, and whatever else I can pack that I would eat. I ask the hotel for a refrigerator and I'm good to go. Oftentimes I can even get to a little store and pick up a salad if I feel I want more...I usually don't. I travel at least once a month and this has worked for me for the past six months. I also just get up and get that food in my stomach immediately upon wakening. Yeah, the food is cold but to me that isn't even a sacrifice as long as I can continue the proper eating.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-22T18:50:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32960>

Not even sure why this is a question? You can eat the same no matter where you go......good food is there you just have to hunt and gather more.

### maggie — 2012-06-22T19:07:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-32964>

Hi Sem

On a recent trip to a city and in a hotel room with no fridge, I found a Whole Foods and bought cans of herring, tuna, and salmon as well as avocados and raddiccio / other raw greens. Also got some nice liver pate that I put on ice and ate right away because of no fridge. On an earlier kayaking trip I packed ghee, cans of coconut cream and a lot of US Wellness beef jerky in my suitcase since I wouldn't have access to a good store. Breakfast is easy cause you can eat it all up before you have to join the group if you're in one.

### BJK77 — 2012-06-22T22:09:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33011>

I always try to get a hotel room with a kitchenette. If that doesn't work out, take a cooler and just keep it filled with ice. You can get a cooktop relatively cheap on amazon. Yes, it's a bit of a pain and more to pack but so worth it to stick to the program.

Another option is to take or go to a nearby grocery store and get things that store at room temp like canned sardines, jerky, avocados, greens, etc. Many large chain grocery stores (I know Whole Foods, Harris Teeter and a few others) will actually steam shrimp and other seafood for you. I've done that many, many times! Makes for a great, quick dinner and if you plan ahead and have leftovers you can just keep it on ice for breakfast the next morning.

It's a pain, but really not that difficult to stick with it. Just requires some planning.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-22T22:28:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33015>

Well I am going to Disney world for two weeks in July and August and I wont buy one thing in their parks.....not one.

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-22T23:26:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33027>

I just spent 3 days/2 nights in Las Vegas and I lost 2 lbs. while eating amazing food there!

I ate two meals a day, no snacks, no alcohol (I'm doing CT and avoiding all alcohol so it also saved me $$ ! )

Each morning, I went to breakfast early and got:

3 eggs plus a side order of bacon and an order of sausage. I asked them to hold the bread, potatoes, etc, so I wouldn't be tempted. The super high amounts of protein & fat filled me up until around 3 or 4 each day.

For my early evening dinner, here were my meals:

1) Huge cobb salad (I subbed crabmeat for the chicken). I liked this so much, I ate it again at a different restaurant the next day.

2) Sturgeon & asparagus. Yum. Never had sturgeon before.

I was so satisfied on these 2 meals, and it occurred to me as I looked around, how much people eat and how often. And how, on previous trips, I was one of those who was constantly in "vacation" mode: Oh, look...I better eat one of THESE, right now! I'm on vacation!! Instead, I ate my Paleo meals, did my CT in the hotel tub, got a lot of sun, and reaaaally enjoyed myself.

This is the first time in my life (I'm 50!) that I've ever gone on vacation and not come home bloated, miserable, and + 5 lbs.

Now, the best thing to do would be to bring all your own food. Or, find a Whole Foods type-store in your destination city. But if you're traveling with others and that's not as feasible, you can definitely order sensibly.

### Sem — 2012-06-23T02:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33075>

Great suggestions! Thank you. I am going to cook up some food and either keep it in the fridge in the room or bring an icebox. My biggest concern was getting breakfast and that much protein into my body in time. I can manage ordering well for any other meals and I'm not really tempted by junk food anymore! We are going to Yosemite but staying at a very nice hotel right outside the park so I should be fine as long as I get some breakfast rather quickly. I was imagining huge room service bills trying to order steak every morning!  I hadn't really thought of precooking and bringing my own meat although it seems so obvious now! Thanks again!

And Disneyland/world really does have the worst food!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-23T02:47:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33080>

I will be chefing it up in Orlando.

### TerrierMom — 2012-06-23T04:56:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33109>

You can find keto food in Disney but as with all food not cooked by yourself, God knows what they've put in it to give it crack-like addiction!!! I'm a frequent Disneyland-goer and know where to find what I can eat...but it ain't optimal (except the seafood buffet at Club 33!)

### Owl — 2012-06-23T08:28:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33122>

2 entire weeks at Disney World!!?? What are you going to do there for that long?

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-23T16:27:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33133>

Its easy to travel and eat paleo and stay on the LR. BRING YOUR OWN FOOD... stay in an extended stay hotel that has a kitchen, buy good food at the local organic market...

even if that isn't an option...bring your coconut oil... eat 2-3 spoonfuls at eat meal, and eat the best LR/paleo foods you can find... they may not be optimal..but at least you'll stay on track..

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-23T16:47:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33143>

> **Owl;33254 wrote:** 2 entire weeks at Disney World!!?? What are you going to do there for that long?

relax and swim......go to the ice bar in orlando......and if any of you show up I may optimize you in the sun walrus style on the pool deck? I just need to get away for a time......and right now this would be a good time. This means I will be free a lot for consults......so when I am free I am not rushed for time.......this is why weekend consults rock too.

### PrimalPam — 2012-06-23T17:22:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33152>

While in Vegas, I had a treatment at the Qua Spa at Caesar's. Amongst the many hot tubs, saunas, etc., they had....an arctic room! It was probably below 50F in there. Unfortunately, I was in a hurry, but I did step in to see what it was. Very cool! (pun intended).

### MartiD — 2012-06-24T16:23:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/leptin-rx-on-vacation.1996/post-33269>

I saw lots of advertising for the ice bar while in Vegas. It wasn't at a spa...actual bar completely made of ice. Photos showed people drinking martinis in full fur coats. They must provide the coats because nobody would need a coat out there now...at 108!

# Thread: My Quest to Look Good Naked
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 17
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Hilltowner — 2012-06-24T00:47:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33222>

Just finishing week 3 of the LR and still reading the MDA monster thread and figured I should start logging what I'm doing.

Female, 52, 5'2" and 123. Not entirely sure I am LR in all aspects but flab is on love handles and saddlebags. So in love with the BAB I can see doing it forever.

Background: born biggish (8+ lbs), never breastfed, grew up on SAD with a mom who hated to cook. Always small and underweight. Undiagnosed ADHD but did well in school. Stayed at 115 or less through age 35 or so, through 3 easy pregnancies. Did not really worry about weight until at about age 41, after yet another baby at 38. Over the last decade I used lower carb and weightlifting off and on to keep myself in an acceptable range but it seems to have lost its magic in the last year. Last period was February and they have been 6 to 8 months apart for over a year.

Current supplements:

Natural Sources Raw Thyroid

Jarrow DHEA 50 mg

Carlson Vit D3 4000 IU

Thorne Vit K2. 15 drops

Lugol's 2% Iodine 1 drop/ day

2 BrazilNuts

Emerita progesterone cream 2 x day

Magnesium Citrate 400 mg

I need to get a hormone panel and a cortisol test. I feel like most of the 5 or so pounds is water. I did strict ZC for over 2 years and while I felt good through most of that time, I do wonder if it is the best long term solution for me. I do not really believe that LC vegetables are harmful for me; I only eat fruit that is growing in my yard.

I also wonder about my thyroid; my hair is thin, and my body hair (arms and legs) almost nonexistent. My eyebrows are almost invisible. I don't feel tired but am unmotivated when it comes to exercise.

Libido...ehhh. Really want this to improve, as I have a wonderful, affectionate husband.

Really happy to be in such a thoughtful group!

Laura

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-24T01:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33228>

If youre serious......then you need to listen to the Deep CT webinar and you better listen up to the June webinar and the post that is going to come on June 30th......because those are critical to good nakedness.

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-24T01:27:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33230>

Thanks for the input, will do!

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-24T12:48:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33261>

Slept from 10 to 5:30... Vivid dreams as usual the last 2 weeks. I was sure it was Monday ( not Sunday) for several minutes and now am so relieved I still have some weekend left!.

123 today. I did CT on my belly last night with the ice wrap. I need to buy another to go around the back side. I found this much easier than the cold tub I had done the 3 nights previous, even though we had had temps in the high 90s.

Breakfast: bone broth, chicken sausage, leftover NY strip

Lunch: chopped tomatoes with evoo and balsamic, yogurt

Dinner: yellow bell pepper w/ tahini dip, oven "pancake" (eggs, CO , butter and almond flour)

Crio Bru, 2 homegrown strawberries

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-25T13:48:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33415>

Lots of dreams again, featuring my co-workers!

124 today. Dairy, why do you hate me? I don't seem to get the sinus/ cough issues from yogurt that I did from even the tiniest amount of cream, but the bloating still happens.

Breakfast: bone broth, 1/2 lb burger.

Dinner: 2 chicken skewers, tomatoes in evoo/ balsamic, 1/2 orange bell with tahini

4 strawberries, Crio Bru

Current supplements:

Natural Sources Raw Thyroid

Jarrow DHEA 50 mg

Carlson Vit D3 4000 IU

Thorne Vit K2. 15 drops

Lugol's 2% Iodine 2 drop/day

2 BrazilNuts

Emerita progesterone cream 2 x day

Magnesium Citrate 400 mg

I should get some prenenolone today or tomorrow in UPS?

Did not get hungry at all at work. I noticed late this afternoon that I managed to get frostibite on a few spots around my waist last night using the ice wraps. It didn't feel uncomfortable at the time but i guess it went numb.

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-26T13:49:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33628>

124 . Still feel puffy. No CT last night.

Breakfast: bone broth, TJ Kobe burger, decaf coffee with coconut milk

Dinner: pork belly, kimchi, orange bell pepper w/ tahini sauce

crio Bru

Coconut water while at the vitamin shoppe

Added: B complex and potassium. Bought some maca and prenenolone too.

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-27T13:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-33904>

123\. Did not sleep well; woke up around 2 and stayed awake for an hour. i now know it was because one of my aged chickens was being taken out by a fox or some other critter, judging by the feathers in my backyard. We will miss you, Farmgirl!

Breakfast: burger with some Bubbie's pick relish, bone broth, 2 Tab CO ( I also get one with my K2 dose), decaf coffee with coconut milk

Started 30 mg prenenolone today, dropped my DHEA to 25. Started Maca (500 I think) also.

Dinner: a small piece of pork belly, grilled salmon with butter, some kimchi and 2 squares Lindt 90%

No lunch, but felt kind of nauseated today around 1.

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-28T14:00:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34122>

122\. Did not get to sleep til about 11, and woke up with the alarm at 6. Something I added this week may be doing this, or maybe the 1 drop increase in iodine? I also seem to have some nasal stuff going on. Plus I think I need to take my mag by 8 pm and I was out with ds13.

Breakfast: 4 egg omelet in a little butter with leftover salmon, bone broth.

That held me REALLY well all day; none of the sleepy feeling like yesterday afternoon. Not starving at 6 when I got home

Dinner: grilled marinated chicken thighs, an orange bell pepper with tahini dip. A little Bubbie's sauerkraut. Crio Bru, though it wasn't all that special today.

DD19 found me some 99% chocolate at a World Market ( we have none anywhe close) but it is silent so far.

My frostbite spots are better but it still feels like someone beat me.

Took my magnesium before 7; I have to be up by 5 a.m.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-28T14:30:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34125>

if youre going to be looking good naked you must get more sleep.......

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-28T14:35:33+0300

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-28T14:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34128>

sleep is greater than supplements.......for nakedness

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-29T02:08:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34279>

i was in a great mood all day long, and now I have a job interview first thing in the morning. Wish me luck! I am happy to be employed right now, but really want to be doing something more challenging.

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-29T19:41:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34411>

Thanks Paulalynn.

122 today. Sleep was evasive so I am omitting new supplements. Today I left out the maca capsule.

Breakfast: bone broth, leftover grilled chicken. 1/2 cup of black decaf. 2 tab CO.

I thought the interview went pretty well, but what do I know.

Dinner: garlic chicken wings, 2 squares Lindt 90%.

really good day as far as mood/ lack of hunger. Vacation tomorrow!

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-30T13:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34556>

122 again, surprise! i am leaving town for a week and getting away from the scale will be a good thing; I realize I let it affect my moods.

Sleep was a little better but still not perfect. Today I will skip the 30 mg pregnenolone and see what that does.

Breakfast: bone broth, burger.

Dinner: greek salad with minimal amount of chicken breast; best I could do! 1.5 glasses of mediocre white wine, 1.5 squares 99% chocolate. Maybe 4 oz pork belly cause I was hungry.

### vkiernan — 2012-06-30T15:03:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34565>

Hi Hilltower. Just curious, why do you weigh yourself everyday anyway? At 122, I don't see where losing weight is a good thing. I weigh about what you weigh and I actually want to gain. But, I want to gain muscle not fat of course. Someday I want to weigh at least 130 but have a rockin' bod. I have a friend who is a year older than me (50) and she is my height and outweighs me by 13 lbs or so and is for sure a size smaller. My thinking is to not weigh myself but rather use my pants as a guide. They should become too big if you are on track. If they start getting tight you need to get tested, if you have not, to see what really is up inside. Throw your scale away or at least put it in the basement or somewhere not easily accessible. Anyway, I hope you have a great weekend.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-30T16:18:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34577>

Looking good naked is all about body comp which is all about your hormone status............your scale contains none of that vital info..........but it will increase your cortisol and make you look like \*\*\*\* when your naked. My PSA for the entire thread.

### Hilltowner — 2012-06-30T16:51:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-quest-to-look-good-naked.2016/post-34588>

Hi guys--

Vkiernan- Just a habit I guess; I am certainly not the only one doing that on this forum. I know the range I am happy with considering body comp is 113 to 120, because I've been there before back in my LHT days. I am only 5'2" and small framed. I DO use my pants as a guide. I would look horrible at 130, sorry!

Completely agree on the cortisol and hormone testing, and this is on my to-do list after my week away. I think I am going to switch to a functional medicine dr just so I don't have to fight my PCP, who doesn't know me in any case since I never go in.

ETA: CT last night for about 20 minutes. I did not check the temp, but we have well water so it was fairly cool.

# Thread: Re-reading archived blogs
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-reading-archived-blogs.2020/>
**Subforum:** The Leptin Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### PrimalPam — 2012-06-24T17:03:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-reading-archived-blogs.2020/post-33276>

I found Dr. Kruse's site in December 2011, so I had a lot of catching up to do then. Basically, I skimmed the blogs and put myself on the LR on Jan. 1, 2012. Since then, I've kept up with every blog, but like many of you, it takes me many reads to get through just one.

Recently, I've started back from the beginning, in June 2011, reading the archives. Wow...there are so many eye-openers there I can't even begin. It's like lightbulbs flashing off for me. Many are in the comments, and Dr.'s responses. It's like...oh yeah, I remember reading this, but NOW it starts to all make sense.

Now I get why he's laid the blog posts out the way he has. Pieces of the puzzle...

Thanks SO much Dr. Kruse!

### chocolate — 2012-06-24T17:15:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-reading-archived-blogs.2020/post-33277>

I've started rereading also... I'm not doing well with the direct searches. It helps.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-24T17:47:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/re-reading-archived-blogs.2020/post-33283>

This is why I decided to use a Quilt format.......one piece at a time to give you parts of the optimal story.....as you assimilate pieces your bits of knowledge coalesce into wisdom of how you get to your inner guru.

I strongly suggest you re read them......and the comments. There is gold in the comments. Just look at BG 1 and BG2. The comments there already are eye opening.

# Thread: Dry Cracked Heels with CT
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### curves64 — 2012-06-24T17:22:19+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33279>

Has anyone else experienced dry cracked heels on their feet as a result of doing CT? Every time I go for an ocean swim (or even when I used to soak in the tub) they get worse and, frankly, it's painful. I might have read on the blog somewhere that dry skin is a result of an Omega 6/3 imbalance but I'm wondering if anyone else has any ideas.

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-24T17:50:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33286>

Result of lots of stuff......mg deficency is up there too.

### shilohman — 2012-06-24T17:52:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33287>

When I started CT my skin was very dry. It means you still have a high omega 6/3 ratio. I used a lot of coconut oil on my face and upper body and on my feel and heels I would rub oregano oil. My skin on my feet/heels is very soft now. I also supplemented with Krill oil. It didn't take long before I saw results.

Its like everything else, it just takes time.

### curves64 — 2012-06-24T18:11:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33290>

> **Jack Kruse;33421 wrote:** Result of lots of stuff......mg deficency is up there too.

Thank you. I am supplementing with mg malate before bed but maybe I'll up my dosage.

### curves64 — 2012-06-24T18:16:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33294>

> **shilohman;33422 wrote:** When I started CT my skin was very dry. It means you still have a high omega 6/3 ratio. I used a lot of coconut oil on my face and upper body and on my feel and heels I would rub oregano oil. My skin on my feet/heels is very soft now. I also supplemented with Krill oil. It didn't take long before I saw results.
>
>
>
> Its like everything else, it just takes time.

Thanks shilohman. The rest of my body is super soft. Even my elbows are soft and silky. That's why I was so puzzled by the bottoms of my feet. The left heel has deep fissures and I even have trouble walking on it. Coconut oil only relieves it slightly. I no longer eat a lot of O6 foods and eat salmon everyday. I'm going to try the oregano oil and go back to supplementing with fish oil. You are probably right, I just need more time to bring it all into balance.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-24T19:02:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33309>

My feet do this every winter..... the heels get all cracked and every spring it goes away and I have the nicest feet for the summer

### janagram — 2012-06-24T19:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33315>

I have this foot thing too....grrr! Sure would be nice to get rid of it! Could it be a sign of low thyroid? (is there a symptom: dry cracking skin...)

### Melinda — 2012-06-24T21:44:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33336>

My husband has this problem much more than I do. I just use regular shea butter like lotions and use one of those abrading tools to smooth them. My husband has to use "Podiatrist's Secret Total Foot Recovery Cream" It really helps him heal the fissures and then do a mild abrading get them smooth.

### Paul N — 2012-06-24T23:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33351>

When I was a kid, I had severe cracked heels and a doctor prescribed an ointment called "calmurid cream" - you can probably still get it. It worked a treat.

The active ingredient in the cream is - lactic acid!

You can get the same result by applying plain kefir (the yogurt-like milk product) to your skin, at night - works for my chapped hands. In fact, the historical "milk bath" used by Cleopatra, etc, was actually a kefir bath. Read everything you ever wanted to know about kefir at Dom's kefir site.

here is an interesting post by an old sailor about cold treatment, and he found that by soaking his feet in ice water, or cold ocean water, every day, his skin was greatly improved. His theory is that the cold drives the blood to the area to warm it up - I think this is what Jack has said too.

[http://www.aloha.com/~craven/hams4.html](http://http://www.aloha.com/~craven/hams4.html)

I'm sure the mg and other sea salts help too.

Presumably, the wrinkled effect from a hot tub is having the opposite effect - when your skin is white, and it is after a hot tub, there is not much blood there.

Unfortunately, the cold makes them worse. In fact, there is no dry skin at all on the rest of my body. I've coconut oiled them and scrubbed them with a pumice stone but they remain. If I don't CT for a while and they start to heal, a jump in the ocean makes them worse again. It's bizarre. I'll look for the calmurid cream too.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-25T00:50:04+0300

I use Burt's Bee's coconut foot cream. My heels are soft and smooth now... actually CT healed my dry heels, but using the foot cream right after CT helped too.

### curves64 — 2012-06-25T00:56:36+0300

### villjamur_stevenson — 2012-06-25T16:10:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33434>

It is funny that an earlier poster says it happens in the winter for them.

For me, it is strictly the summer. I get these large dry skin cracks on the backs of my heels every summer, and usually one of them gets painful enough, it impedes walking. I've always blamed this on wearing teva sandals in the summer, and that the constant contact with the rubber was doing something. If I start coconut oiling them up, treating the cuts with iodine, and wearing socks with shoes, they usually clear right up.

### curves64 — 2012-06-25T16:37:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33438>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;33570 wrote:** It is funny that an earlier poster says it happens in the winter for them.
>
> For me, it is strictly the summer. I get these large dry skin cracks on the backs of my heels every summer, and usually one of them gets painful enough, it impedes walking. I've always blamed this on wearing teva sandals in the summer, and that the constant contact with the rubber was doing something. If I start coconut oiling them up, treating the cuts with iodine, and wearing socks with shoes, they usually clear right up.

It hurts right?! I was thinking last night that maybe it's my body signaling me that there is some vitamin/mineral deficiency I should investigate. Dr. K. suggested maybe it's mag. After reading the iodine thread I am probably low in iodine. And here you go and mention iodine. Coincidence? IDK

### N~Doro — 2012-06-25T17:12:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33447>

> **curves64;33574 wrote:** It hurts right?! I was thinking last night that maybe it's my body signaling me that there is some vitamin/mineral deficiency I should investigate. Dr. K. suggested maybe it's mag. After reading the iodine thread I am probably low in iodine. And here you go and mention iodine. Coincidence? IDK

Just my experience, but I used to have thick heel callouses that I could never make any progress on with creams or abrasive methods. I found out my B12 was abysmal(139) so started to supplement heavily and my feet are now soft and smooth. After this experience I read that thick heal skin can be a sign of pre-diabetes (I think it was in Catherine Shanahan's _Deep Nutrition_) so I am not sure if the callouses went away from the B12 or because I had changed to Primal 6 months prior. Perhaps the reduced insulin exposure finally healed them. As Dr. K said, it could be one of any number of things causing it. I do remember being pretty surprised to have them go away like that since I always assumed it was from shoes/walking. Anyway, my point being good luck figuring out the cause, but don't assume it is just from the CT (obvious as the connection may appear). I continue to be amazed at what we can change with nutrition.

### youthfuliz — 2012-06-25T17:44:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33456>

> **N~Doro;33583 wrote: Just my experience, but I used to have thick heel callouses that I could never make any progress on with creams or abrasive methods. I found out my B12 was abysmal(139) so started to supplement heavily and my feet are now soft and smooth. After this experience I read that thick heal skin can be a sign of pre-diabetes (I think it was in Catherine Shanahan's _Deep Nutrition_) so I can not be sure if the callouses went away from the B12 or because I had changed to Primal 6 months prior. Perhaps the reduced insulin exposure finally healed them. As Dr. K said, it could be one of any number of things causing it. I do remember being pretty surprised to have them go away like that since I always assumed it was from shoes/walking. Anyway, my point being good luck figuring out the cause, but don't assume it is just from the CT (obvious as the connection may appear). I continue to be amazed at what we can change with nutrition.**

You're right. It could be the supplements I'm taking that helped with my dry heels and dry elbow. I always had dry heels since when I can remember. It always took a lot of (daily) preparation for me to go out in sandals in summer. And my left elbow became dry and calloused in the last several years... the right elbow has always been fine, only the left one. Since I started CT... both the feet and elbow have become soft and smooth... but of course I have been doing the leptin reset, and have been taking supplements that I wasn't before. And I used some other foot cream before. So it could have been something else. My skin allover has become better, supple and elastic. Silky smooth to the touch. Sorry I can't stop boasting.  Fine lines dissapearing. I feel 5 years younger. I always say it's because of CT, but it's true that I'm improving my eating, sleeping better, and taking supplements too. So those are important too.

### Souldanzer — 2012-06-25T19:52:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-cracked-heels-with-ct.2021/post-33477>

> **villjamur\_stevenson;33570 wrote:** It is funny that an earlier poster says it happens in the winter for them.
>
> For me, it is strictly the summer. I get these large dry skin cracks on the backs of my heels every summer, and usually one of them gets painful enough, it impedes walking. I've always blamed this on wearing teva sandals in the summer, and that the constant contact with the rubber was doing something. If I start coconut oiling them up, treating the cuts with iodine, and wearing socks with shoes, they usually clear right up.

I used to blame it on abrasion.... in the summer I barely ever wear shoes. I even garden barefoot  Sometimes I wear vibrams. But I'm not so sure about this any longer since I'm barefooted much of the winter as well, just not outside so much. Now I'm more wondering about seasonal effects, possibly in my nutrition. I'm very curious to see if it will stay away this coming winter.

Our new undenatured Bioactive Whey Protein Powder is as close as you'll ever come to pure native protein in powdered form. It comes from **certified organic, grass-fed cows** guaranteed to be free of rGBH and antibiotics. Plus, it's hormone-free! This is the best whey protein powder for optimum wellness and defense, as it is designed to help fuel your body's ability to produce glutathione, the master molecule. In addition to its well-known antioxidant properties (it's one of the body's most important intracellular antioxidants), glutathione is a master detoxifier that is present in every cell in the body and in all bodily fluids. >
> [http://www.amazon.com/Source-Naturals-True-Whey-Ounce/dp/B001G7R4HI/ref=sr\_1\_fkmr0\_1\_sns?m=ATVPDKIKX0DER&ie=UTF8&qid=1340675319&sr=8-1-fkmr0](http://http://www.amazon.com/Source-Naturals-True-Whey-Ounce/dp/B001G7R4HI/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_1_sns?m=ATVPDKIKX0DER&ie=UTF8&qid=1340675319&sr=8-1-fkmr0)
>
>
>
> How is the taste?

I like it. It's really mild vanilla with only the slightest hint of stevia (it's BARELY sweet and there's no aftertaste). It mixes well with other things but isn't bad on its own, either.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/undenatured-bioactive-whey-protein.2029/post-223729>

Hello!

For my BAB as a last resort I want to stock a protein powder. Any recommendations on a brand without hemp, lectins (ie. pea, brown rice), or hydrolyzed/denatured whey? I may get the bulletproof one but not sure what to think of the addition of colostrum. If whey, would also need to be grass fed.

What caused the whey powder at top of post to have a high glutamate content? The denaturing?

Lastly, what are thoughts on isolating collagen in all these powders today? What would make them safe if anything? Is it possible to have a zero risk of glutamate? I know whole, bone broth best but again looking to stock for a last resort.

Thanks!

### Christina Gagnon — 2019-07-09T01:53:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/undenatured-bioactive-whey-protein.2029/post-273887>

[https://www.amazon.com/Grass-Fed-Fr...ey&qid=1562626180&s=gateway&sr=8-1-fkmr0&th=1](https://www.amazon.com/Grass-Fed-Friendly-Non-Denatured-GMO-Free-Concentrate/dp/B00QWKX24S/ref=sr_1_fkmr0_1?keywords=wild%2Bwheybulletproof%2Bwhey&qid=1562626180&s=gateway&sr=8-1-fkmr0&th=1)

Wild Whey is what I have been usingg - it's from grass fed cows so think it's OK.

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=33384)
>
> not from grass fed cows.........and has free glutamate in it..........pass.

### Christina Gagnon — 2019-07-09T13:53:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/undenatured-bioactive-whey-protein.2029/post-273909>

> [finnite@dccnet.com said:](/goto/post?id=33681)
>
> darn, wish I'd seen this before ordering the immunoPro, oh well, once I've finished the immunoPro

Do you have any tips for thickening a smoothie? I have been using a banana but have to stop doing that.

### ElectricUniverse — 2019-07-09T19:05:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/undenatured-bioactive-whey-protein.2029/post-273930>

> [janagram said:](/goto/post?id=33703)
>
> How do you use whey? Smoothies, I imagine. Are they meal supplements? Or an after brfst thing?

Most product directions will usually say take in water or cold beverages (heat will deactivate some of the vital peptides).

Avoid using with acidic foods, or enzyme rich foods like pineapple and papaya for same reason.

To get most benefit out of undenatured whey consume it on an empty stomach before meals (this enhances absorption of glutathione precursors).

It is my current understanding (correct me if I am wrong) that any true cold-processed undenatured whey will not have much if any of d-glutamic acid (the 'bad' form of glutamic acid-- the 'good' form is l-glutamic acid). On the other hand, other processed protein (hydrolyzed and so on) protein powders on market will be reeking with d-glutamic acid.

D-glutamate is also present in certain foods e.g., soybeans and also arises from the turnover of the intestinal tract microflora, whose cell walls contain significant D-glutamate (source: HMDB).

### Shijin13 — 2012-06-25T15:53:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/does-anyone-in-california-like-their-health-insurance.2030/post-33428>

I think your satisifcation w/you health insurance is a result of what you and doctor can figure how to leverage the plan you have. I've got UHC - while not great I've emailed them and asked how to get labs paid for - and they've actually given me my DX codes to get everything paid for...

like everything thing there's a system, if you can figure out how to make it work to your benefit... great. Are there things UHC won't pay for - yeah; but that's a given considering my deductible...

### chocolate — 2012-06-26T05:05:17+0300

It would be easier if we didn't have to justify every move.

# Thread: Hello Everyone. :)
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.2032/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mackay — 2012-06-25T15:09:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.2032/post-33424>

Hello,

My name is Mackay Rippey. I'm an acupuncturist living in Way Up-State New York. My style of acupuncture, 5-element, places a great emphasis on following nature's laws and understanding how we work with or against nature. So Dr. Kruse's work immediately resonated with me.

I grew up in Washington, DC and lived all my life in cities. That all changed when we moved to an old farm in Deansboro. The prize of the farm is a small herd of American Milking Devons. They are wonderfly gentle triple purpose (work, milk, beef) cows that were the first breed to come to America with the Pilgrims. Milking Devons can be seen at the Cooperstown Farmer's Museum, Williamsburg, Virginia, and other historical re-enactment sites.

Anywho. Great to be part of this grand experiment.

### nonchalant — 2012-06-26T00:33:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-everyone.2032/post-33541>

Welcome to the forum, Mackay. Glad to have you here with us.

Welcome Mackay to this great site!

### Jack Kruse — 2012-06-26T05:56:47+0300

### Mackay — 2012-06-27T03:09:47+0300

>
> Concentration of lipoproteins is referred to as quantity and
>

>
> is not that important**. Put another way without concentration data, particle size cannot help you.**

# Thread: Video - me eating high meat. So good for our gut!

**Subforum:** The Epi-Paleo Diet

### Inger — 2012-06-25T17:24:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-me-eating-high-meat-so-good-for-our-gut.2036/post-33450>

I did this video today, to show how I make my high meat and how it looks.

I believe this is very beneficial for our guts, a lot of good bakteria. It is cheap and easy to make, a old remedy in old cultures like Siberian.

I made it from grassfed beef-heart this time, it is about 4 months aged.

I hope this could be helpful for the ones who suffer from gut dysbiosis in this forum.

\[video\]\[video=youtube\_share;rDfBW93BhLY\]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=rDfBW93BhLY\[/video\]\[/video\]

Inger

### janagram — 2012-06-25T19:12:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-me-eating-high-meat-so-good-for-our-gut.2036/post-33469>

It's interesting to revive these old remedies! The ancient Siberian culture sounds really fascinating!

You are way ahead of the rest of us in actually trying stuff. I just don't think I will ever do this!

(Also, have you really fully explained how to make it? Just put meat in a jar for 4 mo.?)

### Inger — 2012-06-25T20:13:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/video-me-eating-high-meat-so-good-for-our-gut.2036/post-33480>

Janagram, if wanting to optimize my health, I need to do stuff that helps the most. I believe this is better than any probiotics you can buy for money. After reading Jacks last two blogposts I got just more convinced it must be great. I feel great too, when eating it, and I have done this on and off for years.

It is so easy, just take piece of fresh (not prefrozen) meat or heart, cut in pieces, and fill a glass jar max 2/3 full, there needs to be enough air circulating. Then put the jar in the fridge, and open it for a few minutes a few times / week to air it. Let it stay for months or years...
