Thanks for your reply. I did CT for a few months, then had to move and no longer have access to a tub. And I basically quit it because I couldn't do it consistenly. I plan on picking it back up here in the fall. I work construction, so despite having 0 energy, I'm on my feet all day. I refuse to go on any anti-depressents of any sort. I'm far from depressed. I just have no energy, always have a thick brain fog, am ALWAYS COLD (hands and feet), and can't sleep. I follow the circadian rhythms for the past 4 months as well, and have no problem getting to sleep around 10:00. It's just that I wake to pee around 12:30 or so, and have a hard time getting back to sleep, and no matter what I always wake up wide awake at 4:00 each morning. I eventually go and make breakfast around 4:30, then can fall back asleep for another hour after I eat. It's a horrible sleeping pattern that I have been dealing with forever

### JanSz — 2012-07-13T21:51:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/please-help-me-with-my-labs.2395/post-37576>

It is possible to do blood tests at LabCorp without doctor's script.

One have to pay cash but the prices are really competitive.

Specially when one have to pay cash for doctor's visit first,

and there is still no guarantee that doctor will write script that one wants.

Going by your ZRT tests, I suggest that your most important test would be PSA.

http://www.privatemdlabs.com/lab\_tests.php?view=all

eat all the supplemental fats mentioned there.

Remember, you are eating lots of shrimps, then fish (and do not supplement with any Krill or Fish oils).

I assume, the smaller the animal, lower on food chain, less pollutants, hence, smaller animal is better.

Dried shrimp 1pound=4pounds of fresh

4oz of dry=1pound fresh

My latest purchase in Asian store here:

http://img155.imageshack.us/img155/7491/image235b.jpg

...............

Life Extension DHEA Dehydroepiandrosterone 50 Mg, Capsules, 60-Count

http://www.amazon.com/Life-Extension-Dehydroepiandrosterone-Capsules-60-Count/dp/B000GTBLW6/ref=sr\_1\_2?ie=UTF8&qid=1342200422&sr=8-2&keywords=dhea+50+mg

...............

Life Extension

Super Selenium Complex, 200 Mcg 100 Caps

http://www.amazon.com/Super-Selenium-Complex-200-Caps/dp/B000X9QZPC/ref=sr\_1\_4?s=hpc&ie=UTF8&qid=1342205006&sr=1-4&keywords=Super+Selenium+Complex%2C+200+mcg

\=====================================

1/wakeup---Pregnenolone 150 mg

1/wakeup---7-Keto 100mg

1/wakeup---DHEA 50 Mg

weak latter add

1/morning---Thyroid-S

two weeks latter add

1/latter in the day----Thyroid-S

2/day----Super Selenium Complex

week after starting selenium add

1dropperfull/day Lugol's (I add to coffe)

This is only a try, if it makes you feel better, keep using it otherwise stop.

Stop and wait for a time when you can do better blood testing.

I am afraid that DHEA may make your acne worst. But there is no guarantee, so try, evaluate.

\=============================

You mostly do not have any testosterone.

Have you been hit in testicles or head, specially where top of your nose meets forehead?

To supplement with testosterone you need doctor's attention.

Plus

you have a head winds,

high PSA and unknown hemoglobin level.

I sympathize with your high PSA level.

Makes people think about prostate cancer, but it may be "just" enlarged prostate.

This is what I faced when I got my low testosterone news.

In the mean time make your own sourcraut and eat it raw, uncooked, also brocoli.

My doctor's web page

tristate-health.com

he takes some insurances, not all.

\==============================

> Symptoms-Can't sleep more than 4 or so hours a night, always cold, absolutely no energy or sex drive at all, bad cystic acne, depression, horrible brain fog, very forgetful, no motivation to do anything, etc etc etc

pregnenolone (above) should raise your cortisol.br />
when you get up, make sure that you are surrounded by the brightest lights possible

if day is sunny, look at the sun (exposure your body, look just under the sun).

That may raise cortisol by 30%

Right away, when you get up, preferably within 0.5-1 hrs, eat big breakfast, that should be 50% of what you eat per day.

Do not eat 4hr before bed time.

No lights, or just very very low light, last 4hrs before bed.

# Thread: Post your very best thought.....here.  Ponder and think before you jump in
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-13T06:43:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-37422>

When you say you understand someone of something you must realize it is far greater than any knowledge that you claim to have. There are many people who know us.........but very few who understand what makes us tick. Thoughts become things for us as we live our life. All of life is in your mind whether you realize it or not today. And your mind is more powerful than we all think. Begin to build your un lived life today. Inspiration is perishable but wisdom is not.

### johnnyb — 2012-07-13T07:26:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-37428>

epigenetics

the ancient pathway reopen

new thoughts bring new life

### Huck — 2012-07-13T07:37:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-37431>

We never truly know anyone. We can only know a person's behavior (actions).

While behavior is a fairly good representation of a person, it doesn't tell us everything a person thinks and feels. It doesn't tell us the total of his/her life experiences. So we fill in the holes based on our own life experiences and often fill in the holes with wrong information.

A question I have been wondering about recently is this. Does our memory store and associate hormone levels, emotions, etc with hard information? For example, if I struggle to learn a calculus problem, does the anxiousness somehow get stored along with calculus? If it causes an increase in blood pressure or heart rate, does that get stored? I'm sure the answer is yes for emotions, but just how much information gets stored? Does the brain have sensory receptors for these functions and how are they wired to memory? I assume they look like vision and auditory inputs to the brain and then axons/synapses connect all the related pieces of information together.

The reason I was thinking about this is because I am interested in how learning actually happens. It seems to me that a great teacher should go beyond visual, auditory, and touch, and include emotion.

### kathylu — 2012-07-13T16:48:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-37477>

One of my favorite quotes from Abraham Lincoln, December 1862:

"The dogmas of the quiet past, are inadequate to the stormy present. The occasion is piled high with difficulty, and we must rise -- with the occasion. As our case is new, so we must think anew, and act anew. We must disenthrall ourselves, and then we shall save our country."

Of course, he was speaking about the war and ending slavery, but I think it could certainly be applied to many modern day situations. Just think about these words applied to CW in healthcare! We must think anew and act anew. We must disenthrall ourselves (to liberate; free from bondage), and then we shall save our health and that of our country....the dogmas of the past are inadequate to the stormy present.

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-07-14T19:37:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-37801>

You create the purpose of your life with a singular organ - the brain. Nurture it and give it freedom to explore new ways of thinking.

### WannaBeOptimal — 2012-07-16T01:46:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-38187>

Internalizing what I have read here has created a burden.

I see sick and broken people...everywhere. I have this visceral reaction to reach out to them-to convey to them what I have learned. It is painful to see them walk away, probably to a future that is much less than what it could be. Being one of them, and then backing away from that ledge, has made me hyper-sensitive to their state.

As my family, friends, and coworkers know what I am doing (I have laid it all out), it is vital to me to be an effective role model...to finish this, to live this like nothing I have ever done. The responsibilty is great because to stakes are enormous.

### nonchalant — 2012-07-26T23:00:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-41330>

We seek to lose weight, to look good.

Then we find that we can get relief from chronic sickness, and prevent future illnesses.

And we want that for us and our families and children, and future grandchildren.

Sleep! Yes, we can sleep all the way through the night at last!

Along the way we are surprised to find increased strength and flexibility.

Then we realize how important mental clarity and good memory is, and oh yes, we need that too.

Then we get a taste of the energy, the focus, the passion and joy of excellent nutrition -- Wow, we can have that too?

Meanwhile, Dr. Kruse continues patiently to explain how it is all connected, it is all the same solution. The body and the mind are one.

### tercay@yahoo.com — 2012-07-27T01:03:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/post-your-very-best-thought-here-ponder-and-think-before-you-jump-in.2398/post-41373>

### janagram — 2012-07-13T09:10:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37439>

OK I think I need to journal my path because I will have a record of what really happens in a day...

Today I had: Brkfst, 2 eggs, scrambled in butter., with crispy fried greens, coffee with cream and green tea.

Lunch: salad with about a 1 inch cube of steak (leftover),

Dinner in a restaurant: spinach salad with Prawns, 2 glasses malbec. Then one more glass of wine plus half a cup of popcorn, (bad) and a dark chocolate bar at the poetry group.

Home late, got to wind down....get some sleep.

### janagram — 2012-07-13T18:52:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37507>

Did I say sleep? WTF? couldn't sleep!!!! I have the horrible feeling it was a case of undigested prawns...I never eat them, but am trying to....and didn't take enzymes. Anyway I just popped awake at midnight and stayed awake till about 4:30....will have to nap today. I wonder if you can take enzymes hours later on a so-called empty stomach? Or if it will eat your insides up?

### janagram — 2012-07-14T05:48:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37697>

Trying to tighten up the diet, right? (argh)

so, today I had 2 scrambled eggs and 1 small cup of coffee with HWC, then a cup of green tea with ginger.

Lunch was a smallish tomato, sliced, with a whole avocado, some feta, some lettuce, onion, 2 sl. bacon, and homemade dressing. Yummmm.

Dinner was about a 3 oz pc of wild king salmon with curried onion, garlic, ginger, tomato.....so yum. And cauli/parsnip mash. Over the top good.

Exercize was minimal, rode my bike around for 15 min., parked far and walked to whole foods and shopped. sunbathed. Napped, since last night was so bad. (I know , its a rough life!)

### Rosekelly — 2012-07-14T07:02:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37703>

You eat so little food! And no coconut oil? I eat about 8 tbsp per day. And I'm down 43 pounds. Just sayin. I live and breathe coconut oil. I slather it alllll over my body too. Dab it in my hair. Add coconut milk to my tea. Surprised I don't have monkeys climbing up me.

### janagram — 2012-07-14T18:29:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37780>

ha ha Rose! I must be lying! Cuz I don't feel like I eat so little food. I slather the kerrygold butter on everything. Oh, here's the lie, I eat a handful of mac nuts after lunch and sometimes dinner.

### janagram — 2012-07-14T18:33:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37782>

I'm seeing Dr. Ann Haiden, a D.O. who practises functional medicine. She is helping me get and interpret labs, etc. So far, so good. No,she's not on board with either Paleo or Dr. K., but she agrees that I don't do well with carbs or grains, so that works for me, for now.

But my labs showed all this inflammation, as well as nasty trigs....apparently I'm kidding myself about eating right...I need to buckle down now and not report eating popcorn....no, I mean not EAT popcorn!!!!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-14T19:08:40+0300

### janagram — 2012-07-14T22:14:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-37828>

I was eating the BAB but then I got tired of it... I'm fine on a medium ass breakfast, I can wait till lunch. in fact its working better for me cuz I then eat an earlier lunch and earlier dinner. Today I had bacon and eggs (2) over mashed parsnip/cauliflower. (1 floret, 1 parsnip)...but I'll consider what yr saying....I realize I'm probably LR still...my biggest prob, besides my labs, is my sleep!

While its true I eat paleo, low carb, i tend to write off the occasional ice cream, glutenfree cookie, etc......(as in, "doesn't everyone?") but I'm seeing, by my labs, that I cannot take breaks! I have inflammation and CRP and whatnot to reverse!

Labs I'm worried about:

LDL particle number-1814 (high)

Triglycerides-192 (high)

small LDL-p--619 (high)

C-reactive protein--3.61 (high)

feedback, anyone?

### janagram — 2012-07-15T18:43:02+0300

Are your trigs really 192? This is really bad if I am reading it correctly. From what I know, high trigs is the result of eating a lot of carbs. Trigs should be around 50 or lower iirc. ### janagram — 2012-07-16T21:43:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38457>

wow...I too am amazed. who knew I could be so off base. I guess the trigs are really bad...my test says less than 150 is where you wanna be and I'm 192! Carbs, what carbs? I guess denial is not just a river, etc. I used to get an ice cream cone about once a wk, but certainly no breads or pasta or ....well, at parties I'd have the rice crackers with tons of brie....etc. Or is it the few french fries I'd sneak off my DH's plate in restaurants? Or ?

It kinda freaks me out cuz I do eat so much fat, like everyone on here.....is it really OK? ....Vkiernan, thanks for commenting!

### janagram — 2012-07-16T23:20:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38503>

well it may not be hscarp, tho. Just plain CRP. But anyway....I've been on one kind of paleo or another for over a yr. I ate more carbs last summer/fall thn I do now...sweet potato every day, but still paleo. Then I started LR around Mar. Haven't had ccortisol or hormones done yet...have been working on sleep. Its better...and that gives me hope. Also my stool test showed no problems, which made me feel good. But the trigs and inflammation...? sigh....I'm just a bit deflated right now. You know how you get on a program and you are so hopeful? well....what next? where to turn? I'm not having my beloved ice cream that's for sure. (favorite cheaat)

And I really thank you. It's great to get feedback.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-17T01:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38544>

Don't get discouraged. It is all in finding out and then making changes that get you there. You cannot fix what you don't know. We all have had our eyes opened believe me! Just keep learning and doing the right things then all in good time good things will happen.

Yes fat is good for you, but maybe you are eating the wrong kind. Could you give us an idea of what you eat? Also, some changes take a long time to make good results. We might have to be more patient in our outcomes desired.

### janagram — 2012-07-17T01:25:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38552>

### janagram — 2012-07-17T17:14:16+0300

Ok, last night was my night to go over the hill to SCruz to do inquiry with 4 other friends...we do it once a wk and it has been hard to get a proper dinner in cuz we meet at 6 and my drive is 45 min. So, last night I was prepared! I brought a salad with a couple slices of steak in a glass refrigerator container with snap top. I can't leave a thing to chance! (I used to get egg salad at the health foods store...but what kind of mayo do they use? Gotta watch those seed oils!)

So yesterday's food was as close to ideal as I cd get without eating mackerel head smoothies!

### kathiebu@hotmail.com — 2012-07-17T19:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38770>

### janagram — 2012-07-18T09:10:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38960>

ok...the book club! Its always a dinner. Oh my! It was pasta and shrimp. I ate shrimp and pushed the pasta around the plate, and ate a paperthin slice of cake after....this is soo hard. I guess my big hurdle is my social life . How can I sit down at the table and say "I don't eat this"? Ok, I know the answer to that....I have to be ready to just not have the sliver of cake. I know it wasn't a good choice but it could have been worse...so much worse. I feel like letting it be and just getting back on strict ketopaleo tomorrow.

I took a lukewarm to cold shower, croak-style, and am icing my belly as I write.

### vkiernan — 2012-07-18T14:26:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-38970>

> **janagram;39140 wrote:** ok...the book club! Its always a dinner. Oh my! It was pasta and shrimp. I ate shrimp and pushed the pasta around the plate, and ate a paperthin slice of cake after....this is soo hard. I guess my big hurdle is my social life . How can I sit down at the table and say "I don't eat this"? Ok, I know the answer to that....I have to be ready to just not have the sliver of cake. I know it wasn't a good choice but it could have been worse...so much worse. I feel like letting it be and just getting back on strict ketopaleo tomorrow.
>
> I took a lukewarm to cold shower, croak-style, and am icing my belly as I write.

Yep, if you cheat you have to enjoy it and not stress as this only raises your cortisol and we both know that is not good. Pick yourself up, dust off and start a new day. Just keep doing this everyday and pretty soon you will figure out how to avoid those situations and keep making forward progress. You did push the pasta away and that was very good. I eventually had to tell the people why I can't eat those things and if I tell them it spikes my blood sugar and further stresses my adrenals, they seem to get it. Then they all make sure I have enough to eat. They actually take a sympathetic tone to the situation. You could go with the bs thing and not worry about it. Or whatever works. Perhaps you bring a big salad and eat the meat and your salad. I try to even take my own salad dressings to restaurants cuz they will feed you crap if you don't. I make mine with macademia nut oil and pear infused balsamic vinegar with some spices. It is sooo good. Just put in a little container for 1 serving and take.

### janagram — 2012-07-18T19:11:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-39067>

Oh my, your dressing does sound good!! I really need to do that! And thank you for your supportive words. I know you have been down this journey and you have some great strategies!

Weighed this morning, (I weigh everyday...I know not everyone goes for this) weight was unchanged!! Ok, onward and upward!

### janagram — 2012-07-19T06:48:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-39296>

We ate well, having gorgeous appetizers in place of dinner:

bison sausages, sliced in coins eaten with toothpicks and mustard, fried padron peppers (these are uncommon, we grow them, they are sooo good, sauteed in EVOO with sea salt!) olives, brie, and homemade wine rounded it off. (I made a point of having 1 oz of salmon as well-I had some leftover)

Glad I did after reading the new blog....may have to try sushi....

### janagram — 2012-07-23T19:44:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-40417>

OK, last night was a horrible night, sleep-wise! After a wonderful night out at a restaurant overlooking the ocean with DH, sons, granddaughter, and a fish dinner, I couldn't sleep!!! but No Sleep is upside down circadian rhythms....

### janagram — 2012-07-26T01:33:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-41110>

interesting...didn't take kelp and sleep was good last night....wore blublocker glasses to read in bed, no alcohol...(uh...could that be it?)

### janagram — 2012-07-26T18:46:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-41225>

hmmm, sleep was poor last night...tho' I iced my belly and neck in bed while reading by orange light..plus no wine. No leptins for you! Guess I'm not LS.....had salmon that DH grilled in foil, but definitely overcooked. Had kale and swiss chard from my yard as a side, also 2 slices sauteed eggplant in garlic butter...mmm.

I am seriously looking at Jack's advise to stop resisting foods you "don't like"....but desire to make those foods taste good. There is a limit, of course. Inger's cocktail is never gonna taste good. Y'all just keep drinkin' it! But I personally have a need for food to be enjoyable....not necessarily "entertainment"...but a sensual pleasure, as I believe it is intended to be. (sometimes food is medicine, I can accept that)...this is a continuing inquiry for me....and I am open to learning that I am off base here.

### janagram — 2012-07-27T20:41:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-41565>

Poor sleep again....I just lie there, tired as H\*\*\*, going-"what the-?" ...but really, I'm not that reactive for the most part, I'm just tired. I don't want to give the picture of a stress-monster, cuz I'm not. I do pray and meditate...(I love my life, now that I am retired...I just wish my sleep were better) and I do not wake up refreshed! and I need coffee to wake up. I keep coffee to a minimum ...one mug, but am now hooked. Should go off the stuff...

### janagram — 2012-07-30T17:24:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-41942>

took a marvelous hike with DH, son, and granddaughter in a beautiful open space preserve yesterday! More exercize than I'd had in a long time....so thought my sleep would be better, but no. Still a typical 5 hrs. Or less, hard to be sure when you actually woke up. But during the day I tend to feel fine. Last couple of days I have taken about a 30 min. nap which is really helpful.

today I'm getting paintings ready for jurying into a really big show....kind of scared, isn't that crazy?

Y'know how you (I) sometimes forget major symptoms and stuff? I should take note of the rotator cuff injury which I've had for half a year, and is healing well, but certain muscles are still just screwed...this is a contributor to my poor sleep (hard to feel really comfy) and maybe to my overall inflammation...eh?

Inger's cocktail is never gonna taste good. Y'all just keep drinkin' it! But I personally have a need for food to be enjoyable....not necessarily "entertainment"...but a sensual pleasure, as I believe it is intended to be. (sometimes food is medicine, I can accept that)...this is a continuing inquiry for me....and I am open to learning that I am off base here.

There does seem to be something in our "human-ness" that elevates food to more than simply fuel. It could be argued our social behaviors are a material component to humanity, and food has played a part in that in all cultures and social groups. I think it's a mistake to try and ignore that instead of work within that.

### janagram — 2012-07-31T09:15:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-42023>

Ah, yes. Thank you for commenting, Cavemam. I do believe that food can be enjoyable and healthful at the same time.

Hoping for good sleep tonite....I had some wine but was mindful. I won;t be surprised if my sleep is as bad as usual....cuz I'm becoming convinced that it is cortisol or hormones.....I do need testing.....will talk to my functional med. doc soon.

### janagram — 2012-07-31T18:10:07+0300

oooh, better sleep last night...I don't have any idea why. I did not eat anything out of line, tho' I had more than one glass of wine....but stretched out over hours. Musta been real tired! (was!) Ahhhh, sleep is so nice! (hm, last night's progesterone cream came out as an extra large dose, by gosh, now I recall, wonder if that was it. Also I never cycle it (I'm 65)...but maybe I should, they say to on the tube. I may start that, but I never want to try a night without it! I just stared a topical Estriol cream a couple weeks ago.

### janagram — 2012-08-28T20:53:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-47851>

Just want to map out my path...I am N-1-ing it now. Finding my rhythm. Right now it's a matter of finding what I can live with while at the same time approaching optimal. The comments on food and the social side of life really touched me. How to draw a line in the sand with the worst foods and not seem from Mars, yet gently influence others for the better. On my recent meditation retreat at Asilomar, I shared meals with friends at every meal. They already eat fairly well (oh really?)....but when out to dinner with them I would just sip my wine while they ate the bread that is served first....and still be able to order normally. I usually ordered the fish. In the mornings they made big smoothies for all. I was not able to do the whey...goes right thru me...but they would use my protein powder (pea and rice...protein only, no starch, but...in the market for a better one)...and use lots of antioxidant rich foods...spinach, celery, carrots, berries, etc......delish. What in the hell protein powder can I use? egg white? mmm, hemp? mmm...so so far smoothie is a doable brkfst that is my new thing....not sure what lunch is...want a kind of uni-lunch, y'know? almost a monodiet...tuna or eggsalad, maybe, over greens.... fish 5 nights a wk. I walk 2 mi. a day and do yoga. I call it "slouching towards optimal"! and I am loving it!

### Rosekelly — 2012-08-29T18:57:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-48108>

Slouching toward optimal. Haha!

You know, in times past, making smoothies for breakfast was considered a little weird and out there, so at some point your friends shifted their eating patterns to include them. Cutting out bread will become much more popular among the health-conscious in due time. You're just ahead of the pack. And your friends will remember that, hey, Jan did it. Think of yourself as an early adopter.

### janagram — 2012-09-22T06:20:15+0300

### jeanie@truelongmont.com — 2012-10-10T18:32:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-57345>

> **janagram;58565 wrote:** Hey, thanks, Cavemam! I'll have to go back and see what I might have missed. I notice folks talking about vegetables, herbs, etc.a bit more than usual, so just sayin...again, thanks.

Ahhh! Ok, sorry - here is my take on those:

1\. Resveretol = super good, take mucho if you can.

2\. Tumeric (curcumin) = super good, take mucho if you can.

3\. Herbs are good - cook with them! Fresh ones!

I know that is not detailed, but it is the crux of the message.

### janagram — 2012-10-11T00:55:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-57462>

A first in a longgggg time: I slept 8 hrs!!!!!!

### janagram — 2012-10-11T22:38:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-57655>

Sleep seems to be happening!! Love it!

Here's the deal: I just don't know what to do next. I have been on so many diets. (I sort of obsess on diet related stuff, which should not surprise anyone here- we probably all do!) I can't even bear to list all the diets I've been on, but let's just look at the life changing ones. The Zone. Barry Sears had me going there...I liked the philosophy. Philosophy anyone? (JK for ex.) Anything with an overarching theory......up my alley! the Zone is fairly hard to do....but I did it. I lost weight. 25 lbs. at least. Gained back after a year....(...same ol same ol)...his secret? precision. I was a vegetarian at the time and ironically he had no plan for a vegetarian back then, but does now, even says they lose faster! (I did...)

But now I believe in this way of eating. Epi-paleo...ok, I sort of do it. No MHS for me. But I eat fish almost every day, no grains, etc.

I feel good. Not losing any weight. Still trying to tweak. I really am taking BG 14 to heart. Just haven't found my way yet. Fingers crossed.

### janagram — 2012-10-19T18:43:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-59186>

Oh, y'know why the Zone worked well for me as a vegetarian? Cuz it made me eat more protein. Sure it was a lot of soy (!)...also eggs, cheese, (I used cottage cheese then...not so good on it now)....get it? That's how bad my diet was before the zone. Oh I could tell stories...adrenal fatigue to the max, asthma, running to the ER...but did I get it? Couldn't be my healthy vegetarian diet!

Next big game changer: Blood type diet! Now we're talking. (I'm type O, as are most people) Lots of meat. Few grains. (an improvement, a huge one, that healed my asthma and more)

Now I'm hoping that epipaleo can get me sleeping and thinner and stronger. Got to find my perfect N-1....

### janagram — 2012-10-19T18:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-59189>

I had never liked fish, having grown up in No. Dakota (ok, only till 8) but with a No. Dak. raised Mom, fish wasn't big in our house. I am very proud of having learned to actually like salmon and halibut. Took me quite some time. Still working on shrimp....scallops are sorta good. I'll get there...

Made the best sockeye salmon last night: Just fried it in olive oil with slices of ginger, garlic, and poured my "teriyaki sauce" over it toward the end of cooking time. Sauce: tamari (wheatfree), lemon juice, agave, about a T. of each! So awesome!

### DarleenMB — 2012-10-19T20:57:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-59214>

The zone also worked for me. 2 pounds a week for the 8 weeks I really stuck to eating 3 meals + zone snack a day. Kept it off for a good years or so. I just got tired of eating all the time.

for me, the LR has become less about focusing on losing weight and more about unearthing what is broken in me and getting it fixed. That, alone, has been worth the price of admission because I was a "diet" queen for a long time. No more.

### janagram — 2012-10-20T06:04:48+0300

Hmmm, I've really got to go buy some candy for tomorrow night! I always buy something yucky so I won't eat it. I consider hard candies, peppermint, etc. to be easy to forego. Or plain suckers. yuck.

### Endless — 2012-11-04T19:55:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62052>

I have my coconut manna and my 99% chocolate always on hand for my treat....I take a glob of manna and a square of chocolate and eat them together. I find that usually keeps me away from the other sweets. If I'm reaallly craving sugar I'll drink some kombucha.

Of course, I don't have a grandchild around wanting to bake....good luck!

### janagram — 2012-11-04T20:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62055>

That's the kicker...what could be more of a grandma thing than baking with the granddaughter? she was brought up SAD....very hard to get her on board with paleo baking, but not impossible. Sometimes I even go over to glutenfree, sugarfree baking (agave...)

### chocolate — 2012-11-04T20:14:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62057>

I don't know how to spell it, (my husband calls them horse douvers) are such an art form. That's getting a gal ready for the world. Little ginger meatballs, etc. I don't know what you could tin up and pass out for gifts, though. You have time to plan.

### janagram — 2012-11-05T02:03:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62110>

great idea, chocolate! My little one would like that too.

### janagram — 2012-11-05T22:34:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62258>

I made up my own version of Ealachan's paleo cauli-mac n cheese! (grainfree)! Oh. Mmmm. So good. I cooked the cauliflower first, then cubed it and then layered it like scalloped potatoes, with a tapioca flour and potato starch mix I made up, then coconut milk and HWC plus grated sheep cheese, into oven and an hour later.....excellent.

### janagram — 2012-11-08T07:58:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62845>

Oh my I ate a lot of it too. but that was then.....

Tonite I went to a fascinating talk that reminded me of Jack Kruse's mindset. the setting of intention, etc. nothing so new, but you have to be ready to take it in. It has to hit you in the moment you are ready to hear. I think I took it to heart tonight....

plus, I feel so inspired by the election. The old always tries to kill the new, but the new survived! What hope that gives me. And I was once the old. I actually voted for Nixon! (that's how old I am)...does that make me an independent?

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-08T18:23:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62920>

Oh my it's confession time. You aren't the only one re: Nixon. DH and I used to be Republicans. He was even a member of the young republicans in college. To our everlasting shame we also voted for tricky Dicky. Ever since then we have paid very close attention.

Although nowadays I'm registered as a Democrat I think of myself more as an Eisenhower Republican. Which in my world makes me a moderate.

and yes. We're still celebrating at our house. Watching the disintegration of the old, rich patriarchal part of society just makes me happy as all getout.

### ealachan — 2012-11-08T20:39:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62967>

> **janagram;64064 wrote:** I made up my own version of Ealachan's paleo cauli-mac n cheese! (grainfree)! Oh. Mmmm. So good. I cooked the cauliflower first, then cubed it and then layered it like scalloped potatoes, with a tapioca flour and potato starch mix I made up, then coconut milk and HWC plus grated sheep cheese, into oven and an hour later.....excellent.

It's good with ham cubed up in it too, or crumbled bacon...or even browned ground beef!

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-08T21:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62984>

So where's the actual recipe? Or am I just gonna have to wing it?

### ealachan — 2012-11-08T22:34:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-62987>

> **DarleenMB;64823 wrote:** So where's the actual recipe? Or am I just gonna have to wing it?

I don't think I ever wrote up an actual recipe - I seldom do.

Basically what I do is like what jan said - pre-cook the cauliflower a little (I steam mine), then make up a batch of cheese sauce, dump it over the cauliflower in a baking pan, and bake it til it's bubbly and the cauliflower is tender. The cauliflower will usually turn to mush if you cook it all the way through before adding the sauce...that's been my experience anyway. Sometimes I don't even bother to pre-cook the cauliflower...I just bake the whole thing a little longer and it turns out fine.

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-08T23:47:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-63005>

OK I think I can manage that. THX!

You too, Darleen? (Nixon voter!) I was in teenage republicans in high school! (San Diego.....verrry conservative!) My dad was a small businessman, thinking the GOP actually gave a \*\*\*\* about him!!! We've learned a lot since then about whom they really serve!

### ealachan — 2012-11-09T19:57:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-63174>

I have to say, I find it really interesting to hear about the "old" GOP, because the party has been getting progressively more and more out of touch with reality and where the world is headed for as long as I can remember. The idea that they were once mostly reasonable is fascinating to me...

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-09T20:24:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-63177>

> **ealachan;65026 wrote:** I have to say, I find it really interesting to hear about the "old" GOP, because the party has been getting progressively more and more out of touch with reality and where the world is headed for as long as I can remember. The idea that they were once mostly reasonable is fascinating to me...

I was a child of the '50's when Republican Dwight D. Eisenhower led this country through its most prosperous years. Those were times when our representatives actually worked with one another (mostly). It was also the time of communist witch hunts. I only remember this stuff as it was filtered through the adults around me and, later, reading about it.

I was, however, very aware in the 70's of the rise of the so-called "born agains" which has become the fringe of the Christian right. That was when they began a systematic infiltration of the Republican party and this year we have all seen their true agenda burst forth.

I'll bang this drum one more time then return the journal to janagram ... "The Handmaid's Tale" was written as all this was coming into being and is the most frightening novel about religious zealotry (of any stripe) to be published so far. If you haven't read it I cannot recommend it highly enough. Atwood really hit the ol' nail on the head with that book.

### janagram — 2012-11-09T20:46:56+0200

### DarleenMB — 2012-11-27T13:30:00+0200

> _**DarleenMB said**

"The Handmaid's Tale" was written as all this was coming into being and is the most frightening novel about religious zealotry (of any stripe) to be published so far. If you haven't read it I cannot recommend it highly enough. Atwood really hit the ol' nail on the head with that book.
>
> Â__ Darleen, thanks for mentioning this book.Â  I remember after first reading it just a few years ago, I checked the copyright date, and I couldn't believe my eyes!Â  She wrote it in the 1980's and I kept thinking, how could she have known?Â  I was living outside the country for some of that decade and have always been kind of oblivious to USAian culture and politics, so I'm sure the evidence was there.Â  But to me it seemed eerily prophetic.Â  Her Oryx and Crake_, published in 2003, and its sequel,Â  the same insight but more about the environment and biotechnology.Â  It's like reading about next week, we're living on the threshhold.

How did she know? It was easy. Pardon me while I step on toes here ... it's not meant to be malicious it's just me.
In the early 70's the "jesus freaks" starting showing up ... They came in pairs and ALWAYS at first light on a Saturday morning trying to convince me that I needed saving. Then in the late 70's they started infiltrating the Republican party. There were stories in the news about how they were teaching each other to hide their true agenda so they could get elected. No one paid attention.
Until this year when several of them just flat out declared what they were really after.

The trail is there to follow if you know what to look for. Atwood saw it (like we did) as it was happening and took it to a horrifying conclusion.

I haven't read Oryx and Crake but I'm pretty sure it's on my bookshelf somewhere. I'll have to go hunting for it. So many books. So little time.

Â 

ETA I have no problem with people wanting to believe whatever. Â Where I have a problem is when they try to shove it down my throat. Â Hence the derogatory term which back then was common usage.

Well, I am very happy with my sleep....but my weight? Not so much. I am still in search of a monodiet that will really fit my needs. I say monodiet in the sense that I know (at least) what I'm having every morning for brk! I don't like to forage around wondering...can I have...? creativity does not belong to morning. I can be more creative at lunch but then more monodiet at dinner.

DH sliced marvelous cheeses and charcuterie as well as sauteed padrons (small peppers) for appetizersÂ for our dinner party. DH and I were ecstatic at how well it went. He grilled lamb lollipops after marinating in tons of herbs from the backyard. (they are the rack, sliced) and you can eat like a lollipop!! yum. Plus fingerling potatoes, roasted....(the one no no), broccollini,Â salad, and my butternut soup was awesome. Now, the amount of wine consumed was frightening...

and we're just gearing up to Christmas! Â  Â  Â  Â  Â I need a monodiet!!!!!

I am going to N=1 it to lose it. I will find out what works! Over and out!

### janagram — 2012-12-22T20:53:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-68222>

Thanks for the support, Caroline! I like the image of standing on your shoulders!! That's what I call support! Good tip about eggs, I've always loved them.....hmm?

I try to go "toward" eli-paleo....so I eat fish and shellfish certain days of the week even though I'd rather have roast chicken. And I'm learning to love some of those. (salmon and halibut...oh, sea bass) and I eat grassfed beef or lamb at other meals. I sleep in a much cooler room, go to bed earlier,

and I take SSKI each morning and selenium each evening, (along with a host of other supps)....hm, orange light in bedroom burned out but will get new one...(I'm making this eli-paleo checklist for my own amusement)

Things I could improve: at holidays a few sugary tidbits find their way onto my plate and I promptly get heartburn, which reminds me not to eat them. I could be more seasonal....ditch the carbs (more than usual) during winter...try face-dunking.

That's where I'm at. I am going to lose the weight. Figuring it out as I go. I know Jack says take action. These things I do (above) are actions and I will add more.

### caroline — 2012-12-23T02:00:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-68226>

I don't know if this will help you or not Â - but when I was first doing the reset - Â I had to eat a lot of protein Â - like upwards of 80 grams to knockout the cravings. Â But I kept thinking all this bacon is wonderful - but .....it seems to me that if you are trying to shed some kilos Â - Â then I think calories do somewhat matter. Â Once I started having more fat and less protein I felt alot better \[ I was really struggling with all that protein\]

and then Inger made a comment one day about not being able to eat all that stuff Â - for example - all the stuff in the cookbood like pizzas made with a bacon crust etc. etc. Â - Â and I was already at that point - I knew I couldn't eat all that epi -paleo stuff - if I did I would be big as a house again! Â It all sounds so wonderful but it just wasn't working for me. Â  I think it works wonderfully well for men but we are definitely different. Â I think this webinar will give us some answers. ### janagram — 2012-12-31T12:59:49+0200

Our society has become so technology addicted (look at me now!) and the kids ...well they sit a lot. Oh my when I was a kid (ho hum, I know)...I really (really) did walk miles to school everyday, from elementary school on. My Junior High was so far away that without a gang of kids to walk with I didn't even know the way home. We went through various neighborhoods, over a railroad track, through a couple of fields.....it was about 5 miles. That's everyday! My granddaughters have never walked to school. (it's not considered safe anymore, which is sad, and I agree, it's not safe in a big city). End of rant.

Happy New Year Everyone! (On the path downward in weight starting today)

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T14:20:43+0200

### janagram — 2013-01-02T16:14:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-69044>

Hey, Darleen! I understand your question completely! I, too needed help with leptin issues when I first came here. I may or may not be LS, mind you. I believe my thyroid has gotten better since following some of the protocols. I had my antibodies tested (you know, via the functional med. doc) and they were very low, so probably no Hashi's. Cholesterol is now normal, BP also. Sleep, the final frontier, has normalized, tho' there are some nights I barely hit 5 hrs, but ....that falls into the normal (barely) category. So, I am not really doing anything radical....I'm just following the epi paleo more closely. And that's none too close in my case. I'm only up for a certain level of it. I had some fun CT exp. at the beach last spring (before it was warm) but this winter...not happening. My nod to CT is to keep my house cooler than I want to, esp. my bedroom. I used to sleep with the heat up so I could sleep naked and toss covers off.....no more. I wear light jammies and a good blanket, no heat or low heat.
This is turning into an epistle....but ...I follow the supp recommends, I greet the sun, (my orange light for my lamp burned out...need new one), I take MACA...etc. I eat lots of fat, I eat as much fish as I can handle (couple times a week)...wish it were more, but I only like a few kinds...and I haven't gotten into shellfish much....tried, and will try more....I love Kerrygold butter...
NEW: there will be no grains consumed.Gluten or not. There will be no sugar consumed... sure I said that before but at book club I would have just a cracker or two, a pc of chocolate or two (chocolate has sugar people!! Sugar! ...Do people eat the bitter one? ...ew.)
And if this doesn't work I may try crystal meth. Nah, better not!

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-02T19:09:49+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-69062>

Not to step all over Jana but no, as we age our hormones drop away. You want optimal you HAVE to supplement. That means BHRT and I still think the best way to go is the Wiley Protocol. You replace to levels you had in your 20's and in the natural rhythm Mother Nature intended. It's very smooth and I LOVE IT!

the Dec webinar really opened my eyes. If you don't have it is definitely money well spent. You can d/l the video and watch to your heart's content. I plan on watching again probably tomorrow. It is just chock full of interesting info. Best one he's done yet.

So Jana ... you plan on sticking closely to protocol but I hate to tell you this ... CT is mandatory if you want to get anywhere. He explains why in the webinar and it really makes me feel good knowing I finally overcame inertia and got with the program. I CT every other day and now our tap water is 44Â°. My skin was bright cherry red when I climbed outta the tub this morning. At some point my body is going to wake up and go WTF?! and turn on the heat. That's when the fat melt will start. I hope.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-03T06:27:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-69109>

I have been wondering, and what you said, Darleen, stuck. That we need to do hormone replacement when we get older. Well, what I noticed is, that my hormones are quiet, real quiet. I contributed this to becoming old and thought that was normal. What I mean by quiet is, that I have no sexual desires whatsoever. Ok, I am divorced since 1995 and living alone since and for a few years, they kept "acting up" occasionally. But since 2005 I experiene nothing on that front anymore. Men just didn't cut it for me anymore. Looking at men my age or older just turns me off rather than on. Its not even the way they look (although I do think that women are a much better sight at any age), it is the dead look in their eyes, the 'I want to get home on my couch and have a beer attitude', the rigidity and so much more that I do not find appealing.
And quite honestly, I am not sure if I would want to get into this game again, as I can see myself, wandering around like a stray cat, howling away because I cannot find a good match. So I am not sure what to do. My doc just sent in my hormone panel and I get the results in two weeks. Then I have to make a decision.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-03T14:10:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-69164>

Charlotte, (sorry Jana for highjacking your journal) SS looks good but she's had work done. Not being catty, just it's pretty obvious. I credit her with pointing me towards the Wiley Protocol. That's how I found it and it has been a godsend.

As to your not wanting to jump back in that pool I'd suggest you read through the "let's talk about sex" thread. My sex drive evaporated over the last 7 to 10 years as well and that is NOT good. I figured at that point I had one foot in the grave and not just because sex, or should I say orgasms, make you feel alive. Read the thread.

That's an "order." LOL.

Back to Jana ... BHRT isn't about following the protocols. It's about fine tuning your hormones and getting your health moving towards optimal. I cannot stress enough how important Wiley's book is to ANY and all women. She really did her homework on that book and everything she says makes perfect sense.

/soapbox

### janagram — 2013-01-09T18:58:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-70102>

Well, the weight loss project was going ok until....book club! My nemesis! you see, we cook to go with the book and we take turns so I have no control over what the others cook....it's like Christmas every month complete with dessert! I will clearly have to have a strategy for this monthly event.
My approach to any new diet is to get very excited about the project so that when anything negative comes along my initial excitement will carry me over the tough spots....but that can only carry you so far....and its wearing thin. That's when I have to remember that I am Done. Being. Fat.
But this morning the scale told me different. Ah.....well.....let's see how tomorrow goes, and then next month, and then the whole of 2013....and...(I will figure this thing out!)

### caroline — 2013-01-09T23:11:39+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-70119>

yeah - book clubs are a problem ...... we used to meet at a restaurant so I could just get some grilled fish while most everyone stuffed their face with 3+ courses and wine of course. Now we takes turns at each other's house and it is a food fest from start to finish. I just take something I can have and eat before I go.....
I think - tho - you are kinda stuck on the word "diet' Personally - this is how I am eating and living my life..... it isn't a diet anymore - it is the way it is. Food doesn't have power over me any more. The only thing that I crave is apples - how silly is that? Jan - have you done the lepton reset? anyway - onwards and upwards - you will get there ...it has taken many of us years and years so you are definitely not alone.

### Charlotte — 2013-01-10T08:15:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-70142>

thats tough, Janagram. I think that Carolines strategy is good, to eat before you go there and then just have something small there.
I need a strategy also, otherwise I am not doing well. And when I am not doing well, I lose my purpose. Its like sitting on a horse having lost the reigns and the horse takes you whereever it wants. I had that happen so often in the past years and the fall off the horse was always painful leaving me with bruises. Then, to get back on the horse is quite tedious. I cannot let that happen anymore. The damage to myself is just too big.
So stick with it, and do not get discouraged.

### DarleenMB — 2013-01-10T14:00:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-70169>

Patience, grasshopper! Caroline and Charlotte are right on the money ... ditch the whole "diet" thinking. I know it's hard to let go of because it's taken me the better part of a year to get here.

If you haven't read the dopamine blog please consider doing so. If you have, maybe it's time to read it again. Just parroting Jack here ... bad dopamine levels = bad choices. Fixing it is the ONLY option.

Learn to let go when you slip, because you will. We all have. I still make bad choices (not very often though) and when I do I acknowledge I'm doing it, chide myself, do it anyway and then move on. It gets easier to acknowledge, ask myself why I think I need to do whatever it is I'm about to do, and then NOT do it.

I think this comes from months of eating good food and making an honest attempt to go epi=paleo AND doing CT. There is a cumulative effect as your body switches gears and the engine begins to hum. Jack talks about the Ferrari in your head ... mine's more like a Saturn V rocket that's been sputtering on the launch pad for a looooong time. Ya'll better stand back once it fully ignites and takes off. LOL. Every day I can feel myself getting closer to the countdown. It's why I"m still here.

Keep your feet on the path, your eyes on the prize. don't judge yourself. None of us walks on water. Yet.

I'm not striving for perfection anyway. I'm looking for OPTIMAL.

### Zorica Vuletic — 2013-02-14T23:47:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-75883>

> Janagram I think I mentioned this before ... you could eat BEFORE you go. Then just have some wine at the meeting. I did that for the UTU xmas party (and it drove everyone nuts) because the food is typical BAD SAD junk. I was fine and pretty much had to laugh at how everyone was so upset that I wasn't eating. Even though I told them repeatedly I had eaten before we came.

That's what sucks about society. I hate all the food they have. Sometimes I fool myself into thinking I'll be OK to eat it with no problems. But nope I'll get some shitty reaction and it's not worth it. But I feel so obliged and I even feel obliged b/c I question the level of isolation I create for myself.

Maybe make a paleo dessert of some sort and bring that. That you have MSG allergies (I know I do...) and you say you will get a bad reaction if you eat the crap chinese food. Then just eat a cupcake that you make and some wine. The social obligation will be fulfilled (at least you hope).

Some foods I question why I don't eat it but others I will get an instant reaction and I don't feel 'bad' for not eating. Chinese food has loaded MSG and if I want to get in a state as if I was drugged, well then I will pass thank you very much. How do people not understand that?

The Christmas thing...was there not SOMETHING edible? I can usually find at least something miniscule to eat. Funny thing is that I will not react as badly to gluten as to MSG (at least on same day that is). Also if I theorize that my immune system is any bit better, then it will take more time for gluten to 'attack' vs. MSG. Ugh...MSG. I felt so bad the other day for having those chicken wings! It really messed my head up.

I am taking more things that should help than ever...take potassium iodide (small) and a lot of the mitochondrial sups (let's see, ubiquinol, b-100's, l-carnitine, mag., ...hm, what's the other one? um, D-3?) Any thoughts???? Oh, and I guess I should drink ...pelligrino? gerolsteiner???
Thoughts! Please...

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-03-16T20:52:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-80799>

Two weeks ago I switched from filtered water to spring water from a bottle. Thirst seems a bit less, and getting up to use the bathroom at night is no longer an issue. In addition, the need to pee during the day seemed to have lessened in frequency.

This is my n=1, and not sure I can give spring water all the credit, but it sure seems to have helped.

### janagram — 2013-03-25T21:58:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/janagrams-journey.2399/post-81765>

Ok, once......upon a time......once, a couple years ago, I went slightly mad...stressed out over mom's cancer diagnosis (terminal), and began to behave strangely. I went around with a cloth wiping every corner of my house (I may have reported this before, sorry) and cleaning all day..as well as eating very rigidly...VLC and not much of it. One poached egg in the a.m., 2 grassfed beef hotdogs for lunch, maybe some lettuce and small pc of meat for dinner. It was strange. I lost weight. I never thought about food except how to avoid it. I wouldn't go to book club or out with friends. Oh, yeah, I lost a lot of weight. I loved it.
But sooner or later, sanity returned, I went out with friends, I cooked meals, I returned to my previous weight. (25 lbs. higher)
So, I guess I know how it is done, don't I? (yiiikes)

# Thread: C677T  A1298C MTHFR mutations
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### primat — 2012-07-13T12:51:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-37449>

I'm wondering what's considered the normal and what's the mutation for the MTHFR gene, but I guess the way of referencing is used for other mutations, too.

Does C 677 T mean that the C is normal and one or more T's are hetero/homozygous ?

For the curious;

me C677T:AG & A1298C:GG

SO C677T:GG & A1298C:GT

Would this mean that I've got the homozygous mutation for C677T and homozygous for A1298C while and my wife is homozygous for A1298C only?

Edit: from 23andme

### JanSz — 2012-07-13T18:53:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-37508>

> **primat;37616 wrote: I'm wondering what's considered the normal and what's the mutation for the MTHFR gene, but I guess the way of referencing is used for other mutations, too.
>
>
>
> Does C 677 T mean that the C is normal and one or more T's are hetero/homozygous ?
>
>
>
> For the curious;
>
> me C677T:AG & A1298C:GG
>
> SO C677T:GG & A1298C:GT
>
>
>
> Would this mean that I've got the homozygous mutation for C677T and homozygous for A1298C while and my wife is homozygous for A1298C only?
>
>
>
> Edit: from 23andme
>
>
> 23andme may be more detailed, but this is what I got:
>
>
>
> [http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/mthfr/tab/faq](http://http://labtestsonline.org/understanding/analytes/mthfr/tab/faq)
>
>
>
>
> > MTHFR
> >
> >
> >
> > Also known as: MTHFR DNA testing
> >
> > Formal name: Methylenetetrahydrofolate Reductase Mutations, C677T and A1298C
> >
> > Related tests: Homocysteine, Factor V Leiden Mutation and PT 20210 Mutation, Vitamin B12 & Folate, Cardiac Risk Assessment, Methotrexate
>
>
>
> The Test Sample
>
>
>
> What is being tested?
>
> The methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase (MTHFR) gene oversees the production of the MTHFR enzyme. This enzyme changes one form of folate into another and is part of the process that converts homocysteine into methionine, an important building block for many proteins.
>
>
>
> MTHFR testing detects two relatively common DNA sequence variants (single nucleotide polymorphisms, SNPs) in the MTHFR gene, C677T and A1298C. These SNPs result in changes in the DNA (or mutations) and are associated with increased homocysteine levels in the blood, which may increase the risk of premature cardiovascular disease (CVD), thrombosis, and stroke. Approximately 10-12% of the North American population is homozygous for C677T. There is some ethnic variability in the frequency, with the highest being in those of Mediterranean ancestry and the lowest in those of African ancestry.
>
>
>
> The C677T variant results in a **less active form of the MTHFR enzyme. When a person has two copies of the MTHFR C677T gene mutation (homozygous) or one copy of MTHFR C677T and one copy of A1298C (compound heterozygous), decreased MTHFR enzyme activity slows down the homocysteine-to-methionine conversion process and can lead to a buildup of homocysteine in the blood**.
>
>
>
> The increase in homocysteine is often mild to moderate but will vary from person to person depending upon the amount MTHFR enzyme activity. Homocysteine buildup is worsened when the affected person is folate-deficient.
>
>
>
> Results of some studies suggest that high levels of homocysteine in the blood may contribute to risk of CVD by damaging blood vessel walls and promoting formation of plaque (atherosclerosis) and inappropriate blood clots (thrombosis). However, a direct link between homocysteine levels and CVD or thrombotic risk has not been proven. For more on this, see the article on Homocysteine.**

**

Read top four lines in the blue chart for supplementation.

Use this test to guide doses.

Test check 34 items, ( 33 vitamins, minerals, amino acids and antioxidants are evaluated) repeat this test every 2x/year until all 34 are satisfied, then test 1x/year

Spectracell Comprehensive Nutritional Panel

[http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular\_vitamin\_analysis\_spectracell.html](http://http://www.healthremedies.com/intracellular_vitamin_analysis_spectracell.html)

HI, just saw your post. this is what I see

For the curious;

me C677T:AG & A1298C:GG you are +/- for C677t & +/+ for A1298C (corrected this. I looked at the chart wrong) Thanks WeAre Devo for responding.

SO C677T:GG & A1298C:GT wife -/- for C677T & -/+ for A1298C

### Szara.loring@gmail.com — 2012-07-22T18:01:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-40224>

Primat - yes the way it is written, C677T is where the T replaces the normal C at location 677 on the MTHFR gene. For A1298C a C is replaces the normal A at location 1298 on the MTHFR gene. This gene encodes the enzyme methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase. Reduces 5-10 methylene THF to,5 methyl-THF for methionine biosynthesis. If yours is not working well you can attempt to by-pass this limitation by supplementing with 5 methyl-THF.

[http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=MTHFR&search=MTHFR#function](http://http://www.genecards.org/cgi-bin/carddisp.pl?gene=MTHFR&search=MTHFR#function)

Just an aside, depending on the orientation of 23andMe you will have to remember when comparing to yasko documentation that C=G and A=T (if orientation is minus versus plus).

If I am reading your post correctly, you are hetero (+/-) for C677T and homo for A1298C (+/+). Your SO is hetero for 1298 only. Now that you know you might have an issue you may want to consider doing a methlyation panel to see how your current boichemistry is working. I would recommend doing this prior to supplementing. There is only one lab that does this (Vitamin Diagnostics). Maybe you can talk your doc into ordering this for you, but expect to pay out of pocket on this one.

[http://www.seekinghealth.com/methylation-pathway-panel-vitamin-diagnostics.html](http://http://www.seekinghealth.com/methylation-pathway-panel-vitamin-diagnostics.html)

Recycled info (about a year old) from a forum on mothering.com....

MTHF. FOR C667T, make sure you take 200mg folate, 200mg folinic acid a day (Yasko doesn't recommend high doses here). For A1298C, she believes it has more to do with recycling BH4 than with folate. People with this mutation tend to end up low in BH4 (which is hard to supplement). Consider the metagenics supp intrinsi B12/folate (it has intrinsic factor that helps B12 get absorbed, and enough mthf folate).

Anyhow, the C677T one means you will not use folic acid very well, and need to take folinic & mthf folate (Yasko says 200mg of each a day, others have used more and seen benefit). A1298C is less about folate, more about recycling BH4. Low BH4 can cause low serotonin & dopamine, and build-up of ammonia. Liver support is good (liver glandulars, milk thistle, dandelion root). And something to clear ammonia if you need to. You can tell if you have an ammonia problem by doing a urine amino acids test.

And of course this is a good source as well. [http://mthfr.net/additional-resources/](http://http://mthfr.net/additional-resources/)

### Jack Kruse — 2012-07-22T18:59:08+0300

### Joann — 2012-08-03T01:26:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-42580>

After reading and reading .....the more I know the less I know. The is data out there that states hetero for c677t is no big deal, but if also a1298c then it is called "compound" and should be treated as homo. Then I have read it could raise homocysteine, cause blot clots, risk for CRC, heavy metal toxicity. BUT it may also be protective for CRC, leukemia.

Also, very interesting, is the video by DR Ben, I think thats his name......says Italians in Sicily that have the snps show no risk ......food is better. He says not to take folic acid, but ok from food. only the already converted folacin supplement wit b12 and I think b6.

### MamaGrok — 2013-02-27T21:02:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-78078>

Bumping old post ... I just got my 23andme results, and cannot figure out what I'm looking at.

When I search for MTHFR, it shows me neural tube defects analysis for marker rs1801133, and says:

\- for C677T, I am AG (typical odds of having a child with a neural tube defect). Clearly this is heterozygous, but I cannot yet understand whether simply having C677T, or having one A, is a problem

It also offers a whole chart showing all the SNPs they chart for MTHFR. For instance, the first few entries look like this:

Gene Position SNP Versions MamaGrok's Genotype
LOC100506310, MTHFR 11846198 rs15854 A or G AA
LOC100506310, MTHFR 11846252 rs4846048 A or G AG
LOC100506310, MTHFR 11846270 rs1057624 C or T TT
...
MTHFR 11854476 rs1801131 G or T GT

I don't really yet understand what MTHFR means, what methylation defects imply, how they form, how there are so many SNPs for MTHFR, or what C677T means, or where the A1298C y'all mention above is, but all I'm trying to figure out right now is...

Does what I found show far show a methylation defect issue? How do I interpret the data to know this?

### ATL_Paleo — 2013-02-27T21:39:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-78091>

Have you tried GeneticGenie.org ?

It provides an easy to understand document of SNPs from your 23andme raw data. A result of +/-, +/+ or -/- is the easiest way to know if a SNP is heterozygous, homozygous or not an issue.

### lioness7 — 2013-02-28T16:42:34+0200

Livewello has come out with an app which reads even more snps than GG and is constantly being updated. It is 19.95. The link is found at [www.mthfrsupport.com](http://www.mthfrsupport.com). The site recently had a blogtalk radio explaining how it works. The app is free, you only pay to have your 23andme uploaded and deciphered.

[http://blogtalk.vo.llnwd.net/o23/show/4/465/show\_4465349.mp3](http://blogtalk.vo.llnwd.net/o23/show/4/465/show_4465349.mp3)

### freesia — 2013-02-28T22:29:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/c677t-a1298c-mthfr-mutations.2402/post-78387>

What I got from the podcast was that regardless of SNPs, if you're running hard in the non fat burning (quick ATP recycling) pathways you deplete methyl groups, particularly through the ATP PC system. This is a double whammy because if you're in that pathway it's at the expense of the PPP where you make reduced glutathione. I **think**(?) this is where you're going to get optimal gene expression too.

Some SNPs may be significant (I guess thats where people like Tim Jackson come in) but you could still have plenty of the biomarkers of a methylation block like high oxidative stress, low reduced glutathione, low nadph....and not have anything "wrong" with your genetics.

What is Livewello ?
Livewello is a Social Health Platform that provides tools to make it easier for people to be cared for, in collaboration with their Health Practitioners or Health Coaches.

[http://www.mthfrsupport.com/livewello-mthfr.html](http://www.mthfrsupport.com/livewello-mthfr.html)

Where can I go to start using Livewello?
Livewello is available as a free iPhone, iPad and iPod touch App.
Livewello is also free to use online; Go to livewello-app.appspot.com to Sign Up for a Livewello account.
You will need to Sign Up for a Livewello account if you need your 23andMe Raw Data interpreted.

[http://livewello-app.appspot.com/](http://livewello-app.appspot.com/)

.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-14T03:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-37665>

> **BJK77;37800 wrote:** Is the raw head that much more nutrient dense than just plain old cooked fish? Convince me it's worth it and I'm totally in

OK, here is my guess (not to say that Inger's word isn't good enough). The fish's brain contains the highest levels of the stuff in fish that we're after. Like with meat, when we avoid the 'gross parts' (offal, etc) we often miss out on the highest nutrient-dense bits.

### Souldanzer — 2012-07-14T05:00:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-37687>

> **BJK77;37800 wrote:** Is the raw head that much more nutrient dense than just plain old cooked fish? Convince me it's worth it and I'm totally in

I'm guessing yes. You eat a lot of parts that you don't normally eat with the head. There's the brain which probably concentrates DHA and choline, then there's the eyes, which are super rich in zinc, and you'll also eat the thyroid gland if I recall correctly from my WAPF readings. I would love to hear from Dr K though if there's something else we're going for and why raw (might protect the DHA from oxidizing).

Sunday is live crab eating (we'll boil it first ) planned with one of my friends and maybe tomorrow he'll take me out to the best seafood place in Boulder (Jax ) so I may finally learn how to slurp raw oysters. I'm sorta excited about my new food adventures.... currently trying to learn how to make pickled salmon without whey. Any ideas?

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-15T08:45:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-37999>

OK, so I think I've found an optimal way to eat the fish head smoothies. Here's my method (combining the advice of Inger and Souldanzer and then a bit of my own). Frozen fish heads are the way to go... keeps smell to a minimum. Put fish heads in blender. Add fresh juice of 2 lemons and some salt. Blend to smooth (I don't add any extra water, just the lemon juice). Before you drink it, pour about 1 inch of red wine in a glass. Then drink smoothie as fast as possible. Immediately after, drink the red wine (actually, I used half of it to swish in my mouth and sort of gargle and then I drank the rest... even if you're doing a protocol where you can't drink any wine, just the swishing really did the trick... totally killed the lingering fish taste.) All in all, pretty palatable.

### Linz — 2012-07-15T11:36:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-38005>

Successful second attempt. I used herring as they were much fresher than the mackerel.

I added some lemon and parsley, held my breath and drank with a little tabasco - quite fishy and think it will take a bit of practice before the smoothie tastes as good as the herring and roe baked in the oven afterwards.

Still need to perfect the recipe before I can give my friends fish head smoothie for dinner (can I even admit to trying it myself?) but they are starting to ask the right questions. It's just so hard to overcome their fear of fats so imprinted on our brain by cw.

Inger, how hard was that first bite of high meat?? I've still got a long way to go.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-17T08:07:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-38664>

Optimal fish head smoothie accomplished. I cut whole fish at the middle (I know that includes the liver... just don't tell me what else I'm eating!), put 2 in the blender with ghee and 2 Tablespoons of wild caught, raw fish roe. Added juice of 3 lemons and salt and blended. I do not have a super-duper blender so it was rather, um, thick (and bigger than last time because of the added fish bits and the roe) but it got drunk! Three big gulps of Australian red wine were consumed immediately afterwards. Optimal dinner complete!

### Inger — 2012-07-17T08:49:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-38668>

> **KiwiLauren;38841 wrote:** Optimal fish head smoothie accomplished. I cut whole fish at the middle (I know that includes the liver... just don't tell me what else I'm eating!), put 2 in the blender with ghee and 2 Tablespoons of wild caught, raw fish roe. Added juice of 3 lemons and salt and blended. I do not have a super-duper blender so it was rather, um, thick (and bigger than last time because of the added fish bits and the roe) but it got drunk! Three big gulps of Australian red wine were consumed immediately afterwards. Optimal dinner complete!

LOL...

perfect!

I did this a week ago, with two fishes that a friend had caught himself- somewhere . I just cut the whole fish with innards and shells and head all in pieces and mixed with water into a smoothie. It was sooooo nasty!! UH! It was totally slimy and strange.. smelled so not good.. it was not mackerel though, but a local freshwater-fish from waters that are half salty half sweet. And not the purest by any means.

After that nasty experience, drinking the mackerelhead-smoothies are just so easy, nice texture, like a creamy chocolate for sure!

I was nauseated the whole day after. I bet the fish was really polluted. I will never do that again.

But, I am still here, alive and well.. LOL. So guys and girls who are reading this, you can all do the mackerelhead-smoothie! No question.

It really is not that bad at all.

Beautiful beautiful mackerels. Because they are, take a closer look next time!

I bet any fish from deep cold clean waters will be just as good.

### Glamazon — 2012-07-17T15:54:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-38700>

My favorite part of sushi is the raw roe: Masago and tobiko. I like tobiko best which is harvested in Iceland or Canada, sent to Japan for processing and then sold in the US. Tobiko is from a tiny cold water fish. [http://www.sustainablesushi.net/the-fish/masago/](http://http://www.sustainablesushi.net/the-fish/masago/)

### Inger — 2012-07-18T19:06:54+0300

> **KiwiLauren;39027 wrote: My son went and told his friends (aged 8!). He said he asked them, "Do you think fish head smoothies are good for you?" They screamed 'NO!" **

This was too funny!!!!!!

Guess what! Whole family was drinking mackerelhead-smoothie today, first time**!!! YAY!!!!!!

Mom, dad, two brothers!!! And they don't even hold their breath, not one of them!!! They said, it was not bad at all, and now my dad wants one smoothie EVERY day!!! Because Kruse said it was good... He also starts to sleep with a icepack under his head.. haha.. I have told daddy, if he does this he will not get Alzheimer like his mom. It is working! They have started to eat lots of fish too, every day. Life is good.

I LOVE THIS!!!!!

And I checked if viruses gets killed too by cooking and they do.. and bakterias of course too.. and now a light went on in my head, I mean for real (even if I kind of knew this before but today i REALLY realized HOW good this is!!!). The BG last series, about viruses and our DNA.. now I know why they have to be raw!!! We get lots and lots of amazing viruses from them!!!! Bakterias too for sure!

Wow!

(and me, happy girl, have gotten 4 raw mackerel heads to drink today )

### Rosekelly — 2012-07-18T21:16:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-39119>

I'm joining the club. This morning I pulverized three fresh whole sardines in my blender, added lemon juice, ghee, and water, and drank that sucker down. It wasn't bad. Just too much. It made over a cup. I wanted it to serve as an entire BAB, so I weighed out 7 oz of fish. I think I'll do less next time and just saute some shrimp to go along with it. The fish smoothie was just so darn fishy.

Now I know this wasn't a mackerel but I just discovered my local Asian grocery store yesterday and went wild over the seafood section. They have everything, and they have it frozen and fresh, and they have it marked wild or farmed and the country of origin. Having never cooked a whole fish, I wanted to start slow, so I bought a pound of whole sardines with the heads attached. Then I was reading this thread and thought, hmm, why not -- a sardine smoothie! So I did it.

The tip about having a sip of red wine after? Awesome. I swished it around and swallowed it, leaving my mouth refreshed and not fishy.

Then I drove over to the ocean and swam for a half hour. So far, I'm sure feeling great. I think if I keep the smoothie to half that size, I can do this every day. That was an awful lot of fish to glug down.

### KiwiLauren — 2012-07-19T07:17:15+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mackerel-head-smoothie.2403/post-39298>

> **Cavemam;39405 wrote:** Has anybody tried with a food processor? I've got a superduper one of those, so not inclined to go get a blender to handle scales (there goes the gag reflex again!)

Well, I don't have a Vitamix (they start at $600 here) and with today's fish head smoothie my trusted blender broke. In the middle of attempting to blend the frozen fish and not getting too far. So I can now answer your question, Cavemam. YES, a food processor works fine. I put whole (minus the tail) fish plus extra roe with lemon juice and salt into it (only fish frozen) and it worked like a charm. Yum. (Scales are the least of your worries... I've learned gills and spines and fins are much trickier to puree.) Keep that red wine on hand... it really helps!
